label: Tears of the Kingdom

REGIONS.name: Great Sky Island
REGIONS.tagline: Wake on a floating island, learn Link's four new arm powers, then dive to the surface of Hyrule below.

sections.name: Awakening and First Steps
sections.sub: Room of Awakening, meeting the Steward Construct

steps.t: Wake in the Room of Awakening. Pick up the Decayed Master Sword off the ground and slash the vines blocking the way to exit.

items.name: Decayed Master Sword
items.note: Power 1; only used to cut the exit vines, then sent back to Zelda in the ending cutscene.

steps.t: In the main hall, touch the Dragon Head Island terminal. It starts the cogs turning and lights the gate, then head out toward the water.
Dive down through the pools to reach the island surface. On the third, biggest dive, surface by the rubble and open the chest for Archaic Legwear.

items.name: Archaic Legwear
items.note: Your starting trousers; equip them right away for a little defense.

steps.stuck: From the terminal, drop into the water and dive (hold the jump button while falling) through each pool. The Archaic Legwear chest sits on the rubble ledge right where you surface after the last, longest dive.

steps.t: Approach the sleeping Steward Construct. It wakes, hands you the Purah Pad, and points you toward the Temple of Time.

items.name: Purah Pad
items.note: Your map and ability menu, like the Sheikah Slate in the last game.

steps.t: Your right arm has no power yet. Each Sky Island shrine restores one ability. Take them in the order below for the smoothest run.

sections.name: Ukouh Shrine - Ultrahand
sections.sub: Your first ability: grab, move, and glue objects

steps.t: Head to the glowing Ukouh Shrine near the Steward and enter to receive Ultrahand.

items.name: Ultrahand
items.note: Pick up, rotate, move, and stick objects together to build things.

steps.t: Use Ultrahand to grab the boards and lay them across the gap to make a bridge, then cross.
steps.stuck: Hold the Ultrahand button to grab a board, drag it over the gap, and rotate it flat (hold R and tap the D-pad) before releasing so it lands as a walkway. One long board spans the first gap.

steps.t: Attach a hook to the moving rail platform, then ride it across the next gap. Glue boards together if you need a wider deck.
steps.stuck: Grab the loose hook with Ultrahand and stick it onto the top of the wooden platform sitting on the rail, so it hangs from the line like a ski lift. Climb on the board and it slides you across.

steps.t: Touch the green altar to finish the shrine and earn a Light of Blessing. Collect four of these for a heart container.

items.name: Light of Blessing
items.note: Four equal one heart container at a Goddess Statue.

steps.t: Hold the Ultrahand button to enter build mode. The control stick rotates the held piece; let go to drop it.

sections.name: In-isa Shrine - Fuse
sections.sub: Stick materials onto weapons, shields, and arrows

steps.t: From Ukouh, head toward the lake. Use Ultrahand to build a raft from logs and a fan or sail, or attach logs and paddle across.
Enter the In-isa Shrine to receive Fuse.

items.name: Fuse
items.note: Combine a material with a weapon, shield, or arrow for more power, reach, or new effects.

steps.t: Fuse a rock to your stick to smash the cracked wall, and fuse an item to your shield where the puzzle asks.
steps.stuck: Hold the Fuse button, aim at a loose rock on the ground, and confirm to stick it onto your sword or stick; the heavy rock-hammer smashes the cracked wall in one swing.

steps.t: Fuse a Fire Fruit to an arrow to hit a target or light the way, then claim the chest reward inside the shrine.

items.name: Fire Fruit
items.note: Fuse to arrows for a fire shot that bursts into flame on impact.

steps.stuck: Draw your bow, hold the Fuse button while aiming, pick the Fire Fruit from the menu, and shoot the dry leaves on the wall ahead; the fire burns them away and drops the chest down to you.

steps.t: Reach the altar to complete the shrine and bank another Light of Blessing.
Just east of In-isa, slip into Pondside Cave through the narrow tunnel and open the chest for the Archaic Tunic so you are not freezing.

items.name: Archaic Tunic
items.note: Your starting shirt, found in Pondside Cave near In-isa Shrine.

steps.stuck: The cave mouth is a low slit in the rock just southeast of In-isa; crouch and walk in. A few Keese swarm you in the dark, but the Archaic Tunic chest sits past them down a tunnel near the back.

sections.name: Gutanbac Shrine - Ascend
sections.sub: The snowy peak: swim up through ceilings

steps.t: The route climbs a cold, snowy mountain. Without cold protection you will lose hearts steadily.
At the base of the slope, pick the Spicy Peppers scattered nearby and cook them at a cooking pot to make a warm dish.

items.name: Spicy Pepper
items.note: Cook into a dish for temporary cold resistance.

steps.stuck: Spicy Peppers grow at the foot of the hill near the friendly Construct's cooking pot. Drop a few into the pot and cook to get a Spicy dish that grants Cold Resistance for several minutes.

steps.t: Pass through the caves on the way up. Throw or fuse Brightbloom Seeds to light the dark passages.

items.name: Brightbloom Seed
items.note: Throw to light up caves and the Depths later.

steps.t: Eat the warm dish, climb to the Gutanbac Shrine, and enter to receive Ascend.

items.name: Ascend
items.note: Swim straight up through most ceilings and pop out on the surface above.

steps.t: Use Ascend to rise through the platforms instead of climbing, then finish at the altar for another Light of Blessing.
steps.stuck: Stand under the platform you want to reach, hold the Ascend button, and a green dome appears when a solid surface is overhead; press to swim straight up through it. Use it twice to climb the shrine's tiers.

steps.t: Look up before using Ascend; you surface at the first solid ceiling above you. It is the fastest way out of caves and tall rooms.

sections.name: Temple of Time - Recall and the Closed Door
sections.sub: Meet Rauru's spirit and start The Closed Door

steps.t: Glide or build your way to the Temple of Time with your three Lights of Blessing. Wing devices or a fan-and-board flyer make the trip easy.
Touch the large tear-shaped stone inside. You see a vision of Zelda and are granted Recall.

items.name: Recall
items.note: Send an object backward along its own recent path through time.

steps.t: Two giant cogs turn on either side of the hall. Use Recall on one to reverse it, hop on, and ride it up to the central ledge.
steps.stuck: Stand near a cog, hold the Recall button, and aim at the spinning gear; it rewinds and turns the opposite way. Hop onto a tooth as it carries you upward to the central ledge, then step off at the top.

steps.t: Try to open the big sealed door. Rauru's spirit appears but lacks the vitality to open it, starting the main quest The Closed Door.
Rauru is the founding king of Hyrule, not the fifth sage. You meet Mineru and the Spirit Temple much later in the game.

sections.name: Nachoyah Shrine - The Fourth Blessing
sections.sub: Earn the heart container to open the door

steps.t: Rauru sends you to the hidden Nachoyah Shrine. Use Ascend and the cogs to climb up to reach it.
Enter Nachoyah, which tests Recall. Reverse the moving parts so the path lines up and you can reach the altar.
steps.stuck: Ride a wooden platform that falls down the waterfall, then Recall it to float back up the falls. At the gate, Recall the swinging clock hand so it lines back up with the other and the path locks open.

steps.t: Finish the altar for your fourth Light of Blessing.
Return to the Goddess Statue and trade four Lights of Blessing for a heart container.

items.name: Heart Container
items.note: Your fourth heart; enough vitality to open the sealed door.

steps.t: Open the big door at the Temple of Time to complete The Closed Door and trigger the cutscene that sets up your descent.

sections.name: Diving to the Surface
sections.sub: Leaving Great Sky Island for Hyrule below

steps.t: After the cutscene, head to the edge of Great Sky Island. You have no paraglider yet, so you will free-fall toward Hyrule's surface.
Skydive off Great Sky Island down toward the surface of Hyrule far below.
While skydiving, tilt forward to fall faster and spread out to slow down. You splash into water below, so aim for the lake to land safely.
steps.stuck: Hold the left stick forward to angle head-down and fall fast; pull back and spread out to slow down and steer. You have no paraglider yet, so line yourself up over a lake or pond and you splash down unharmed.

steps.t: You touch down on the surface and are told to head for Lookout Landing, the new base camp near Hyrule Castle. This begins your surface adventure.
You do not get the Paraglider here; Purah hands it over at Lookout Landing after you raise the first Skyview Tower. Until then, use water to break falls.

REGIONS.name: Lookout Landing
REGIONS.tagline: Land in Hyrule's wartime hub, reunite with Purah, raise your first Skyview Tower, earn the Paraglider, and set out to fix the four Regional Phenomena.

sections.name: Landing at Lookout Landing
sections.sub: From the Great Sky Island to the Surface hub

steps.t: After diving from the Great Sky Island, you land near central Hyrule. Head to Lookout Landing, the walled camp just south of Hyrule Castle.
Open the map and place a pin on Lookout Landing if you wander. It is the game's main hub, with shops, beds, and an Emergency Shelter.
Enter the camp and find Purah, the small white-haired researcher in a lab coat. She is near the central Skyview Tower with Josha and Robbie.
Gloom-covered enemies roam Hyrule now. Gloom caps your hearts until you reach light or a Lightroot, so avoid the red-black ooze early on.

sections.name: Meeting Purah
sections.sub: Reunion and the Purah Pad

steps.t: Speak with Purah at Lookout Landing. She explains the Upheaval, Zelda's disappearance, and the Gloom spreading across Hyrule.
Your slate-like device is the Purah Pad, your new map tool. It will sync with Skyview Towers to chart the world once activated.

items.name: Purah Pad
items.note: Your map device. Stores your abilities and unlocks fast travel and tower sync. The successor to the BotW Sheikah Slate.

steps.t: Your five abilities are already learned on the Great Sky Island: Ultrahand, Fuse, Ascend, Recall, and Autobuild. They are not runes; the Pad just stores them.

sections.name: Crisis at Hyrule Castle
sections.sub: Investigating the disturbance to the north

steps.t: Purah sends you north to the Hyrule Castle area. The castle has torn free of the ground and floats above a column of Gloom.
Head to the First Gatehouse in the Hyrule Castle Town Ruins and speak with Captain Hoz, who is watching the castle and the figure resembling Zelda.
steps.stuck: Head north through the Castle Town Ruins; at the big closed gate, pull it open with Ultrahand. Hoz is atop the gatehouse, so climb to a stone ledge inside and Ascend up through the ceiling to reach him.

steps.t: Report back to Purah at Lookout Landing. She realizes the old map data is gone and that you need a Skyview Tower to chart Hyrule.
You can't storm the castle yet. The story routes you through the four regions first, so Crisis at Hyrule Castle stays open until much later.

sections.name: Raising the Skyview Tower
sections.sub: Your first tower and the Paraglider

steps.t: Meet Purah at the Lookout Landing Skyview Tower beside the camp. Step inside and interact with the terminal to register your Purah Pad.
The tower scans you, then launches you high into the sky, revealing the surrounding central Hyrule Surface map for the first time.
As you fall, Purah hands you the Paraglider so you can glide down safely. Press the jump button while airborne to deploy it and float to the ground.

items.name: Paraglider
items.note: Glide after tower launches or any fall. Press the jump button mid-air to deploy; gliding drains stamina.

steps.stuck: Press X (the jump button) once while you're falling to pop the Paraglider open. Glide distance is limited by your green stamina wheel, so aim for the nearest ground.

steps.t: Skyview Towers double as fast-travel points and launch pads. Use them to reach the Sky islands floating above each region.
Return to Purah after activating the tower. She gives you your next main objective.

sections.name: The Four Regional Phenomena
sections.sub: Main objective: help the four peoples

steps.t: Purah starts the Regional Phenomena quest: strange disasters are striking the Rito, Goron, Zora, and Gerudo. Investigate all four.
Wind Temple (Rito, Tulin) near Rito Village; Fire (Goron, Yunobo) at Goron City; Water (Zora, Sidon) at Zora's Domain; Lightning (Gerudo, Riju) at Gerudo Town.
Tackle the regions in any order, but Rito Village (Wind) to the northwest is the gentlest first temple and a great starting point.
A fifth sage, Mineru, and her Spirit Temple come later in the story after the four phenomena. Don't worry about her yet.

sections.name: The Camera and Hyrule Compendium
sections.sub: The Camera Work in the Depths quest

steps.t: After getting the Paraglider, talk to Josha and Robbie in the Lookout Landing lab. They start the quest Camera Work in the Depths.
Robbie adds the camera to your Purah Pad and asks you to meet him at the chasm south of Lookout Landing. Dive in and glide down into the Depths.

items.name: Camera
items.note: Purah Pad feature. Open the ability menu and pick the camera to take photos and register Compendium entries.

steps.stuck: The chasm is the wide gloom-ringed pit in Hyrule Field just south of the camp. Jump in and free-fall to the bottom, then follow the glow to Robbie's little camp on the Depths floor.

steps.t: Photograph the statue Robbie points out to unlock the Hyrule Compendium, then report back to the lab. Josha rewards you with some Zonaite.

items.name: Hyrule Compendium
items.note: In-game catalog. Snap photos to fill entries for monsters, materials, weapons, and creatures; handy for tracking locations.

steps.stuck: Open the camera with L and aim at the statue with the raised arm until a label with an exclamation mark pops up, then press A to snap it. That confirms the right target before you head back to Josha.

steps.t: Lightroots, not shrines, are the Depths' map and light points. Activate them like Skyview Towers to brighten and chart the dark underground.

REGIONS.name: Wind Temple
REGIONS.tagline: Brave the Hebra blizzard, climb the sky islands with Tulin, and storm the floating Stormwind Ark to wake the Sage of Wind.

sections.name: Reaching Rito Village
sections.sub: Find your way into the snowstorm

steps.t: Head northwest from Lookout Landing across Hyrule Ridge toward Rito Village; the unending blizzard over Hebra marks your destination.
The cold up here saps hearts. Bring warm food, a cold-resistance set like the Snowquill armor, or spicy peppers before you push into Hebra.
Climb to the top of Rito Village to Revali's Landing, where Tulin is arguing with his father Teba, the new Village Elder, and his mother Saki.
The Tulin of Rito Village quest begins. Tulin flies off to investigate the storm, and you set out after him.

sections.name: Tracking Down Tulin
sections.sub: Across the Hebra mountains

steps.t: Follow the snowy trail north into Hebra. The blizzard blocks the open slopes, so take the cave route that climbs up through the mountain.
steps.stuck: Climb the mountain path past the Hebra Trailhead hut (northeast of Rospro Pass Tower, near Sahirow Shrine) to the Hebra South Summit Cave. The open slopes are blizzard-blocked, so the cave is the only way up.

steps.t: In the cave, light the campfire and drop a Hylian Pine Cone on it to make an updraft, then glide up through the shaft to keep climbing.
steps.stuck: Light the unlit campfire (toss a Fire Fruit, or shoot a fire arrow at it), then drop a Hylian Pine Cone on the flames. That spawns an updraft column - open your paraglider inside it to rise up the shaft.

steps.t: Reach the lone cedar tree at the summit of Talonto Peak. Tulin is perched there, upset that an enemy stole his bow nearby.
Tulin joins you and lends his Power of Wind, firing a gust that hurls you forward while you glide.

items.name: Tulin's Gust
items.note: A wind burst from Tulin that boosts your paraglide forward through the air; he travels with you for the quest.

sections.name: Climbing to the Sky
sections.sub: The Rising Island Chain

steps.t: From the Hebra peaks, paraglide off the heights and use Tulin's Gust to launch between the floating stone ruins of the Rising Island Chain.
Alternate Tulin's Gust to cross gaps horizontally and Ascend to rise up through the undersides of the higher platforms.
steps.stuck: Tulin glides alongside you now - point his Gust and pulse it to fling across wide horizontal gaps. To go up, stand under a higher platform and Ascend straight through its underside.

steps.t: Make a campfire and drop a Hylian Pine Cone on it to create an updraft, then glide up to islands that look out of reach.
Activate any shrine you find among the sky islands to set a fast-travel point and a warm respite from the cold.
Climb to the top of the chain, then skydive into the eye of the giant tornado to drop onto the Stormwind Ark - the Wind Temple.
steps.stuck: Climb to the top of the chain, then dive straight down into the center of the swirling storm. You free-fall through the hole in the clouds and land on the deck of the Stormwind Ark.

sections.name: Inside the Stormwind Ark
sections.sub: The five locks puzzle

steps.t: The Ark is a flying ship with a sealed hatch in the center. Activate all five locks to open it and quell the storm.
Every lock works the same way: get a fan or windmill spinning, then hit it with Tulin's Gust. You can tackle the five in any order.
steps.stuck: For every lock: get its fan or windmill turning, then aim Tulin's Gust so it blows through the blades. The device spins up and the lock lights. You can do the five in any order.

steps.t: One lock sits behind a broken lever: grab a fallen icicle with Ultrahand, attach it to the lever to free it, then aim a Gust at the fan.
steps.stuck: Grab a loose icicle with Ultrahand and attach it to the broken lever handle to make the lever whole again. Use the lever to open the gate, then hit the fan with Tulin's Gust.

steps.t: Another lock uses rotating gears - line up a slab or icicle piece with Ultrahand, use Recall on a turning wheel if needed, then Gust the fan.
steps.stuck: Ultrahand a slab or icicle to bridge the gap between the gears; if a wheel is already spinning, use Recall to send it back into position. Once the fan is reachable, hit it with Tulin's Gust.

steps.t: Drop to a lower deck and cross the scaffolding gaps with Tulin's Gust, then blast the fan set up there to light that lock.
Out on the exterior, glide a laser-lined tunnel and dive past the obstacles to reach a fan platform, then power it with a Gust.
For the last lock, clear the Constructs guarding the rigging, get the final windmill turning, and finish it with Tulin's Gust.
With all five locks lit, return to the center. The hatch opens and Colgera bursts out, dragging you into a midair duel.

sections.name: Boss: Colgera
sections.sub: Aerial Phenomenon

steps.t: You skydive after Colgera. Target its three ice-covered cores - the rounded weak points on its head, body, and tail.
Aim the bow while falling to enter slow-motion. Fuse arrows with a Keese Eyeball to home in, or with fire to crack the ice faster.

items.name: Bow
items.note: Any bow works; pair with arrows for the diving aerial fight.

items.name: Keese Eyeball
items.note: Fuse to an arrow so the shot homes in on Colgera's weak cores.

steps.stuck: Pull the bow while falling to trigger slow-motion, then shoot the three icy bulges on its underbelly. Fuse a Keese Eyeball for homing arrows, or fire/a Fire Fruit to crack each core in fewer hits.

steps.t: Shatter all three cores. Colgera dives away through a portal, then reappears with its weak points restored for phase two.
In phase two Colgera summons rows of tornadoes that sweep the arena - glide through the safe gaps while you keep diving on the cores.
steps.stuck: Don't fly straight into the spinning tornado rows - they form a moving wall. Wait for a gap to open, glide through it, then drop back under Colgera to keep hitting the three cores.

steps.t: Break the three cores a second time to finish Colgera and disperse the blizzard choking Hebra.
Clearing the temple grants a Heart Container that permanently raises your maximum hearts.

items.name: Heart Container
items.note: Permanent +1 to your maximum hearts, awarded for clearing the temple.

sections.name: The Sage of Wind
sections.sub: Tulin's vow
sections.reward: Vow of Tulin, Sage of Wind

steps.t: Tulin awakens as the Sage of Wind and gives his vow, joining you permanently as a summonable sage companion.
You gain the Vow of Tulin, letting you call his avatar and use Tulin's Gust anywhere in the open world.

items.name: Tulin's Gust
items.note: Summon Tulin's avatar and interact to fire a forward wind burst that supercharges your paraglide and aerial attacks.

steps.t: The Hebra blizzard ends and Rito Village's regional phenomenon is resolved - one of four temples down.
Tulin's Gust is amazing for travel: jump, glide, and pulse the Gust to cross huge distances and reach far-off sky islands.

REGIONS.name: Fire Temple
REGIONS.tagline: Snap Yunobo out of his Marbled Rock Roast craze, blast Moragia off Death Mountain, dive into the Depths, and ring the gongs to clear the Fire Temple.

sections.name: Crisis at Goron City
sections.sub: Eldin region, near Death Mountain

steps.t: Head to Goron City in the Eldin region. The area is volcanic, so gear up for heat before you go.

items.name: Flamebreaker Armor
items.note: Sold by the Goron in Goron City, or cook fireproof meals. Stops Eldin's heat damage.

steps.t: Eldin's heat sets wooden weapons and shields on fire and drains hearts. Carry fireproof food or wear heat-proof gear.
In Goron City the Gorons are obsessed with Marbled Rock Roast, a gloom-tainted rock food that has them too dazed to listen or work.
The Marbled Rock Roast is the corruption itself. Don't try to feed or bargain with the dazed Gorons; you'll fix it by clearing the temple.
Boss Yunobo is up at YunoboCo HQ, north of the city. Goron kids guard a hot cave nearby where he waits in a strange mask; make your way in.
steps.stuck: Heat resistance: buy Flamebreaker Armor (the chest piece is ~700 rupees at YunoboCo HQ) and equip it before going down, or carry Fireproof Elixirs / Fireproof-Lizard dishes. One piece is enough to survive the descent.

sections.name: Snap Yunobo Out of It
sections.reward: Yunobo joins you and teaches his rolling charge

steps.t: The masked Yunobo turns hostile, curls into a ball, and revs in place before launching himself at you across the room.
Dodge sideways so he rolls past and slams into a wall; he's stunned briefly. Run in and attack the mask before he recovers.
steps.stuck: No audio tell before he launches, so lock on and strafe sideways the instant he revs. He only takes damage right after smacking a wall; rush in and hit the mask during that brief stun, then back off before he re-curls.

steps.t: He's immune to damage until he hits a wall. Don't stand in his charge line; a wall hit is the only opening to damage him.
Stun and hit him three times until the corrupted mask shatters. Yunobo comes back to his senses and offers to help reach the temple.

items.name: Yunobo's Charge
items.note: Field version: aim and fire Yunobo like a rolling cannonball to smash marbled rock, gongs and foes.

steps.t: Each time he's hit, his wind-up gets shorter, down to about two seconds. Once stunned you can also tag him with a bow.

sections.name: Up Death Mountain, Then Into the Depths
sections.sub: Beat Moragia, then dive the crater chasm

steps.t: Follow Yunobo to the mine-cart rails up Death Mountain. Use Ultrahand to attach a fan to a cart so it self-propels along the track.

items.name: Fan (Zonai device)
items.note: Stick it to a mine cart with Ultrahand for forward thrust along the rails.

steps.t: As you ride up, fire Yunobo's charge to smash marbled-rock blockages on the track so the cart keeps climbing toward the summit.
At the peak, the dragon-like boss Moragia rises from the crater. Board the nearby Zonai fan-glider with a cart and steering stick to fight it.
steps.stuck: Don't build from scratch: a pre-made fan-plane with a steering stick sits on the platform. Ultrahand it so the nose points off the crater edge, hop on, grab the control stick to fly. Yunobo rides along automatically.

steps.t: Fly the glider and fire Yunobo at each of Moragia's three marbled-rock heads. Destroy all three and its body crumbles.

items.name: Yunobo's Charge
items.note: Aim from the glider and launch Yunobo at a head; each one downed cuts Moragia's health by a third.

steps.stuck: Aim with the camera and fire Yunobo's Charge at a head; each of the three heads dies in one hit. Bank side to side to dodge the lava-rock streams, and circle back if the angle's bad rather than tanking fireballs.

steps.t: Moragia spits fireballs and lava rocks at the glider. Keep moving and watch your Zonai battery so you don't drop into the lava.
With Moragia gone, dive into the Death Mountain crater chasm to descend into the Depths beneath Eldin.
Activate the Mustis Lightroot to light up the area and add it to your map for fast travel.

items.name: Mustis Lightroot
items.note: Lightroots are the Depths' answer to shrines; touch it to banish the gloom-dark nearby.

steps.t: Head west through the Depths toward Lost Gorondia, using carts, fans and Brightbloom Seeds for light until you reach the Fire Temple.

sections.name: Inside the Fire Temple
sections.sub: Ring five gongs to open the boss door

steps.t: The temple's central gate has five padlocks. Each lock opens when you ring its matching gong somewhere in the building. Any order works.
Explore the multi-level rooms and fire Yunobo's charge into each large gong. A solid hit rings it and pops one lock off the gate.
Aim Yunobo from the top of a ramp, not the bottom; he loses steam uphill. Use rail carts and fans to line up tricky gong angles.
Use Yunobo to smash the marbled rocks blocking paths, and Ascend through ceilings to reach gongs on the upper floors quickly.

items.name: Ascend
items.note: Swim up through any solid ceiling; great for skipping back up after a lower-floor gong.

steps.stuck: For the 5F gong, a metal block drops down a shaft; use Recall on it to ride it back up to the gong's level. Use Ascend through low ceilings to skip climbing whole floors.

steps.t: Watch for Hydrant Zonai devices: spray water onto lava to harden it into safe stepping platforms toward out-of-reach gongs.
Open chests along the way for arrows, weapons and a small key while hunting gongs.

items.name: Fire Temple chests
items.note: Side rooms hold useful gear; grab them before the boss door.

steps.t: With all five locks broken, fire Yunobo up the wall to smash the red boulders on the ceiling above the gate, dropping the temple boss.

sections.name: Boss: Marbled Gohma
sections.sub: The spider that spawned the rock roast

steps.t: Phase 1: Gohma stands on marbled-rock legs. Fire Yunobo's charge at a leg to shatter it; break enough and it collapses, exposing its eye.
steps.stuck: Fire Yunobo's Charge at a leg to crack it; break TWO legs and Gohma topples, exposing the glowing eye. Stand where you have a clean line to a leg so Yunobo doesn't fizzle on the way over.

steps.t: While it's down, climb onto the boss and wail on the glowing eye with your best melee weapon before it recovers.
It hurls explosive rocks and swipes with its legs when you're close. After enough eye hits it shakes you off and counters; back away when it stirs.
Phase 2: below half health it climbs onto the ceiling. Center the camera on the leg farthest from you and fire Yunobo to knock it down.
steps.stuck: On the ceiling, lock the camera on the leg FARTHEST from you (near legs are too steep for Yunobo's Charge to reach), fire to drop Gohma, then climb on and pound the eye. Repeat the far-leg shot each time.

steps.t: Pick the far leg each time; closer legs are hard for Yunobo to reach on the ceiling. Drop it, attack the eye, repeat until it falls.
Defeat Marbled Gohma to clear the Fire Temple and claim a Heart Container.

items.name: Heart Container
items.note: Permanently adds one full heart to your max health.

sections.name: Yunobo, Sage of Fire
sections.reward: Sage's Vow; Yunobo's Charge sage power

steps.t: With Gohma gone, the gloom-tainted Marbled Rock Roast loses its hold across Goron City and the Gorons snap back to normal.
Yunobo grants you his Sage's Vow, summoning his spirit to fight alongside you.

items.name: Sage's Vow (Yunobo)
items.note: Yunobo's avatar joins your party; activate his Charge to roll through rock and enemies.

items.name: Yunobo's Charge
items.note: Sage power: press the Sage button near Yunobo to launch a fiery rolling charge that smashes ore, rock and foes.

steps.t: Yunobo's Charge is great for ore deposits and breakable walls out in the field, not just combat. Keep him handy in Eldin.
Talk to the recovered Gorons and shopkeepers around Goron City; many reopen their stores and offer new dialogue and side quests.
Report back to advance the main quest. With Fire done, pursue the remaining temples and the wider Regional Phenomena story.

REGIONS.name: Water Temple
REGIONS.sub: Sidon of the Zora - Lanayru Regional Phenomenon
REGIONS.tagline: Clear the sludge drowning Zora's Domain, shoot open a fish-shaped sky island, and wash the muck monster Mucktorok out of the Water Temple.
REGIONS.champion: Vow of Sidon, Sage of Water

sections.name: Sludge Over Zora's Domain
sections.sub: Reach the Domain and find Prince Sidon

steps.t: Travel to Zora's Domain (northeast Lanayru). Sludge coats everything, the locals are suffering, and King Dorephan is gravely ill.
Sludge is sticky and damages over time. Wash it off by jumping in clean water or rolling, and clear it before it builds up on you.
Meet Prince Sidon at Mipha Court, the statue plaza near Ploymus Mountain. He asks for help, kicking off the main quest Sidon of the Zora.
Find King Dorephan hidden in the Pristine Sanctum behind a waterfall (between Mipha Court and Mikau Lake). Talk to him for the key item.
Receive 5 King's Scales from King Dorephan.

items.name: King's Scale
items.note: Fuse one to an arrow and shoot the floating teardrop near the sky island to open the way. You get five, so misses are forgiven.

steps.t: Grab the Zora Armor if you don't have it. It lets you swim up waterfalls, very handy around Zora's Domain and for the temple approach.

items.name: Zora Armor
items.note: Swim up waterfalls. Helpful for getting around the Domain and reaching the sky.

sections.name: The Fish-Shaped Sky Island
sections.sub: Shoot the teardrop to open the sludge source

steps.t: Look up to the fish-shaped Floating Scales Island in the sky, source of the sludge. That island is your target.
Get airborne to reach it: glide from a high point, or ride a falling sky-island chunk up using Recall.
steps.stuck: Easiest way: watch where a sky-rock chunk crashes down nearby, climb on top of it, then use Recall. Time rewinds and the rock carries you back up; jump off at the peak and glide to the island.

steps.t: Climb to the island's peak and look southwest to spot a cluster of floating rocks forming a giant teardrop shape hanging in the air.
Fuse a King's Scale to an arrow and fire it through the center of the teardrop. Jump first to slow time and steady your aim in midair.
steps.stuck: Hold ZR to aim the bow, press up on the d-pad and pick King's Scale to fuse it on the arrow, then fire through the dead center of the teardrop. Aim from the peak looking southwest so the rocks line up into the shape.

steps.t: The teardrop lights up and a pillar of light erupts from East Reservoir Lake, marking your next destination on the map.

sections.name: Sidon's Power of Water
sections.sub: Reach East Reservoir Lake and dive to the temple

steps.t: Head to East Reservoir Lake and meet Sidon at the green light in the water. He shares his Power of Water for the journey ahead.
Stand near Sidon and use the prompt to wrap yourself in his water bubble. Your attacks now fling water that washes off sludge.

items.name: Sidon's Power of Water
items.note: Sidon's water bubble: melee swings throw a tidal wave of water that strips sludge and breaks shields.

steps.t: Practice the loop on any sludge enemy: blast the muck off with the water bubble, then strike the exposed foe until it falls.
With Sidon along, dive into the whirlpool he forms at the green light. It pulls you up into the sky toward the floating Water Temple.
Re-grab Sidon's bubble any time it lapses. It refreshes for free and is your main tool for clearing sludge inside the temple.

sections.name: The Four Faucets
sections.sub: Restore water flow inside the Water Temple
sections.reward: Access to the boss; faucets can be done in any order

steps.t: Touch the central terminal to mark the four faucets on your map. Open all four to restore the flow and reach the boss. Any order works.
Most faucet wheels just need water hitting the paddles. Use Sidon's water blast, a nearby water bubble, or a water spout to spin them.
One faucet uses Ascend to reach a high platform; have Tulin's gust push a paddle, or carry water to the wheel to turn it open.
Sludge-blocked switches: clear the muck with Splash Fruit or Chuchu Jelly arrows, an Opal-fused weapon, or Sidon's water, then hit the switch.
For the fast-spinning box atop the tall tower, leap off, aim your bow in midair to slow time, and shoot the switch inside to drain the room.
steps.stuck: Climb the tower (use Hover Stones if needed), then jump off and hold ZR midair for bullet time to shoot the switch inside the spinning box. If sludge covers it, hit it first with a Splash Fruit or Chuchu Jelly arrow.

steps.t: Stuck on a wheel? Look for a water source nearby. Ultrahand and Recall can reposition or replay water bubbles and platforms to get flow going.
Open the chests around the faucets for arrows and useful materials while you explore.

items.name: Bundle of Arrows
items.note: Common temple chest reward; stock up before the boss.

sections.name: Boss: Mucktorok
sections.sub: Scourge of the Water Temple

steps.t: With all four faucets open, use the central pedestal to drop into the arena. Bring Sidon's Power of Water; it's the fastest way to expose the boss.
Phase 1: Mucktorok rides as a sludge shark, slamming shockwaves and firing a sludge beam. Hit it with water (Sidon's bubble) to wash off the sludge.
Washing it reveals a small Octorok. While it's exposed and flopping, rush in with melee. Repeat the wash-then-hit cycle to drop its health.
Phase 2 (half health): it coats the floor in sludge pools and hops between them while spewing muck. Wash a pool with water to deny it cover.
Catch the Octorok on cleared ground, stun it with a water hit, then close in and finish it with heavy attacks.
steps.stuck: Once washed, the exposed Octorok darts off to dive into fresh sludge, so chase it onto clean floor and unload your strongest melee at once. Staying in Sidon's bubble lets you wash and hit while shielded from its muck.

steps.t: Stay on clean tiles. Standing in sludge slows you and chips your health. Low gravity here lets you jump and glide over the wave and beam attacks.
Defeat Mucktorok for a Heart Container plus some Octorok materials, and the temple's sludge is cleared for good.

items.name: Heart Container
items.note: Permanent extra heart awarded for clearing the temple boss.

sections.name: Sidon's Vow
sections.sub: Awaken the Sage of Water
sections.reward: Vow of Sidon, Sage of Water

steps.t: After the fight, Sidon awakens as the Sage of Water. Speak with him to complete the main quest Sidon of the Zora.
Receive the Vow of Sidon. His avatar now follows you; trigger his water ability in the field to shield yourself and clear sludge.

items.name: Vow of Sidon, Sage of Water
items.note: Summon Sidon's water bubble in the overworld for an offensive and defensive water shield.

steps.t: Sidon's vow pairs well with archery: stand in his bubble so arrows pick up water, useful against fire foes and Gibdos later.
Return to King Dorephan and the now-clear Zora's Domain for grateful villagers, restocked shops, and follow-up side quests.

REGIONS.name: Lightning Temple
REGIONS.sub: Gerudo Desert - Riju, Sage of Lightning
REGIONS.tagline: Pierce the sand shroud, stand with Riju against the Gibdo swarm, and charge the temple's four batteries to face Queen Gibdo.

sections.name: The Sand Shroud and Gerudo Refuge
sections.sub: Reaching Gerudo Town and finding Riju

steps.t: Head to the Gerudo Desert in Hyrule's southwest. A thick sand shroud now blankets it, so visibility on the ground is near zero.
To navigate the shroud, climb high and paraglide, or pop a Zonai Rocket to rise above the haze where the map reads clearly again.
steps.stuck: The shroud kills your map. Stick to the road, and when you see a swirling air vortex jump in and open your paraglider to ride it above the haze; from up high the map re-syncs so you can spot Gerudo Town.

steps.t: Gibdos roam the sand. These mummy-like foes resist normal hits but crumble fast to electric attacks, so pack shock arrows or electric weapons.
Reach Gerudo Town. With the town evacuated, the residents shelter in the Gerudo Shelter cave. Head inside to find Riju and Buliara.
Speak with Riju and Buliara. Riju agrees to help you find the source of the sand shroud and confront whatever is sending the Gibdos.

sections.name: Riju's Lightning and the Town Defense
sections.sub: Learning her power and holding the line

steps.t: Riju shares her lightning power. She creates a charged field around your aim point; draw your bow inside it and fire to call a lightning strike.

items.name: Riju's Power of Lightning
items.note: Stand in Riju's electric field, aim an arrow, and release to call down a lightning bolt on the target.

steps.stuck: Riju walks behind you and casts a glowing electric field at your crosshair. Aim your bow so the target sits inside that ring, then release the arrow to drop the lightning bolt exactly there.

steps.t: Riju's lightning is your best tool against the Gibdo nests that keep spawning enemies, and it lights up dark spaces so you can see nearby walls.
Fend off the Gibdo assault on Gerudo Town. Clear the waves and protect Riju while you fight your way toward the desert beyond the walls.
Lightning conducts through water and metal. Don't fire Riju's lightning while standing in water or holding a metal weapon, or you'll shock yourself.
Stock up first: grab electric weapons, shock arrows, and plain arrows. You'll burn through arrows powering Riju's lightning all dungeon long.

sections.name: Reaching the Lightning Temple
sections.sub: Crossing the desert to the temple entrance

steps.t: With Riju along, ride out into the desert toward the source of the shroud, following the trail of Gibdos to the half-buried temple.
At the entrance you fight a preview battle against Queen Gibdo. You can't kill her yet; survive her charge, sand beam, and tornadoes to learn her patterns.
At the doors, a gloom cocoon blocks the way. Hit it with Riju's lightning, then loose an arrow into it to burst it open and step inside.

items.name: Riju's Power of Lightning
items.note: Zap the gloom cocoon over the door, then fire an arrow to break it and open the temple.

steps.stuck: First hit the purple gloom on the doors with Riju's lightning, then loose one arrow into it to burst it. Inside, swing the Korok Frond to clear sand off the floor switch, then stand on it to open the way.

steps.t: Just inside, grab the Korok Frond. Swing it to blow away mounds of sand covering devices and paths as you explore the temple.

items.name: Korok Frond
items.note: A leaf-fan weapon found inside; swing it to blow sand off hidden devices and platforms.

sections.name: Inside the Temple: Four Batteries
sections.sub: Light, mirrors, and powering the elevator

steps.t: Enter the main room and activate the central Zonai device. This unlocks the temple's travel point and sets the goal: charge four batteries.
The four batteries sit on different floors and can be charged in any order. Power all four to bring the central elevator online.
Each battery is reached by routing a beam of light through mirrors to a receptor. Use Ultrahand to grab and rotate mirror panels and aim the beam.

items.name: Ultrahand
items.note: Grab and rotate mirror panels to aim light beams at the receptors that open the way to each battery.

steps.stuck: Use Ultrahand to pull a mirror panel out of the sand, carry it into the room's light beam, then rotate it so the reflected beam lands on the receptor and locks in to power the battery's door.

steps.t: Watch for Soldier Constructs guarding the rooms, some perched on Hover Stones. Clear them, then steady the reflected beam so the receptor activates.
Use Ascend to reach higher floors and Recall to reverse rotating wheels and platforms when lining up a beam.

items.name: Ascend
items.note: Rise up through ceilings to reach battery platforms and upper floors.

items.name: Recall
items.note: Reverse moving platforms or wheels to line up a light beam.

steps.stuck: Ascend (jump menu, point up at a ceiling) to pop through floors instead of hunting for stairs. If a wheel or platform is spinning the wrong way mid-puzzle, give it a nudge then trigger Recall to send it back.

steps.t: At each battery, fire Riju's lightning at the prong on top to charge it. With all four charged, return to the center and ride the elevator to the boss.
All four batteries charged opens the way down. Reactivate the central Zonai device to power the elevator that carries you to Queen Gibdo.

sections.name: Boss: Queen Gibdo
sections.sub: The source of the sand shroud
sections.reward: Vow of Riju and a Heart Container

steps.t: Queen Gibdo's hardened shell shrugs off ordinary attacks. You must break it with Riju's lightning before any of your hits will land.
Stand in Riju's field, aim, and fire to zap the Queen. Her armor flakes off and she turns white and vulnerable for a short window.
While she's exposed, rush in and unload with your strongest melee weapon before the armor reforms. Repeat the shock-then-strike loop.
Dodge her three attacks: a forward charge, a ground-tracking sand beam, and summoned tornadoes. Keep moving and hold some distance to read them.
At about half health she enters phase two and summons Gibdos from the nests around the arena. Blast the nests with Riju's lightning, then resume the loop.
steps.stuck: In phase two, sprint around the arena blasting every glowing Gibdo hive with Riju's lightning. Clearing a hive drops a pillar of light that strips nearby Gibdos' armor and freezes them, then return to the Queen.

steps.t: Down Queen Gibdo to lift the sand shroud and free the Gerudo. Riju joins you as the Sage of Lightning, and you claim a Heart Container.

items.name: Vow of Riju
items.note: Sage of Lightning. Summon Riju's avatar in the field to fight alongside you.

items.name: Heart Container
items.note: Boss reward; permanently adds one heart to your max health.

sections.name: Using the Vow of Riju
sections.sub: The Sage of Lightning's power in the field

steps.t: Leave any settlement, then approach Riju's avatar in the field and press the prompt to activate her power, conjuring a lightning field around your aim.

items.name: Vow of Riju
items.note: Activate near Riju's avatar, then fire an arrow into her field to call a lightning strike on enemies.

steps.t: Fire an arrow inside the field to drop a lightning bolt that hits everything caught in it. Great for crowds, electric puzzles, and Gibdos anywhere.
The field also outlines nearby walls and mounds, so it doubles as a way to see and navigate dark areas like the Depths.
Lightning conducts through water and metal. Don't trigger it while standing in a puddle or holding a metal weapon, or you'll shock yourself.
Bring a Sage's Will to the Spirit Temple later to upgrade the Vow of Riju, raising its strength.

REGIONS.name: The Fifth Sage
REGIONS.sub: Main Quest: Guidance from Ages Past
REGIONS.tagline: Chase the masked light to the Construct Factory, build Mineru a body, then duel the Seized Construct to wake the Sage of Spirit.

sections.name: Dragonhead Island & the Mask
sections.sub: Sky layer above Faron

steps.t: This quest opens after the four temples. Bring plenty of hearts and stock Zonai devices like wings, fans, and a steering stick.
Travel to Dragonhead Island in the sky, the large stone dragon-head ruin floating above the Faron region. Use a tower launch or skydive in.
steps.stuck: Easiest route: from Popla Foothills Skyview Tower, skydive into Thunderhead Isles and ride the chain to its end, then press A to dive down onto Dragonhead Island itself.

steps.t: Reach Joku-u Shrine on the island. Just past it, pick up the Zonai mask that emits a green homing light beam.
Build a flying machine (wings or fans plus a steering stick) and Ultrahand the mask onto the front so the green beam points your flight path.

items.name: Ultrahand
items.note: Attach the mask to your build so its light guides you.

steps.stuck: Make wings or a few fans plus a steering stick, then Ultrahand the mask onto the very front so the green beam shines forward. If the beam points sideways, the nose is aimed wrong, so re-grab and rotate the mask.

steps.t: Follow the green beam down to the surface. It leads to a large stone owl statue with a pedestal out front, near Tobio's Hollow Chasm.
steps.stuck: From the island, build your flying machine and just follow the green beam down. It lands at an owl statue on the surface near Tobio's Hollow Chasm, with a pedestal out front where the mask goes.

steps.t: Land and set the mask on the owl's pedestal. A cutscene plays and the platform lowers you into the Depths toward the Construct Factory.

sections.name: The Construct Factory
sections.sub: Faron Depths

steps.t: The platform drops you at the Construct Factory in the Depths, where Mineru's spirit greets you and asks you to build her a body.
First, rotate the Zonai mask and set it into the head slot on the construct frame at the central platform before gathering the limbs.

items.name: Ultrahand
items.note: Rotate and seat the mask into the head socket.

steps.stuck: Grab the mask with Ultrahand and rotate it (hold R, then use the D-pad) until its outline lights up in the head slot, then release. It only locks in one orientation, and goes in before the limbs.

steps.t: Light a nearby Lightroot if you pass one to brighten the Depths. The four limb depots branch off around the central factory.
Visit the four storehouses in any order: Left-Arm Depot, Right-Arm Depot, Left-Leg Depot, and Right-Leg Depot. Solve each room's Zonai puzzle to claim its part.

items.name: Ultrahand
items.note: Most depot puzzles use Ultrahand to move parts, rails, and platforms.

steps.t: Carry each retrieved limb out yourself or build a hauler. If you drop one, your map still marks the depot so you can backtrack.
Return to the frame and use Ultrahand to attach each arm and leg to its correct socket. Once all parts fit, Mineru's Construct comes to life.

sections.name: Piloting Mineru's Construct
sections.sub: To the Spirit Temple

steps.t: Climb aboard and pilot Mineru's Construct. Moving steadily drains its energy, so let it rest to recharge as you travel and watch the gauge.
Fuse weapons to the construct's hands. Mineru suggests a Spiked Iron Ball and a Shock Emitter; a Zonai Cannon also helps a lot for the boss.

items.name: Fuse
items.note: Attach a Spiked Iron Ball and a Shock Emitter (and a Cannon if you have one).

items.name: Spiked Iron Ball
items.note: Heavy melee hits to combo the boss while it is down.

items.name: Shock Emitter
items.note: Zonai device; stuns the boss so you can close in.

steps.t: Follow the path through the Depths, clearing Constructs as practice, until you reach the entrance to the Spirit Temple.
If the energy gauge empties the construct slows and stalls. Pause to let it recharge, or stock extra Zonai Charges before the boss arena.

sections.name: Boss: Seized Construct
sections.sub: Spirit Temple

steps.t: Stay aboard Mineru's Construct. The arena floor is covered in Gloom that drains Link fast on foot, and the ring is fenced by barbed wire.
Phase 1: guard its telegraphed punches, then counter. Stun it with the Shock Emitter, walk in, and land spiked-ball hits to shove it into the wire fence.
steps.stuck: Fire the Shock Emitter or a Zonai Cannon from range to stun it, then rush in and swing the spiked ball; a few hits knock it into the electric fence ringing the arena, where the real damage is done.

steps.t: Knocking it into the barbed-wire fence is the big damage. Circle the boss to dodge its melee swings and ranged shots between knockdowns.
Phase 2: it grows extra arms and fuses a Rocket to fly, firing Cannons. Hit it with a Cannon shot to slam it back down, then resume your combos.
steps.stuck: When it bolts a rocket on and lifts off firing cannons, hit it with one Cannon blast to slam it back to the ground, then go back to stun-and-spiked-ball to knock it into the fence.

steps.t: Beat the Seized Construct to earn a Heart Container.

items.name: Heart Container
items.note: Adds one full heart to your maximum.

sections.name: The Sage of Spirit
sections.sub: Quest reward

steps.t: Mineru is freed as the fifth sage. She grants the Vow of Mineru, joining your party as the Sage of Spirit.

items.name: Vow of Mineru
items.note: Sage of Spirit; summons her construct avatar to fight at your side.

steps.t: Mineru's construct avatar charges in and pounds enemies, and rounds out all five sage vows.
With all five sages secured, you're ready for the endgame. Pursue the Trail of the Master Sword and the final approach to Ganondorf.

REGIONS.name: Find the Princess
REGIONS.tagline: Follow the Dragon's Tears, face the false Zelda at Hyrule Castle, claim the Master Sword, and ready yourself for the Depths.

sections.name: The Dragon's Tears
sections.sub: Read the geoglyphs and uncover Zelda's fate
sections.reward: Story memories that reveal Zelda's journey to the past

steps.t: Find the first geoglyph by New Serenne Stable in Hyrule Ridge, then head to the Forgotten Temple northwest of there to begin The Dragon's Tears.
steps.stuck: Impa stands across the road from New Serenne Stable beside a broken hot-air balloon. Use Ultrahand to refit the burner basket, drop flint and wood under it to light it, and ride it up to reach the first geoglyph.

steps.t: Meet Impa and Cado at the Forgotten Temple. They reveal a floor map marking every geoglyph and point you toward the rest.

items.name: Impa
items.note: Quest-giver who studies the geoglyphs from her hot-air balloon.

steps.t: Touch the Dragon's Tear in the first geoglyph, the King Rauru drawing by New Serenne Stable, to watch its memory cutscene.
steps.stuck: Float up over the Rauru drawing first to spot it, then drop in. The real tear is the one solid white teardrop (the rest are hollow); it sits near the top, in the figure's left eye. Touch it to play the memory.

steps.t: Visit all 11 geoglyphs across the surface and touch each Dragon's Tear, the solid teardrop among the hollow ones, to collect its memory.
Activate Skyview Towers first. From the air you can spot the huge ground drawings and pin each geoglyph on your Purah Pad map.
steps.stuck: Map each glyph from the sky: glide off a Skyview Tower or use the Purah Pad's scope to pin the spot, then dive with the paraglider. Recall a fallen object or build a Zonai flier if you overshoot.

steps.t: Memories play out of order, so the story may feel scrambled. Viewing them by number in your Adventure Log keeps things clear.
These memories are heavy spoilers for the whole plot. They are optional, but they explain who you have really been chasing.

sections.name: The Twelfth Tear
sections.sub: Chase the dragon for the final memory
sections.reward: The truth: Zelda became the Light Dragon

steps.t: After the 11 geoglyph memories, you're told a final, 12th tear waits in the Akkala region. A new marker appears far to the northeast.
Travel to the tip of Rist Peninsula in Akkala and touch the final Dragon's Tear resting there.

items.name: Light Dragon
items.note: The eternal dragon that circles Hyrule; the final tear reveals its true identity.

steps.stuck: Rist Peninsula is the spiral-shaped beach in far eastern Akkala. There's no geoglyph here; the tear sits in the open near Gemimik Shrine. Fast-travel or launch from the Ulri Mountain Skyview Tower and glide in.

steps.t: The last memory confirms Zelda swallowed her Secret Stone and became the Light Dragon to one day restore the broken Master Sword.
Remember this. The Light Dragon's flight path now matters: she carries the Master Sword you'll claim later in this chapter.

sections.name: Crisis at Hyrule Castle
sections.sub: Chase the Zelda sightings into the throne room

steps.t: At Lookout Landing, Purah's telescope spots Princess Zelda at risen Hyrule Castle. Speak with Purah to start Crisis at Hyrule Castle.

items.name: Purah
items.note: Lookout Landing's lead researcher; sends you to the castle after the Zelda sighting.

steps.t: Cross to Hyrule Castle, now floating above its moat. Climb or use Ascend to follow the figure of Zelda deeper inside.
Chase the phantom Zelda through the halls. She lures you on, and enemies like Shock Likes block the way as you press toward the throne room.
In the throne room the false Zelda vanishes and the real threat appears: Phantom Ganon, a puppet of Ganondorf, attacks in multiple forms.

items.name: Phantom Ganon
items.note: Ganondorf's puppet boss; several spawn at once, then a second wave adds gloom to the floor.

steps.t: Defeat every Phantom Ganon to win. Summon your sages to keep the copies busy so you can fight them one at a time.
steps.stuck: Dodge a Phantom Ganon's swing and counter on the Flurry Rush prompt for fast damage. Summon Tulin/Yunobo/etc. so the sages tie up the copies, and use Riju's lightning to zap several at once. Two waves total.

steps.t: You earn a Heart Container. The castle Zelda was a fake; return to Purah to close the quest and begin Find the Fifth Sage.
Gloom lowers your max hearts. Bring sunny dishes made with sundelions to recover, and strong fused weapons for the Phantom Ganon fight.

sections.name: Recovering the Hero's Sword
sections.sub: Cleanse Korok Forest and seek the Deku Tree
sections.reward: A quest marker tracking the Master Sword

steps.t: Head for the Lost Woods and Korok Forest. The surface path is sealed by gloom fog, so you'll need to reach the Great Deku Tree from below.
Drop into the Depths via a nearby chasm, then use Ascend to rise up inside the Deku Tree and defeat the Gloom Hands and Phantom Ganon at the source.

items.name: Gloom Hands
items.note: Crawling gloom arms; fire and bomb flowers work well, and a Phantom Ganon appears once they fall.

steps.stuck: Enter the Depths via the nearest chasm, travel under Korok Forest, then Ascend up through a ceiling near Korok Grove to surface inside the Deku Tree. Beat the Gloom Spawn and Phantom Ganon there to lift the fog.

steps.t: With the forest cleansed, speak with the Great Deku Tree to start Recovering the Hero's Sword. He confirms the Master Sword rests with the Light Dragon.

items.name: Great Deku Tree
items.note: Guardian of Korok Forest; points you to the Master Sword on the dragon.

steps.t: The quest adds a marker that always points to the Master Sword, tracking the Light Dragon wherever she flies.

sections.name: The Master Sword
sections.sub: Pull the blade from the Light Dragon

steps.t: Pulling the sword drains stamina fast. You need two full stamina wheels, so upgrade with Stamina Vessels and Lights of Blessing first.
Track the Light Dragon along her slow circuit. Launch from a Skyview Tower or sky island and glide down onto her back.
steps.stuck: There's an updraft all around the dragon: open your paraglider near her body for a free full stamina refill, even mid-flight. Glide along her broad back toward the head; eat stamina meals here if you need more.

steps.t: Walk along the dragon's spine toward her head. Tulin's gust helps cover distance if you fall short on the glide.

items.name: Tulin's Gust
items.note: Rito sage ability; a burst of wind that extends your glide to reach the dragon.

steps.t: At the dragon's head, hold the interact button as she shakes to draw out the restored Master Sword, the Blade of Evil's Bane.

items.name: Master Sword
items.note: Legendary sword; never breaks, recharges after use, and doubles its power against Ganondorf and Phantom Ganon.

steps.stuck: At the head, hold the interact button to pull; keep holding as your stamina slowly drains, no mashing. If your wheel empties before it comes free, reopen your paraglider in the dragon's updraft to refill, then retry.

steps.t: No safe landing spot? Stand on the dragon's broad back to refill stamina between attempts, then move up when your wheel is full.
While riding, harvest dragon parts. Touching the Light Dragon's scales, claws, fangs, or horn yields rare crafting materials.

items.name: Light Dragon's Scale
items.note: Rare fuse and upgrade material gathered by touching the dragon's body.

sections.name: Preparing for the Depths
sections.sub: Gear up before going underground
sections.reward: A safer descent into the dark

steps.t: Find a chasm on the surface and drop in to enter the Depths, the pitch-black world layer beneath Hyrule.
Activate Lightroots to light up areas and reveal the map. Each Lightroot sits directly below a surface shrine.

items.name: Lightroot
items.note: Glowing root that illuminates a chunk of the Depths and acts as a fast-travel point.

steps.t: Stock Brightbloom Seeds. Throw them for light, or fuse them to arrows to scout ahead and reveal nearby Lightroots in the dark.

items.name: Brightbloom Seed
items.note: Glowing seed; throw or fuse to arrows to light the Depths.

steps.t: Gloom covers much of the Depths floor and lowers your max hearts. Bring sundelion dishes to recover safely.
The Master Sword and gloom-resistant gear like the Depths armor sets make exploring far less punishing down there.
Use Autobuild to quickly rebuild light-rigged vehicles or bridges once you've collected Zonaite and schematics in the Depths.

items.name: Autobuild
items.note: Recreates saved or recent builds, great for traversing the vast dark Depths.

REGIONS.name: Imprisoning the Demon King
REGIONS.tagline: Plunge into the chasm below Hyrule Castle, fight through the Demon King's Army, and end the war with Ganondorf for good.

sections.name: Before You Drop In
sections.sub: Get ready for the point of no return

steps.t: Once you reach the final arena you cannot leave or warp out without reloading a save. Finish your prep beforehand.
This quest is the endgame, not the end of the game. Beating it returns you to before the descent, so feel free to dive when ready.
Cook a stack of Sunny meals with Sundelions to recover hearts lost to Gloom, plus your best hearty or full-heal dishes.

items.name: Sundelion
items.note: Cook into Sunny dishes to restore Gloom-locked hearts during the fight.

steps.t: Equip Gloom-resist gear if you have it. The Depths armor set or Gloom-resist food eases the long descent through the chasm.

items.name: Depths Armor Set
items.note: Optional. Reduces Gloom buildup while traversing the chasm; not required if you carry Sunny food.

steps.t: Bring the Master Sword if you have it. You can still start the fight without it, but you will want it for the Demon Dragon finale.

items.name: Master Sword
items.note: Strongly recommended for the dragon. If missing, Zelda the Light Dragon delivers it before that phase.

steps.t: Pack plenty of arrows plus Bomb Flowers, Puffshrooms, and tough Fuse materials. Stock shields and a few spare weapons too.

sections.name: Into the Chasm
sections.sub: Descend beneath Hyrule Castle

steps.t: Travel to Hyrule Castle and dive into the Hyrule Castle Chasm. Paraglide down into the dark and land safely in the Depths below.
steps.stuck: The Hyrule Castle Chasm is the dark hole just north of Lookout Landing, under the castle. Fast-travel to Serutabomac Shrine on the castle's northeast side and jump off that cliff into the opening to drop straight in.

steps.t: Activate the Lightroot at the bottom to light the area and set a fast-travel point right at the start of the descent.
Follow the path downward. Slip past Horriblins and Like Likes; you do not have to clear every enemy to keep moving.
Lynels roam parts of the route. Fight one for great loot or sprint past to the next passage if you would rather save resources.
Pass the Forgotten Foundation and the familiar opening-scene mural, then reach the Imprisoning Chamber where Ganondorf was sealed.
Blast open the path and dive off the platform into the hole below to fall into Gloom's Lair. A cutscene plays, then the gauntlet begins.

sections.name: The Demon King's Army
sections.sub: Survive the gauntlet of monsters
sections.reward: The sages rejoin you for the battle

steps.t: Battle four waves of the army: a swarm of Bokoblins with a Boss Bokoblin, then Lizalfos, then Gibdos, and finally Moblins.
Any sages you have unlocked fight alongside you here. Stand near a sage and press the prompt to trigger their ability and thin the crowd fast.
Stun Gibdos with a Dazzlefruit blast, a Splash Fruit, or any elemental hit to break their armor, then strike while they are exposed.
steps.stuck: Plain swings and arrows do only 1 damage to Gibdos. Hit one with fire, ice, or shock (a Ruby/Topaz-fused weapon, a Splash/Ice/Fire Fruit arrow, or Riju's lightning) to crack its armor, then they take normal damage.

steps.t: The floor pools with Gloom in spots. Avoid standing in it; eat a Sunny dish if your max hearts shrink too far.
Clear all the waves to trigger a cutscene. The sages pledge to fight with you, then Ganondorf rises for the final battle.

sections.name: Demon King Ganondorf: Phases 1 & 2
sections.sub: Sword duel, then the Phantom Ganons

steps.t: If you arrive without the Master Sword, a cutscene has Zelda the Light Dragon deliver it before the dragon phase. Having it early is easier.
Phase 1: Ganondorf cycles one-handed, two-handed, spear, and bow attacks. Watch his telegraphed swings, dodge late, and Flurry Rush for free hits.
His attacks deal Gloom damage that locks off hearts. Keep distance, heal with Sunny food, and do not over-commit.
Phase 2: He summons five Phantom Ganon copies. Send your sages to occupy the phantoms while you focus the real Ganondorf.
When he hurls Gloom projectiles, use Recall to fling one straight back at him, opening a window to rush in and attack.
At the halfway mark Ganondorf absorbs the phantoms and banishes your sages, ending the phase and starting the one-on-one fight.

sections.name: Demon King Ganondorf: Phase 3
sections.sub: One-on-one with the secret stone

steps.t: Ganondorf draws on his secret stone and powers up alone. His attacks shift to fast melee combos and Gloom magic AoE blasts.
Keep dodging into Flurry Rushes; it is your safest damage. The Master Sword hits especially hard against the Demon King's power.
Beware his unblockable Gloom-hand grab and ground eruptions. Stay mobile and bait attacks rather than trading blows.
Empty his health to trigger a long cutscene: Ganondorf swallows his secret stone whole and transforms into the Demon Dragon.

sections.name: The Demon Dragon
sections.sub: Aerial finale with the Light Dragon

steps.t: You skydive onto the Demon Dragon's back. The Light Dragon, Zelda, soars beside you to carry you back up whenever you fall.
Use the Master Sword here; it deals huge bonus damage to the dragon's weak points and trivializes the phase. It is delivered now if you lacked it.
Glide and run along the dragon, slashing the clusters of glowing demonic eyes set in the Gloom on its back and limbs until they are destroyed.
steps.stuck: The weak points are the four red blistered eye-clusters bulging from the Gloom on the dragon's back. Run or glide up to each and slash it with the Master Sword until it pops; clear all four to expose the head.

steps.t: With the back weak points cleared, climb to the head and strike the secret stone glowing on the dragon's forehead.
Dive-attack the eyes and the head stone for big damage, or fire arrows mid-dive, then glide back up as the dragon recovers. Repeat the cycle.
steps.stuck: Dive straight down onto a weak point and slash it, or fire arrows on the way down. If you fall off, the Light Dragon swoops under to carry you back up, so you can dive again and again without fear.

steps.t: Shatter the forehead secret stone to defeat the Demon King for good and trigger the ending. The war for Hyrule is over.

sections.name: The Ending
sections.sub: Zelda returned, Hyrule at peace

steps.t: As they fall through the sky, Link reaches out and catches Zelda mid-air. The two reunite as the dragons fade.
Channeling Rauru and Sonia's power through his arm, Link recalls Zelda's true form, restoring her from dragon to human despite the supposedly irreversible change.
Enjoy the credits and the reunion with Purah, the sages, and your friends. Hyrule begins to rebuild in peace.
After the credits roll, your save loads back to just before the final dive, with a star marking your completed playthrough.
Return to the world to finish shrines, Lightroots, side quests, the Master Kohga arc, and the rest of the map at your pace.

shrines.name: Ukouh Shrine
shrines.location: Southwest of the Temple of Time, just past the first pond at the start of the game.
shrines.oneLine: Grants Ultrahand. Build a bridge across the gap, then attach a hook to a plank and ride it along the rail to reach the exit.
shrines.solution: Touch the pedestal to receive Ultrahand. You're meant to build, not climb. Use Ultrahand to grab the large boards lying on the ground and lay one across the first gap to make a bridge, then walk over. At the next gap, line a board up across it; if one won't reach, attach a second board to the first (move them together until they turn yellow to show they'll stick, then press the attach button) to extend it. For the optional chest on a raised stone ledge in the corner, grab a long slab and prop it as a ramp to reach it, then open it for a piece of Amber. At the last section, grab the hook lying nearby, attach it to the middle of a board so the hook catches the overhead rail, then jump on and ride it across. Step off at the far end and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: In-isa Shrine
shrines.location: West side of the island, south across the frozen lake near a stone ruin and the In-isa cold cave.
shrines.oneLine: Grants Fuse. Fuse a rock to your sword, fire fruit to arrows, and a rock to your shield to clear the rooms, then beat the Construct.
shrines.solution: Touch the pedestal to receive Fuse, which sticks a material onto your weapon, shield, or arrows. Pick up the Rusty Claymore on the ground, open Fuse, and stick one of the boulders to it to make a heavy Boulder Hammer, then smash the cracked stone door to move on. In the next room you can also fuse a boulder to your shield to try it out. Grab the arrows and Old Wooden Bow, then fuse a Fire Fruit to an arrow (hold the aim button and press up on the D-pad) and shoot the dried leaves on the high wooden shelf to burn them away and drop a chest holding a Small Key. For an optional chest, smash the cracked stone pillar in the far corner with your boulder weapon to drop a chest with 5 Arrows. Use the Small Key on the locked door upstairs, then beat the Captain Construct (fused fire arrows work well), smash the last cracked wall, and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Gutanbac Shrine
shrines.location: Snowy peak in the island's upper northwest area, reached by climbing up through the cold caves.
shrines.oneLine: Grants Ascend. Ascend through ceilings to bypass obstacles, including rising through a moving spike door, then defeat the Construct.
shrines.solution: Touch the pedestal to receive Ascend, which lets you swim straight up through any ceiling and pop out on top. Walk to the end of the path, aim Ascend at the ceiling and rise through it. For the optional chests, stand under the shorter block on the left and Ascend up into it to reach a chest with a Stone Axe; later, after climbing higher, a second chest holds a Construct Bow. A Soldier Construct guards the way, so shoot its glowing eye to take it down quickly. Break the wooden crates blocking a small alcove on the right, step inside, and Ascend through that ceiling to reach the upper level. At the end, a hanging slab blocks you, so cut its two ropes (an arrow works) to drop it, then Ascend up through it. For the last gap, stand under the moving spike-topped platform, Ascend up into it, ride it until it lines up beneath the ledge above, and Ascend again to reach the exit. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Nachoyah Shrine
shrines.location: Near the Temple of Time; unlocks after the first three shrines and getting Recall at the Temple of Time.
shrines.oneLine: Practice Recall. Rewind water-borne planks and waterwheels to cross, rewind a cogwheel to climb to a chest, then stack and rewind the clock hands to open the exit.
shrines.solution: This shrine opens after you clear the first three Great Sky Island shrines and earn Recall at the Temple of Time; Recall rewinds an object back along the path it just moved. Inside, get onto one of the floating planks in the water, then aim Recall at it to send it rewinding back the way it drifted and carry you across. Hop onto the next raft that comes down the waterfall and use Recall on it to ride it back up the falls to the higher area. For the optional chest, find the large spinning cogwheel, use Recall on it so it reverses, jump on, and let it carry you up to the ledge with a chest holding 10 Arrows. At the exit, a gate is blocked by two clock hands that only line up briefly; Recall them while they're aligned to hold the gate open, or grab one hand with Ultrahand and attach it to the other so they stay stuck together. Pass through and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing and finish the Great Sky Island.

shrines.name: Kyononis Shrine
shrines.location: Hyrule Castle Town Ruins central square, north of Lookout Landing
shrines.oneLine: Combat Training: a training Construct teaches side hop, backflip, perfect guard (parry) and charged attacks; clear the drills to finish.
shrines.solution: This is the Combat Training shrine, so there's no puzzle - just follow the drills a Soldier Construct walks you through. First it teaches the Side Hop: ZL-target the Construct, then dodge sideways the instant it does a vertical (overhead) slash to trigger a perfect dodge. Next is the Backflip: dodge backward when it does a horizontal (sideways) swing. Then the Parry: raise your shield and parry right as its attack lands to knock it back. Finally the Charged Attack: hold the attack button to wind up, then release. Do each move when prompted and the gate opens. Open the chest in the altar room for a Zonaite Sword, then walk to the monk's altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Yamiyo Shrine
shrines.location: Romani Plains, east of Hyrule Castle Town Ruins, northeast of Lookout Landing
shrines.oneLine: Combat Training: Throwing - learn to throw materials and fused weapons as projectiles, then defeat the Constructs to pass.
shrines.solution: This is the Combat Training: Throwing shrine, where your normal melee attacks are disabled, so you fight by throwing things. Pick up the Fire Fruit growing inside the room, then hold R to bring up your materials, press up on the D-pad to select the Fire Fruit, aim at the Soldier Construct and release to throw it. After the first hit the Construct starts moving around; keep your aim on it (it's easiest to throw the moment it stops) and pelt it until it's destroyed. You can also throw or fuse-throw any handy weapon. With the Construct down the back door opens. Open the chest beyond it for three Bomb Flowers, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Teniten Shrine
shrines.location: South of Lookout Landing near Lake Kolomo, Hyrule Field
shrines.oneLine: Combat Training: Throwing - ZL-target and throw weapons to hit out-of-reach Constructs; aim carefully to clear the room.
shrines.solution: This is a Combat Training: Throwing shrine, so you clear it by throwing weapons rather than swinging them. A Training Construct stands on a raised platform out of melee reach. Pick from the assortment of weapons offered (you can also throw items from your own inventory), then hold R to aim at the Construct and release R to throw. Land a hit, and for the second part the Construct starts moving - track it with your aim and throw again to finish it. The barred door then opens. Head up to the treasure chest just past it for a Zonaite Spear, then walk to the monk's altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Kamizun Shrine
shrines.location: West of East Post Ruins, southern Hyrule Field
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Beginner - stripped of gear, grab only the weapons in the arena; fuse a spiked ball and defeat every enemy to pass.
shrines.solution: This is a Proving Grounds: Beginner shrine, so all your gear is stripped at the door and you fight with only what's in the room. First grab the starter kit lying nearby: the Old Wooden Bow, Old Wooden Shield, Wooden Stick and ten Arrows. Use these on the Soldier Constructs roaming the floor (they're weak). By the gate to the altar you'll find a Spiked-Iron Ball and a Long Stick; use Fuse to combine them into a heavy spiked club that hits much harder. One archer Construct stands on a raised pillar near the gate - pick it off with arrows, or smash the pillar out from under it with the spiked club so it drops into melee range. Clear them all and the gate opens; grab the Spring Shield from the chest inside, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Jojon Shrine
shrines.location: Crenel Peak Cave, east of Lookout Landing across the river, Hyrule Field
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Rotation - enter with no gear; dodge the rotating flame emitters and use arena weapons to defeat all enemies.
shrines.solution: This is a Proving Grounds: Rotation shrine, so your gear is stripped at the door and the room is full of spinning hazards. Grab the weapons lying around and turn the room's own flame emitters against the Soldier Constructs: with Ultrahand you can pick up a rotating flame emitter and plant it next to a Construct (or fuse one to your shield), or lure enemies into the spinning flames. Pick off the archer Constructs up high with your bow. To reach the upper level, use Recall on the big rotating vertical gear to reverse its spin and ride it up. Defeat every Construct to open the gate and get your gear back; open the chest beyond it for a Magic Rod, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Ishodag Shrine
shrines.location: Rocky hill west of Hyrule Castle Town Ruins, Hyrule Field
shrines.oneLine: An Uplifting Device: attach Zonai fans/lift devices with Ultrahand to raise platforms and carry yourself up to the exit.
shrines.solution: Despite the 'uplifting' label in our notes, the game calls this A Windy Device, and you solve it with Ultrahand and Zonai fans. First set a fan on the floor pointing up, hit it to switch it on, and ride the updraft with your paraglider to the next ledge. Next attach a fan to the wooden plank on the water (fan facing off one end), hop on and turn it on to motor across. For the side treasure, a long platform on a pivot has a chest end; attach a fan to the opposite end and switch it on to tip the platform level, then use Ascend to pop up onto it and open the chest for an Opal. For the exit, attach two fans to the underside of the lift platform at the back, both facing down, stand on it and turn them on to ride it up to the top ledge. Then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Sinakawak Shrine
shrines.location: Near New Serenne Stable, northwest Hyrule Field
shrines.oneLine: An Uplifting Device: build with fans and platforms so a lifting rig rises high enough to reach the upper ledge.
shrines.solution: This is An Uplifting Device shrine, where you build little hot-air rigs (a flame source heats a balloon to float upward). For the first lift, use Ultrahand to attach one of the lit torch/flame pillars to a wooden platform, then attach a Balloon on top of it; stand on the platform and it rises - hop off on the higher ledge. Next, attach a torch to a balloon and float it up under the door's ceiling button to open the gate. In the lower chamber with two orbs, a balloon and flame emitters: stack the smaller orb on the larger one, attach a balloon to it and a flame emitter to the balloon so it floats to the top, then use Ascend in the nearby nook to get back up. Up top, place the larger orb on the right vessel to open a side room with a chest holding an Opal, and the smaller orb on the left vessel to open the gate to the altar. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Jiosin Shrine
shrines.location: South of Lookout Landing, beside the Hyrule Field Chasm
shrines.oneLine: Shape Rotation: rotate the irregular stone blocks with Ultrahand so they pass through the matching symbol-shaped holes.
shrines.solution: This is the Shape Rotation shrine: use Ultrahand to turn each oddly shaped stone block so its profile matches the symbol-shaped hole in the wall, then pull it through. For the first block, line up its outline with the X-shaped hole and slide it out (it's often easiest to grab and rotate it from the far side). Once it's free, lay it across the nearby gap to make a bridge. In the next room rotate the block to fit its hole the same way. For the treasure, look to a chest on a higher ledge: stand a freed block upright as a step and use Ascend to rise through it onto the platform; the chest holds a Hasty Elixir. For the exit, stand the final block upright on the raised platform and use Ascend to reach the top. Then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Susuyai Shrine
shrines.location: Passeri Greenbelt, southwest of Lookout Landing, Hyrule Field
shrines.oneLine: A Spinning Device: use Zonai small wheels to power and time the spinning carts so they carry you across to the exit.
shrines.solution: This is A Spinning Device shrine, which teaches the Small Wheel Zonai device. A line of carts rides past on a track; the middle cart carries a treasure chest, so use Ultrahand to grab that cart, lift the chest off onto safe ground, and open it for five Arrows. For the gate ahead, a crank normally has to be held to keep the gate up - detach a Small Wheel from a cart, attach it to the steering crank and switch it on so it keeps cranking on its own while you walk under. At the final gap, take a Small Wheel and attach it to the hanging platform so it grips the rail above, point it the right way, hop on and activate it so the spinning wheel pulls the platform across to the exit. Then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Mayachin Shrine
