shrines.location: North of Hyrule Field Skyview Tower, next to Exchange Ruins, Hyrule Field
shrines.oneLine: A Fixed Device: anchor and arrange Zonai devices so a fixed mechanism moves you or objects to open the path.
shrines.solution: This is A Fixed Device shrine - a sort of Zonai baseball: you build a swinging bat and use it to knock a rolling ball into a target. Step on the orange floor switch by the pillar to make the orange target appear. Then pull a green Stake from the ground with Ultrahand, jam it into the flat side of the rotating device, and attach a wooden log to the end of the stake to give it bat-like reach. Go back up and hit the orange pillar with a weapon at the moment the ball lines up so the bat swings and knocks it into the target, opening the way to the altar. For the bonus chest, a second target is blocked by a hanging stone slab; attach stakes to the slab and rotate it with Ultrahand so the stakes pin into the ceiling, turning the slab into a second bat - then time the swing to send the ball into that target. The chest holds an Energizing Elixir. Then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Tajikats Shrine
shrines.location: Near Riverside Stable, south of Lookout Landing, Hyrule Field
shrines.oneLine: Building With Logs: use Ultrahand to assemble logs into a bridge or raft to cross the water and reach the altar.
shrines.solution: This is a puzzle shrine called Building With Logs, and the idea is to assemble logs with Ultrahand rather than climb. In the first area grab a log and lean it as a ramp to reach the higher ledge. Further on, glue logs together to span the gaps: attach two logs end to end to make one long beam and lay it across a chasm to walk over, and where you need to climb higher, fuse several logs into one long pole. Held pieces glow yellow when they will stick, and pressing up on the D-pad detaches a mistake. The last room is water: grab about three logs and attach them side by side into a flat raft, stick a Zonai fan on the back so it drives you forward, then ride it across. There's an optional treasure chest off to the left wall holding a Spiky Shield, reachable by steering your raft over to it. Step onto the pad and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Kyokugon Shrine
shrines.location: Great Plateau foothill, southwest Hyrule Field
shrines.oneLine: Alignment of the Circles: rotate and line up the circular gears/discs with Ultrahand so the mechanism unlocks the way forward.
shrines.solution: This puzzle shrine is called Alignment of the Circles, and the trick is to read the ceiling. Look up: there are green glowing glyphs marking certain spots, and on the floor there are bowl-shaped receptacles. Grab the round stone spheres sitting around the room with Ultrahand and drop each one into the pit that sits directly beneath a green glyph on the ceiling. Match every glyph and the gate opens. In the next area there's a hidden bit: use Ultrahand to lift the loose stone floor tile on your left, which uncovers another receptacle, then grab the nearby stone sphere and drop it in to open a side gate to a treasure chest. That chest holds a Hearty Elixir. Continue to the back and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Tsutsu-um Shrine
shrines.location: South of Outskirts Stable, southwest Hyrule Field
shrines.oneLine: The Stakes Guide You: drive and use the protruding stakes as steps and anchors to climb and reach the exit.
shrines.solution: This puzzle shrine is called The Stakes Guide You, and stakes are how you pin and steer things. At the start, use Ultrahand to grab a stake and stick it into the rising-and-falling pillar when it's low, then ride the attached platform up and paraglide to the higher ledge. For the optional chest, attach a metal panel to a stake, set it on the posts above you as a ledge, then use Ascend to pop up through it; that chest holds 5 Arrows. The main puzzle is getting a glowing ball into a hole on the far rail. The ball falls from a spinning panel, so stick stakes to keep it on course and stop or steer the rotating mechanism with a stake so the ball lands on the rails, then attach a stake to the spinning panel's edge so it nudges the ball along the rail and into the hole, opening the gate. Walk to the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Riogok Shrine
shrines.location: East of Hyrule Field Mini Stable, west of Hopper Pond, Hyrule Field
shrines.oneLine: Force Transfer: route Zonai energy/motion through connected devices so the transferred force opens the gates.
shrines.solution: This puzzle shrine is called Force Transfer, and the idea is to route spinning force through connected parts. In the first chamber, grab the white cylinder off the ground with Ultrahand and wedge it so it meshes between the teeth of the two gears overhead, then hit the orange glowing pillar on your right with a melee swing to spin the gears, which transfers the motion and opens the path. For the optional chest high on a back-right ledge, attach the two cylinders end to end, stick the far end onto the chest, and pull it down off its perch; it contains a Mighty Construct Bow. For the final climb, attach a cylinder horizontally under the white platform on the left wall so a turning gear catches it and lifts the platform, then ride it up (use Ascend if you need to get aboard) as the gear pushes it higher. Climb up and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Tadarok Shrine
shrines.location: Waterfall cave near the River of the Dead, southwest Hyrule Field
shrines.oneLine: Fire and Water: use flame and water elements together (melt ice / douse fire) to clear the path and finish.
shrines.solution: This puzzle shrine is called Fire and Water and mixes both elements with Ultrahand and Recall. To cross the first electrified pool, grab the big stone cube with Ultrahand and drop it in to make a safe stepping stone, then jump across. Next, ice blocks keep falling and melting against wall-mounted flames, so slide a cube in front of the flames to block the heat and preserve an ice block. For the optional chest, use a preserved ice block as a step up to the elevated ledge to open the chest for a Mighty Zonaite Shield. For the final stretch, pull the electrified metal cube out of its pool, then stack the ice cube on top of the stone cube, climb on, and use Recall to reverse their descent so the stack rises; stick the rising stack onto the metal cube, then use Ascend to reach the top platform. Walk to the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Serutabomac Shrine
shrines.location: Floating island behind/northeast of Hyrule Castle (reached from the castle)
shrines.oneLine: The Way Up: build and stack Zonai devices to ascend the vertical chamber and reach the altar at the top.
shrines.solution: This puzzle shrine sits at the back of Hyrule Castle, overlooking the castle moat (not on a separate sky island). Reach it by gliding to the rear of the castle; grabbing it early gives you a fast-travel point right at the castle. Heads-up: Hyrule Castle is steeped in Gloom, so bring gloom-resistant gear or food that restores gloom-darkened hearts. It's called The Way Up, and it's all about climbing the vertical chamber with Ultrahand and Ascend. First, grab a stone slab and lay it across the two posts on the wall, then stand under it and Ascend up through it. Next, attach two slabs into an L, prop it upright on a post, and Ascend through the ledge you made. For the optional chest near the spikes, use Ultrahand and Recall on a slab to make a vertical moving platform and ride it to the chest, which holds a Magic Rod. Finally, join three slabs into a flat platform above the pegs and Ascend up to the exit. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Sepapa Shrine
shrines.location: Small land between Hyrule Castle and Crenel Hills, north-northeast Central Hyrule
shrines.oneLine: Backtrack: use Recall to send moving objects (gears, platforms, debris) back through time and ride them to the exit.
shrines.solution: This puzzle shrine is called Backtrack, and it leans on Recall to send moving objects back the way they came. In the first room, cast Recall on the large moving platform so it travels back through the gap in the wall, then hop on as it returns and ride it up to the upper room. There, light a torch and ride a wooden raft across the water, then on the far side use the flame to burn the dead leaves/vines on the wall, dropping an optional chest that holds a Strong Construct Bow. Recall the raft to ride back, relight your torch on the braziers, and light the two stands by the door to open it. In the final room the puzzle is a ball and two sockets: place the ball in the top dish, wait a moment, then move it to the bottom dish; once it's there, cast Recall on the ball so it retraces its path back up and seats correctly to open the gate. Walk to the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Ren-iz Shrine
shrines.location: Carved tree at Crenel Hills, northern Hyrule Field
shrines.oneLine: Jump the Gaps: time jumps and glides across moving/disappearing platforms to cross the chasms to the altar.
shrines.solution: This puzzle shrine is called Jump the Gaps, and despite the name it's about getting fast-moving balls across chasms with Ultrahand-built ramps, not jumping yourself. In the first room, grab the metal plate near the edge with Ultrahand and set it as a ramp, one end on the white wall and the other on your side of the gap, then hit the pillar to turn it green and release the ball; if your ramp is angled right, the ball arcs across and lands in the net/basket across the gap, which after a moment opens the door. In the next room, combine the plates into a steeper ramp to launch a larger ball into its basket and unlock the final gate. For the optional chest, instead attach the two plates into one long flat plate and bridge the gap to its ledge; it holds a Mighty Zonaite Shield. Cross to the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Tenmaten Shrine
shrines.location: drop in, light the cavern with Brightbloom seeds, grab the chest and blessing.
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing inside Elma Knolls Well in Hyrule Field — drop in, light the cavern with Brightbloom seeds, grab the chest and blessing.
shrines.solution: This is a Rauru's Blessing shrine hidden at the bottom of Elma Knolls Well in Hyrule Field, so the work is getting down to it, not solving a puzzle. Drop into the well and drop again through the lower hole to splash into water in a pitch-black cavern. Light it up: fuse Brightbloom Seeds onto arrows and fire them around to illuminate the cave, or hit the Brightbloom flowers already growing down here (eating a Bright-effect dish so Link glows helps too). Watch out for a couple of Horriblins that drop from the ceiling, and a Fire-Like blocks the way just before the shrine, so deal with them as you cross. Cross the water using Ultrahand-floated slabs (a fan helps) or by freezing the surface with a Sapphire-fused weapon. The shrine itself has no puzzle: open the treasure chest inside for a Large Zonai Charge, then head to the monk's altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Makasura Shrine
shrines.location: West Necluda, on a cliff just west/southwest above Kakariko Village; NE of Sahasra Slope Skyview Tower.
shrines.oneLine: Use Ascend through the rock ceilings and ride the stone platforms up; cross the gaps to reach the exit.
shrines.solution: This is the "An Upright Device" puzzle, built around Zonai Stabilizers (the device that snaps a tipped object upright when you hit or shoot it). Drop in, stand under the first platform, and use Ascend to pop up onto it. Ahead, hit the Stabilizer on the metal structure so it stands up like a ramp at the edge, then climb it and paraglide across. At the tall metal fence, use Ultrahand to set the next Stabilizer-equipped metal piece beside it, hit the Stabilizer to raise it, and climb over. For the optional treasure, attach a Stabilizer to the metal piece with the bowl, stand in the bowl, and activate the Stabilizer (hit or shoot it) to be flung onto a ledge with a chest holding a Fairy Tonic. Next, drop the round Shrine Sphere (orb) into the bowl, aim the Stabilizer end at the fence and activate it to fling the orb across, then drop the orb into the slot to open the gate and grab the metal piece inside. Finally, use Ultrahand to glue the two metal pieces into one long arm, stand in the bowl at the end, and activate the Stabilizer to be launched to the altar. Touch the altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Joju-u-u Shrine
shrines.location: West Necluda, Ubota Point south of Lakeside Stable, above the cliffs by Lake Floria.
shrines.oneLine: Building Bridges: use Ultrahand to drape the hinged bridge over the post and add the stone box as a counterweight.
shrines.solution: This is the "Building Bridges" puzzle, all Ultrahand. Cross the first hanging bridge as-is. At the next gap the loose bridge is too long to lay flat, so grab it with Ultrahand and stick its far end a few planks onto the next platform; walk out and jump across at the sagging middle. At the following section, grab the large stone block nearby and glue it to one end of the bridge, then hang that weighted end over the metal stake jutting from the wall so the block acts as a counterweight and holds the bridge taut while you climb up and cross. For the optional treasure, stand on the ledge and use Ultrahand to lift the middle of the hanging bridge and connect it across to the lone bridge piece on the side, making a path to a chest containing Large Zonaite. Cross the final span and touch the altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Jochisiu Shrine
shrines.location: West Necluda, west side of South Dueling Peaks by Squabble River.
shrines.oneLine: Keys Born of Water quest: use the fire and ice emitters to melt/freeze ice and clear the path, then enter.
shrines.solution: This shrine stays buried until you finish the shrine quest "Keys Born of Water," so do that first. Go to the marked spot on the west side of South Dueling Peaks by the Squabble River and talk to the Steward Construct, who gives the riddle that ice is the key. You need ice "keys" for three stone altars near the water: the easiest way is to fire Ice-Fruit-fused arrows (or attach a Frost Emitter to a weapon/shield) at the river to freeze diamond-shaped ice blocks, then carry each one over with Ultrahand without getting too close to any fire, which would melt it. Slot one ice key into each of the three altars. Once all three are seated the quest completes, a green crystal rises, and it becomes the shrine entrance, so walk in to activate it. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle, so the Light of Blessing is essentially free. Open the chest, which holds a Big Battery, then touch the altar at the back to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Eshos Shrine
shrines.location: West Necluda, on the eastern cliff of Dueling Peaks.
shrines.oneLine: Combat Training: Shields. Parry the Training Constructs' attacks; use a non-metallic shield against the electric one.
shrines.solution: This is the "Combat Training: Shields" shrine, which teaches you to parry. If you need gear, several shields are provided near the base of the stairs, including a wooden shield and a metal one, so grab both. Approach the center and the first Training Construct appears, lobbing fireballs. Raise your shield with ZL, then press A right as the fireball hits to deflect it straight back and destroy the construct. In the second phase a construct on the right adds electric (shock) projectiles while the other keeps throwing fire. The key trick: never face the shock attack with a metal shield, it will zap you, so switch to a wooden (non-metallic) shield to parry the lightning, and use a metal shield for the fire. Time each parry to send the projectiles back and clear both constructs. Open the treasure chest for a Mighty Zonaite Shield, then walk to the altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Susub Shrine
shrines.location: West Necluda, inside Deya Village Ruins East Well, south of Deya Village Ruins.
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: no puzzle. Drop into the well and reach the shrine; grab the chest and the Light of Blessing.
shrines.solution: This is a Rauru's Blessing shrine, so there's no puzzle, but the catch is getting to it: it sits at the bottom of the Deya Village Ruins East Well, south of the ruins. From the Popla Foothills Skyview Tower, glide northeast to the ruins; the well mouth is sealed, so blast it open with a Bomb-Flower-fused arrow (or anything that smashes rock) and drop in. The walls down here drip water and are slippery, so a Sticky Elixir or Frog armor helps if you keep sliding, and there's a Stone Talus near the bottom platform you can simply avoid. Make your way over to the shrine entrance. There's no challenge inside: open the treasure chest for a Magic Staff, then touch the altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Utojis Shrine
shrines.location: West Necluda, inside Tobio's Hollow Cave.
shrines.oneLine: Legend of the Soaring Spear: fuse a Keese Wing to a spear, stand on the glowing pedestal and throw it through the ring.
shrines.solution: This shrine is hidden until you finish the shrine quest "Legend of the Soaring Spear," inside Tobio's Hollow Cave in East Necluda. Work through the cave tunnel, dealing with the Keese hanging from the ceiling, and be sure to grab a Keese Wing they drop since you'll need one. At the end is an abandoned campsite with an old notebook; read it to start the quest, and note the scattered Zonaite Spears. Pick up a Zonaite Spear and use Fuse to attach a Keese Wing to it, which makes a spear that flies far. Then stand on the circular pedestal and throw the fused spear so it sails through the ring of green energy ahead. Land the throw and a green crystal rises and becomes the shrine entrance. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle, so the Light of Blessing is essentially free: open the chest for a Large Zonai Charge, then touch the altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Tokiy Shrine
shrines.location: East Necluda, inside Oakle's Navel Cave (north of Rabella Wetlands Skyview Tower).
shrines.oneLine: The Oakle's Navel Cave Crystal: carry the crystal past falling boulders (Recall the big one) to its stand to reveal it.
shrines.solution: This shrine is hidden behind the shrine quest "The Oakle's Navel Cave Crystal," inside Oakle's Navel Cave in East Necluda (north of the Rabella Wetlands Skyview Tower). Head into the cave (clearing the Like enemy on the way) until you find a glowing green crystal on a pedestal; examining it starts the quest. Pick the crystal up with Ultrahand and follow the beam of green light it shines, which marks the way through the cave. The path runs through a boulder run, so time your steps between the rolling rocks. When a boulder comes straight down the path at you, use Recall to send it rolling back the way it came and clear your route. Keep following the beam to its end and set the crystal down where the light points to reveal the shrine entrance. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle, so the Light of Blessing is essentially free: open the lone chest for a Large Zonai Charge, then touch the altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Zanmik Shrine
shrines.location: East Necluda, on a hill southwest of Hateno Village.
shrines.oneLine: Scoop it Out: Ultrahand four plates into a box, attach it to the wheel as a scoop, then plate the nodes to power it.
shrines.solution: This "Scoop it Out" puzzle is all Ultrahand. First get the big wheel turning: pick up one of the loose metal plates from the lower floor and lay it across the gap in the yellow electric circuit so the current connects and the wheel starts spinning. Now build a scoop. Attach a metal plate to the rim of the spinning wheel so it dips into the pit of balls as it turns and carries one up. When a ball reaches the top, grab it with Ultrahand and drop it into the empty slot (the goal) to score your "hole in one." Tip: if you clear out enough balls from the pit while you work, you'll uncover an optional chest on the left side holding a Strong Zonaite Longsword. Once the ball is in the slot the gate opens, so head to the back and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Mayahisik Shrine
shrines.location: East Necluda, inside Retsam Forest Cave near the Hateno Ancient Tech Lab.
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: no puzzle. Enter the cave, break the rocks and follow the path to the shrine for the Light of Blessing.
shrines.solution: There's no puzzle here at all, this is a Rauru's Blessing shrine, so the Light of Blessing is basically free once you reach it. The catch is getting in: the shrine sits inside Retsam Forest Cave near the Hateno Ancient Tech Lab, and the cave mouth is sealed by a pile of breakable rocks. Smash them open (a Bomb Flower, a hammer-type weapon, or fire all work), head inside, and follow the passage down until you spot the shrine. Inside, open the treasure chest near the altar for a Magic Scepter (a stronger version of the Magic Rod), then walk to the monk's altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Anedamimik Shrine
shrines.location: East Necluda, inside Deepback Bay Cave, east of Hateno Village.
shrines.oneLine: A Retraced Path: ride the moving platform across, then use Recall on the rolling ball to send it into the receptacle.
shrines.solution: This "A Retraced Path" shrine sits in Deepback Bay Cave east of Hateno Village, and its entrance is underwater at first, so you must drain it. Drop through one of the holes beside the shrine to a lower ledge (you'll meet a Like Like here, which drops a chest), then aim a Bomb Arrow at the cracked rock wall; breaking it lets the water drain out around the shrine. Use Ascend to get back up, then enter. Inside, hit the orange switch to start the platform structure moving, drop beneath the ledge holding the treasure chest and Ascend up to open it for a Large Zonai Charge. Now the trick: hit the switch again and watch the ball roll toward the gate; just before it arrives, use Recall on the ball and hit the switch once more, sending the ball traveling backward into the orange receptacle. That opens the gate, so step through and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Bamitok Shrine
shrines.location: East Necluda, inside Mount Dunsel Cave near Lurelin Village.
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: no puzzle. Swim across, lift the plank and Ascend up to reach the shrine and Light of Blessing.
shrines.solution: No puzzle inside, this is a Rauru's Blessing shrine, so the Light of Blessing is free once you fight through the cave to reach it. The shrine is inside Mount Dunsel Cave near Lurelin Village; the entrance is surrounded by water, so swim across to get in. Inside you'll meet a Horriblin and a pair of Shock Likes along the route, plus a Rock/Stone Like blocking the way (Bomb Arrows make short work of it). Two optional chests sit along the path: the first chest, soon after the entrance, holds a Royal Bow, and a chest up on an elevated ledge holds a Royal Shield. To reach that ledge, grab the nearby wooden board with Ultrahand, set it under the overhang, then climb on and use Ascend to rise up through the ledge. Push on to the shrine, open the chest inside for a Big Battery, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Sifumim Shrine
shrines.location: East Necluda, on a hill just northwest of Lurelin Village.
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Flow. Weapons removed. Knock the Soldier Constructs off the rotating platforms into the water.
shrines.solution: This is a "Proving Grounds: Flow" combat trial, so all your gear is taken away at the entrance, leaving only a few basic weapons on a stand to the left to grab. The whole arena floats over water and the Soldier Constructs can't swim, so water is your weapon: knock them in and they die in seconds. Hide behind the crates, and when a Construct stands on its wooden tower platform near you, use Ultrahand to grab the platform and shake it so the Construct tumbles into the water. Clear the outer ring this way, then collect the bows, arrows, and Ice Fruits they drop. Move up to the inner ring and repeat the platform-shake trick. For the tougher final Construct, fuse an Ice Fruit to an arrow and shoot to freeze it, then rush in and hit it with a stick (fuse a spiked metal ball or rock for real damage), knocking it off into the water. Grab the chest (a Captain II weapon) and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Marari-In Shrine
shrines.location: Necluda Sea, on Eventide Island via the sea cave entrance (far southeast).
shrines.oneLine: Solve the puzzle inside the Eventide cave to reveal it; reach it by raft/boat or paraglide from the Hateno coast.
shrines.solution: First you have to reach Eventide Island, off the far southeast coast in the Necluda Sea, easiest by air (a flying machine or paraglide from the Hateno coast) or by building a boat. The shrine stays locked behind a gate until you finish its side quest, "Seeking the Pirate Hideout." Land at Toronbo Beach, talk to a man named Sesami standing by a red tent to start it, then clear out the monsters holding the island's three strongholds. After that, head to the easternmost part of the island where the tallest stronghold stood and drop down the cliff there to find a cave entrance; inside, defeat the Bokoblins guarding the pirate ship. The shrine sits across from the ship but stone spikes line the gap, so use Ultrahand to pull the loose wooden planks from the ship's stern and lay them across as a makeshift bridge. It's a Rauru's Blessing shrine, so there's no puzzle inside; open the treasure chest for a Ruby, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Tukarok Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Wetlands, just south of Wetland Stable
shrines.oneLine: Forward Force. Stick the ball to a wheeled cart and ride it across lava, then build a paddle raft to ferry the ball to the altar.
shrines.solution: This is a three-stage "Forward Force" puzzle where you ferry a glowing ball across three hazards using Zonai wheels. First, drop down to the ball and use Ultrahand to attach it to the four-wheeled cart, hop on, hit a wheel with a weapon to start it, and ride across the lava. Second, stick the ball onto the stone slab that sits on the rail to your right, Ultrahand a big Zonai wheel onto that slab's side with its arrows pointing into the slope, add a tall slab from the wall onto the wheel's open face, then shoot or hit the wheel so the whole rig climbs the incline. Third, attach the ball to the top of a wheeled raft and stick the long wooden boards onto the wheels on each side so they paddle; set it in the water with the arrows facing forward and ride it across (the sunken chest in this room holds a Strong Zonaite Sword). At the end, step on the glowing floor switch to open the gate, then place the ball into its glowing vessel. Walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Morok Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Wetlands, atop a floating island near Sahasra Slope, northwest of Sahasra Slope Skyview Tower
shrines.oneLine: A Bouncy Device. Stack and fuse the Zonai springs, then activate them to launch yourself up to the chest and the altar.
shrines.solution: This "A Bouncy Device" shrine is about stacking Zonai springs to bounce ever higher. From the entry, step onto the launchpad ahead to be flung up, then glide to the next level. There, stand on the single spring and hit it with a weapon to fire yourself upward; a ball needs to roll into a floor hole to open a gate (nudge it with Ultrahand if it stalls). Move the two-spring stack into position, stand on it, and strike it to bounce up to a ledge with a treasure chest holding a Sneaky Elixir. Then drop back down, reset the springs, and use Ultrahand to glue the single spring onto the top of the two-spring stack to make a taller three-spring tower. Stand on it and strike it to launch up to the shrine's top level. Walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Jonsau Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Wetlands, on the southern hills of Mercay Island near the center of the Wetlands
shrines.oneLine: Deep Force. Ultrahand the ball under the target and push it deep underwater so it rockets up and strikes the switch.
shrines.solution: This "Deep Force" shrine relies on dense water shooting a light object skyward. For the first puzzle, use Ultrahand to grab the yellow ball floating on the water, hold it directly under the target-shaped device overhead, push it down into the water, and let go; the ball rockets up, sticks to the upside-down platform, and opens the door. Before moving on, look into the water for a sunken treasure chest, drag it up with Ultrahand and open it for a Strong Construct Bow. The second puzzle is the same trick: hold the ball (or a yellow plank) under the target and release it from underwater to open the next door. For the final puzzle, place the ball at the front center of the slim pillar, push it underwater and let go so it knocks loose a wooden board above; a basket then drops onto the water. Stand in the basket and launch it one last time with the ball to reach the altar. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Maoikes Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Wetlands, inside Bone Pond Cave (drop in through the skull on the hilltop)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. No puzzle inside; just reach it through the cave, grab the chest, and touch the altar.
shrines.solution: Maoikes is a Rauru's Blessing shrine, so there is no puzzle once you get inside. The work is reaching it: it sits inside Bone Pond East Cave. Head to the huge skull resting on top of the hill and drop in through the skull's mouth, which lets you glide straight down to the shrine and skip the monsters lurking in the cave's other passages. Inside, the Light of Blessing is essentially free. Grab the treasure chest in the middle of the chamber for a Diamond, then walk to the monk's altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Mogawak Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring, below the walkways of Zora's Domain beneath the Great Zora Bridge
shrines.oneLine: The Power of Water. Charge the battery with the waterfall's hydro power to run the elevator up to the altar.
shrines.solution: This "The Power of Water" shrine runs on a battery you charge from the waterfall. Head down and use Ultrahand to attach the square panel from the water onto the device by the waterfall to make a water wheel; the falling water spins it and the circuit lights up. Grab the energy cell from the middle of the room and set it on the charging pad beside the wheel to charge it. For the optional chests: pull the sunken chest from the water near the start for a Magic Scepter, and to open the locked side gate carry the charged cell to its left-hand pad, then Ultrahand the two big metal balls in the water together so the current bridges them — that gate's chest holds an Opal. Once the cell is charged, step into the lift basket first, then place the charged cell on its port so it powers the lift and carries you up to the altar. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Ihen-a Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring, Mipha Court atop Ploymus Mountain, east of Zora's Domain
shrines.oneLine: Midair Perch. Splash-arrow the gooped entrance, then Ascend up a Hover Stone and lay the bridge as a ramp to cross the gaps.
shrines.solution: Before completing the Sidon of the Zora quest the shrine entrance is buried in sludge, so fuse a Splash Fruit to an arrow and shoot the doorway to wash it clean (Chuchu Jelly or a hydrant work too). This "Midair Perch" shrine has you bridging gaps with floating Hover Stones. Hit the hover stones to switch them on so they hang in place, then stack them into a staircase up to the first ledge. At the gap, attach a metal panel onto a floating stone to form a bridge across; at the next gap, carry that bridge over and tilt it into an angled ramp to walk up. Stack more hover stones to climb to a ledge with a treasure chest holding 5 Arrows. For the final gap, set the small ball on a hover stone near the ram (don't fuse it), climb on, then hit the orange crystal so the ram shoves you and the ball across. Pull the ball off, drop it into the floor socket to open the gate, then walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Apogek Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring, southeast of East Reservoir Lake near Zora's Domain
shrines.oneLine: Wings on the Wind. Fuse a fan to a Zonai wing, set it on the rail, then ride it across the room to the altar.
shrines.solution: This "Wings on the Wind" shrine has you riding Zonai wings on rails. In the first room, use Ultrahand to pluck a wing as it glides past, stick the glowing ball to it, set it on the rail, and ride it down the slope to the gate; pull the ball off and drop it into the dish to open the door. Next is a fan section: paraglide in the updraft off the Zonai fans to reach the upper platform. For the optional chest, take one of the wing devices, Ultrahand it up beside the chest's platform, bring it back down, climb on, and use Recall to ride it back up to a Strong Zonaite Spear. For the final stretch, look over the ledge and grab two fans, fuse one to each side of a wing, set it on the rail (stand in front so it doesn't leave without you), hit a fan to start it, and ride it across to the altar. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Yomizuk Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring, inside Tarm Point Cave, north of Tarm Point
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. No puzzle; find it through the cave, open the chest, and touch the altar for the Light of Blessing.
shrines.solution: This shrine is visible on the map but sits at the far end of Tarm Point Cave, north of Tarm Point. From the Mount Lanayru Skyview Tower, glide northeast toward the column ruins and drop through the hole in the ground to enter the cave. Inside is a flooded passage that periodically rises and drains; you must cross the water to reach the far end. The simplest way is to swim, resting on the large rocks between strokes when your stamina runs low (a stamina dish or Zora Armor helps); if you'd rather not swim, fuse a Sapphire to a weapon and strike the water to freeze stepping stones. Once across, the shrine itself is a Rauru's Blessing with no puzzle. Open the treasure chest for a Diamond (a gem worth a lot of rupees), then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Joniu Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring, inside Ralis Channel, northwest of Veiled Falls
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing, unlocked via a shrine quest. Toss a Brightbloom Seed to light the dark channel and follow the right path in.
shrines.solution: This shrine stays hidden until you finish the shrine quest "The Ralis Channel Crystal," so do that first. From the Upland Zorana Skyview Tower, glide southeast to the entrance of Ralis Channel; drop in and head down to the water below, where you'll find Zonai parts and a glowing green crystal. Examine the green crystal to start the quest, then build a small boat: lay a wooden plank in the water, attach a Zonai Steering Stick and a Zonai Fan, and glue the green crystal anywhere on it. Hit the fan and steer down the winding channel (a steering-stick boat handles the turns better than a kite) until you reach the shrine's lit entrance. Pull the crystal off with Ultrahand and set it on the shrine platform to make Joniu Shrine rise. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing with no puzzle. Open the chest for a Large Zonai Charge, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Kurakat Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring, northwest of Quatta's Shelf
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing behind a dyeing riddle: read the clue, dye your armor the right color at Hateno, then claim the chest and altar.
shrines.solution: This shrine is hidden behind the shrine quest "Dyeing to Find It," northwest of Quatta's Shelf, and despite the name it's a shadow puzzle, not an armor-dyeing one. A Steward Construct gives the riddle: dye the white pattern black when the sun awakens. You must make a stone structure cast its shadow exactly over a white pattern on the cliff at dawn. Use the nearby axle/crank to rotate the structure into position, then grab the loose wooden board with Ultrahand and stick it onto the left side so the combined shape will match the white pattern. When it lines up, light the nearby campfire (a thrown or fired Fire Fruit works) and rest there until morning. Around 6 a.m. the rising sun throws the structure's shadow over the pattern and the shrine appears. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing with no puzzle. Open the chest for a Magic Scepter, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: O-ogim Shrine
shrines.location: Lanayru Great Spring, south of Lanayru Heights below the broken Lanayru Bridge
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing reward for a shrine quest; deliver the crystal to make the shrine appear, then take the chest and altar.
shrines.solution: This shrine stays buried until you finish the shrine quest "The Lanayru Road Crystal," south of Lanayru Heights below the broken Lanayru Bridge. Activate the shrine entrance to fire a green beam, then follow that beam into Lanayru Road South Cave. Inside, a rock wall blocks the green crystal, break it with a Bomb Flower or a boulder-fused weapon, then grab the green crystal with Ultrahand and carry it back outside to the open runway. Build a quick flyer from a Zonai Wing with a Zonai Fan attached, glue the crystal on, and ride it back to the shrine; pull the crystal off and set it on the entrance to make O-ogim Shrine appear. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing with no puzzle. Open the chest for a Big Battery, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Jikais Shrine
shrines.location: Mount Lanayru, east of the Lanayru Range beyond Madorna Mountain
shrines.oneLine: Jailbreak. Use Ultrahand to stack and move blocks, then Ascend up through them to reach the altar.
shrines.solution: This is the "Jailbreak" puzzle, east of the Lanayru Range. In the first room, slide the blocks on rails with Ultrahand so you can Ascend up instead of climb: on the right side push the bottom block right and the top block left; on the left side slide the top block right, under the torch alcove. Ascend up the right side, paraglide left, Ascend through the upper-left block, jump into the alcove, and Ascend once more into the next room. There, drag the long stone slab so it balances over the right edge with part overhanging, stand under the overhang, and Ascend up to advance, and look for a metal platform overhead with the chest, Ascend straight up to it for a Magic Staff. The final room locks you in a cell with non-climbable bars, the slab you need is OUTSIDE the bars, so grab it with Ultrahand and pull it inside, then climb it (or Recall it) to get out. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Zakusu Shrine
shrines.location: Mount Lanayru, Naydra Snowfield, southwest of Mount Lanayru Skyview Tower
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Ascension. Stripped of gear, use only the shrine's weapons and Ascend to beat the foes; gated behind a shrine quest.
shrines.shrineQuest: The High Spring and the Light Rings
shrines.solution: This shrine is hidden behind the shrine quest "The High Spring and the Light Rings," given by Nazbi, who camps outside the Spring of Wisdom up on Mount Lanayru. Climb to the sky island terminal above the spring and activate it, then dive to the slope and shield surf down the mountain (equip a shield, jump, and press A to drop onto it) and ride straight through all 7 glowing light rings before the timer runs out. Clear them in time and Zakusu Shrine appears, southwest of the Mount Lanayru Skyview Tower. Inside it's Proving Grounds: Ascension, a combat trial that strips ALL your gear, so use only the weapons provided at the entrance (Fuse rockets or monster parts on for more punch). Use Ascend and the Zonai balloon/candle to reach the upper levels and defeat every Construct. Clearing them returns your gear and opens a chest holding a Soldier III Spear; then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Jogou Shrine
shrines.location: Mount Lanayru, inside Lanayru Road East Cave on the northwest slopes (below the Lanayru Road East Gate)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. No puzzle inside; just find it within the cave and touch the altar for the Light of Blessing.
shrines.solution: This shrine is visible on the map but tucked inside Lanayru Road East Cave on the northwest slopes, below the Lanayru Road - East Gate. From the Mount Lanayru Skyview Tower, head west to the East Gate, then go under the ruins below the gate to find the cave entrance. Inside, swim across the water to a shore guarded by two Horriblins and a Rock Like, then push deeper to an underwater lake with a central landmass where a Boss Bokoblin leads a pack, fight or simply slip past them. The way to the shrine is sealed behind a cracked rock wall: break it with a Bomb Flower (or a boulder-fused weapon) to open the path, then reach the shrine and activate it. The shrine itself is a Rauru's Blessing with no puzzle. Open the treasure chest for a Hearty Elixir, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: En-oma Shrine
shrines.location: Lake Hylia Whirlpool Cave, beneath Lake Hylia (approx 0105, -2514, 0088); enter by letting the visible whirlpool suck Link down
shrines.oneLine: No trial inside; reward for The Lake Hylia Crystal quest. Grab the crystal on the sky island above the whirlpool and drop it into the hole below.
shrines.solution: This shrine stays sealed until you finish its shrine quest, The Lake Hylia Crystal, so do that first. The shrine sits in a cave under Lake Hylia, reached by letting the big whirlpool in the lake suck you down. The crystal that opens it is high above, on a small floating island in the Faron Sky Archipelago directly over the whirlpool. The easiest route is to fast-travel to Popla Foothills Skyview Tower, launch up, and glide southwest toward the whirlpool, then up to that island; the crystal sits on a pedestal inside a small square structure. Grab it with Ultrahand and drop it straight down through the square hole nearby; it falls toward the whirlpool below. Then jump in after it and dive into the whirlpool yourself, get drawn down into the cave, and set the crystal into the shrine entrance to raise it. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle, so the Light of Blessing is essentially free. Open the chest right in front of you for a Mighty Zonaite Sword, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Ishokin Shrine
shrines.location: Faron Grasslands near Zokassa Ridge, east of Oseira Plains (approx -0565, -3524, 0129)
shrines.oneLine: No trial inside; appears after Ride the Giant Horse. Tame the Giant White Stallion in the nearby grove and show it to Baddek for the crystal.
shrines.solution: This shrine only appears once you finish its shrine quest, Ride the Giant Horse, so start there. Near Zokassa Ridge you'll meet Baddek, who guards a shrine crystal and won't hand it over until you bring him a fabled giant horse. Head to Highland Stable, ask around for clues, then cross the Horse God Bridge over the Lake of the Horse God to find the huge Giant White Stallion grazing in a glade beyond. It's a tough tame: you'll want nearly three full wheels of stamina, so eat stamina-restoring food (or sleep on the Malanya bed at Highland Stable for an extra ring); sneak up, mount it, and mash to soothe it, pausing to refill stamina as needed. Ride it back to Baddek and speak to him while still mounted, and he gives up the crystal regardless of whether you keep the horse. Pick it up, follow the green beam it shines, and carry it down to set it into the shrine. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle, so the Light of Blessing is essentially free. Open the chest for a Topaz, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Jiukoum Shrine
shrines.location: Faron Grasslands, south side of Popla Foothills overlooking Dracozu Lake, SE of Popla Foothills Skyview Tower (approx 0867, -2279, 0141)
shrines.oneLine: Built for Rails: use Ultrahand to join the boards into a stable platform, set it on the rails, and ride it across each gap to the altar.
shrines.solution: This is a Built for Rails shrine, so you ride your own creations along the tracks. In the first room, use Ultrahand to attach the two boards end to end into one long platform, lay it lengthways across the two rails, climb on, and ride it to the far side. For the optional chest, grab more boards and build a ramp across to the alcove on your left to reach the treasure chest, which holds a Sticky Elixir. Back on the rails, build the next platform into a box shape (a square with one side open), set it on the rails with the open side down, then close it off so you can climb inside and ride it deeper in. At the final stretch, arrange four panels into a capital E, set it on the rails so the prongs sit between the rail pairs, and attach the nearby fans so they face opposite your direction of travel. Climb on and turn the fans on (a melee hit on the rear fan switches it on) to ride the contraption all the way to the altar. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Utsushok Shrine
shrines.location: Faron Grasslands, northeast of Highland Stable, east of Fural Plain atop a hill (approx 0668, -3358, 0072)
shrines.oneLine: Long or Wide: with Ultrahand add length or weight to the paddle to knock balls into switches, then ride the spawned cart along the rails.
shrines.solution: This is a Long or Wide shrine built around a swinging paddle that knocks a big ball along a rail. First, grab the paddle with Ultrahand, pull it back as high as you can, and release to bat the ball down the rail into its socket. For the next ball the paddle needs more punch, so use Ultrahand to stick the large metal cube onto the back of the paddle, then swing again to drive the ball home and open the door. In the final room you need the paddle to both reach the ball and hit hard: attach the metal panel to the bottom edge of the paddle to extend its reach, then add another panel to the side facing the rail cart. Swing it to smack the orb, which spawns a rail cart. Climb into the cart and swing the paddle at it to launch yourself along the track. As you ride, watch the wall near the rail for a treasure chest; snatch it with Ultrahand on the way past (Recall on the cart can slow you down to aim) and open it for a Sneaky Elixir. Ride to the end and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Ekochiu Shrine
shrines.location: Rise and Fall: ride rising/falling platforms and time Ultrahand to cross. North
shrines.oneLine: Rise and Fall: ride rising/falling platforms and time Ultrahand to cross. North of Woodland Stable, Great Hyrule Forest.
shrines.solution: This is a Rise and Fall shrine all about timing Recall on moving blocks. In the first room, step on the glowing floor tile to send the white block gliding to the far side, then wait for it to slide back toward you. The moment it returns, hop on top of it and use Recall; the block rewinds its path and carries you across to the next ledge. For the optional chest, use the block to reach the raised section on the right (paraglide over), where the chest holds a Zonaite Shield (it lowers the energy any attached Zonai device uses). In the final room, use Ultrahand to set the loose block onto the platform in the center, lying across the pressure plate, then step on the pressure plate to fling the block high into the air. As soon as it's airborne, jump onto it and use Recall, which lifts the block (and you) back up to its peak; at the top, leap off and glide over to the monk's altar. Touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Kikakin Shrine
shrines.location: Shining in Darkness: cross a pitch-black shrine using light orbs / Brightbloom s
shrines.oneLine: Shining in Darkness: cross a pitch-black shrine using light orbs / Brightbloom seeds. NE of Mount Drena, west Great Hyrule Forest.
shrines.solution: This is a Shining in Darkness shrine: the whole place is pitch black, so first pick up the portable lantern at the entrance and carry it in your hand as you go (throwing your own Brightbloom Seeds works too if you have them). From the locked altar gate, head into the left hallway and turn right, then use Ultrahand to pull a square block out of the wall to reveal a side chest holding Amber. Use Ascend on a ledge along the left wall and follow it to a bright room with a chest containing a Zonaite Bow. Drop back down and push through the central spike-lined corridor to a chest holding a Luminous Stone, and there's a laser hallway with a chest holding an Opal if you want it. At the end of the hallway, use Ultrahand to lift a block out of the floor; the chest underneath holds the Small Key. Bring the key back to the locked door by the altar, walk in to the monk's altar, and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Kiuyoyou Shrine
shrines.location: Fire and Ice: melt and freeze ice blocks with flame and Zonai devices to align p
shrines.oneLine: Fire and Ice: melt and freeze ice blocks with flame and Zonai devices to align platforms. Rowan Plain, east of the Forgotten Temple.
shrines.solution: This is a Fire and Ice shrine where you melt ice to size and then shield it from flames. In the first room, grab the big ice block with Ultrahand and hold it briefly under the flame jet to shrink it down, taking it out before it melts away, then set the trimmed block onto the switch to open the bars into the main chamber. For the optional chest, ride the wind current forward to grab a stone slab, stick it on top of an ice block, slide that down the spiked slope, shake off the slab, and pop the ice onto the ledge below the chest; get under the ledge, use Ascend to come up beside the ice, then stand on it and Recall it (or use it as a step) to reach the chest, which holds a Zonaite Spear. For the final puzzle the trick is to keep ice from melting: put the stone slab back on top of an ice block so it shields it, then set that ice-and-stone piece onto the switch under the downward flame. The slab keeps the ice from melting and its weight holds the switch, opening the door; walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Musanokir Shrine
shrines.location: Swing to Hit: build a weighted pendulum with Ultrahand to smash targets and ball
shrines.oneLine: Swing to Hit: build a weighted pendulum with Ultrahand to smash targets and balls into goals. Within Korok Forest.
shrines.solution: This is a Swing to Hit shrine that has you build weighted pendulums with Ultrahand. In the first room, grab the metal ball hanging under the white platform and attach it to the large metal cube to swing the platform down into place as a bridge so you can cross. In the next room, detach the cube and stick it onto the ball on the chain by the big orange target to make a heavy pendulum, then pull the cube back and let it swing into the target to open the door. For the optional chest, stand the wooden log upright on the platform beside the chest, climb up, and glide across to the treasure chest, which holds a Large Zonai Charge. For the final puzzle, attach the pole to the top of the stone block so it points horizontally toward the far switch like a battering ram, then attach the cube to the bottom of the hanging ball. Pull the cube back as far as it goes and release so its momentum drives the block forward and rams the pole into the switch, opening the last door. Walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Ninjis Shrine
shrines.location: appears after the 'Maca's Special Place' quest (post-Phantom Ganon). South edge
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing — appears after the 'Maca's Special Place' quest (post-Phantom Ganon). South edge of Korok Forest; collect the blessing.
shrines.solution: This shrine stays hidden until you finish its shrine quest, Maca's Special Place, which only becomes available after you've restored Korok Forest by curing the Great Deku Tree (the storyline that involves beating Phantom Ganon beneath the forest). Then go to the southern part of Korok Forest and talk to a little Korok named Maca; he marks his special place on your map, out in the foggy depths-like wilds you can't simply walk to. Travel to the Thyphlo Ruins Skyview Tower and launch up, then glide toward Maca's pin, touching down on floating islands along the way to top off your stamina. As you near the marker you'll spot a green swirl around the shrine entrance below; dive and land beside it. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle, so the Light of Blessing is essentially free. Open the chest for a Mighty Construct Bow, then walk to the monk's altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Pupunke Shrine
shrines.location: Quest-gated blessing: finish 'A Pretty Stone and Five Golden Apples' (give a lum
shrines.oneLine: Quest-gated blessing: finish 'A Pretty Stone and Five Golden Apples' (give a luminous stone + 5 golden apples), then walk in.
shrines.shrineQuest: A Pretty Stone and Five Golden Apples
shrines.solution: This shrine won't appear until you finish its shrine quest, A Pretty Stone and Five Golden Apples, so do that first. Near the eastern exit of Korok Forest, speak to a Korok named Damia, who offers to trade his pretty green stone (a shrine crystal) for five Golden Apples. Golden Apples grow on trees you can cut down in the Mido Swamp area to the north (trees near Sonapan Shrine work too); follow the path carefully, because touching the fog there warps you back to where you started, and watch for Evermeans and Stalkoblins. Gather at least five and bring them to Damia, and he hands over the crystal. Carry it and follow its green beam of light to the shrine entrance, then place it to raise the shrine. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle, so the Light of Blessing is essentially free. Open the chest for a Mighty Zonaite Sword, then walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Sakunbomar Shrine
shrines.location: Quest-gated blessing: complete 'None Shall Pass' in Great Hyrule Forest, then fo
shrines.oneLine: Quest-gated blessing: complete 'None Shall Pass' in Great Hyrule Forest, then follow the light beam to the shrine.
shrines.solution: This shrine stays buried until you finish its shrine quest, None Shall Pass?, so start there. On the west side of Korok Forest, talk to a Korok named Zooki, then follow the trail he points out, staying on the path marked by Blue Nightshade flowers; Evermeans, Stalkoblins, and Stalmoblins will ambush you, so fight through them. At the shrine site, interact to fire off a green beam of light, then follow it to the crystal, which is stuck to a suspicious buried boulder that wakes up as a Stone Talus, with the green crystal sitting where its weak point would be. Use Ascend from underneath to pop up onto its top surface, then hack at the green crystal until the Talus falls (you can also shoot the crystal with arrows). Carry the freed crystal back and set it at the shrine entrance to raise the shrine. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle, so the Light of Blessing is essentially free. Open the chest for a Diamond, then walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Kudanisar Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Desert, west of Gerudo Town near the Statue of the Eighth Heroine
shrines.oneLine: Bridging the Sands: use Ultrahand to build wooden bridges and a fan-cart to cross the stamina-draining quicksand to the altar.
shrines.solution: This one is all Ultrahand and quicksand, so don't try to walk on the sand. At the first gap, grab the loose wooden board and lay it across to make a bridge, then walk over; if a board won't reach, glue a second one onto it (held pieces turn yellow when they'll stick). Keep building plank bridges and ramps across the flowing sand, dealing with a Soldier Construct on the way. Partway through you reach Zonai parts: attach a Fan to a board to make a little motorboat and ride it across the sand to the platforms. Step on the trigger to launch a stone sphere, then use Ultrahand to stick that sphere onto your fan-cart, drive it back, and drop it into the bowl-shaped socket to open the final gate. Two optional chests are worth grabbing: shoot the rope holding the hanging chest, grab it with Ultrahand for Arrow x10, and stack two boards into a ramp to climb to a higher ledge for a Hasty Elixir. Then step onto the altar at the end and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Mayatat Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Desert, southwest of Gerudo Desert Gateway near Kara Kara Bazaar
shrines.oneLine: A Sliding Device: hop onto one of the descending sleds, then use Recall to ride it back up across the quicksand to the exit.
shrines.solution: The trick here is to stop building and start riding. At the entrance, wait for one of the sleds to slide down the rail from the right; hop onto it and immediately use Recall to send it back up, carrying you to the upper level. In the next area you make your own sand-boat: use Ultrahand to set a Steering Stick on top of a sled, then grab a Fan from nearby and stick it on the back so it can push you. Hop on, smack the fan to start it, and steer across the quicksand. Before you finish, drive to the back-left platform and climb the steps to open the chest there for Arrow x10. Then steer your fan-sled to the altar on the back-right side, step off, and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Soryotanog Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Desert, high on the cliffs directly above Gerudo Town
shrines.oneLine: Buried Light: turn on a fan with Ultrahand to blow away the sand, then guide the light beam to the crystal to open the way.
shrines.solution: In the first room, use Ultrahand to pull the Zonai Fan out of the sand pile and turn it on (hit it once), then aim its wind at the heaps of sand to blow them away — this uncovers a chest holding a Small Key. Use that key on the locked door. In the next room, blow away the sand in the right corner to reveal a small hole and crawl through. Clear more sand to find a fan, set it upright against the wall so its updraft lifts you to the upper ledge, then defeat the Construct up there. For the optional chest, use the Fan Guster you picked up to blow the sand off the big gear — that uncovers a chest holding Arrow x10. The finale is a light puzzle: place the Zonai Mirror on the source of the big light beam and angle it so it shines back into the previous room, then use Ultrahand on the second mirror there to redirect that beam onto the hexagonal receptor above the door to open it. Then step inside and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Siwakama Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Desert, north of the East Barrens
shrines.oneLine: Moving the Spheres: Ultrahand the big balls to bridge the gaps, then use Recall to ride a returning sphere across to the exit.
shrines.solution: This shrine teaches Ultrahand and Recall with big stone spheres. First, use Ultrahand to grab a sphere and drop it into the gap below you, then walk across it to the next platform. For the optional chest, grab a sphere, set it at the top of the ramp and let it roll down to smash the breakable rock wall, then open the chest behind it for Bomb Flower x5. Now combine spheres: stick two together side by side and lay them across the next gap as a bridge to walk over; further on, fuse three spheres into a rough triangle and set them on the pointed pillar to make a crossing toward the exit. The classic stuck-point is the moving sphere — use Ultrahand to hold a sphere up on the ramp for several seconds, then switch to Recall so it stays put (Recall makes it retrace where it just was) and quickly scramble across it. Reach the back, step on the altar, and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Chichim Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Desert, inside the Ancient Prison Ruins reached by sinking through the quicksand at Palu Wasteland east of Gerudo Town
shrines.oneLine: Drop into the Ancient Prison Ruins via the sinkhole, flip the Ultrahand switches and dodge Gibdos, then clear the shrine inside.
shrines.solution: Chichim is hidden underground in the Ancient Prison Ruins, so the real puzzle is just getting in. East of Gerudo Town in the Palu Wasteland there's a patch of quicksand (around coordinates -3114, -3074); sink through it to drop into the ruins. Inside, use Ultrahand to pull the lever and open the first gate, then follow the corridor past the Gibdos (hit them with a fire or shock arrow first to make them collapse, then finish with melee) to a second lever that opens more cells. Reach the statue of a woman holding a sword and use Ascend on the opening above it to reach an upper cell with a chest holding a Gerudo Scimitar, then Ultrahand the stone slabs on the floor to expose a hidden lever; pull it to open the way to the shrine. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine — no puzzle, so the Light of Blessing is basically free. Grab the chest straight ahead for a Mighty Zonaite Spear, then walk to the monk's altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Karahatag Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Desert, south of the Southern Oasis
shrines.oneLine: Drifting Flame: lift a lit brazier high with Ultrahand, hit Recall so it hangs, then run to light the inverted ceiling pillars.
shrines.solution: First room: there's a closed gate with an unlit, upside-down ceiling torch above it and a lit flame nearby. Light an arrow on the flame (or toss a Fire Fruit / lit torch) and hit the inverted torch to ignite it and open the gate. The second room is the Recall trick. Glide down to the lower platform first and open the chest there for a Mighty Construct Bow, then Ascend back up. Now use Ultrahand to lift the movable stone torch up into one of the three hollow chambers in the ceiling so it can light that pillar's wick. The reliable way: set the torch down, activate Recall on it, then step on the pressure pad — the floor drops while Recall floats the torch up into the chamber to light the candle as it returns. Step off, stop Recall, move the torch under the next chamber, and repeat for all three. Once all three are lit, walk to the altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Irasak Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Desert, south of Arbiter's Grounds
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: no trial inside. Just reach it across the desert, open the chest, and claim the Light of Blessing.
shrines.solution: This is a Rauru's Blessing shrine, so there's no trial inside — the whole challenge is reaching it across the quicksand south of Arbiter's Grounds. The cleanest approach is from above: if you've cleared the Lightning Temple, fast-travel there, Ascend to its roof, look southeast to spot the shrine, then glide down (Tulin's gust helps you land right on the platform), since touching the surrounding quicksand will pull you under. If you can't, use Recall on the wooden boards drifting across the sand to ride them, hopping between boards and the ruins poking up until you reach the shrine. Once you're inside there's nothing to solve — open the chest straight ahead for a Large Zonai Charge, then continue to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Miryotanog Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Desert, south of Toruma Dunes, east of Gerudo Town and north of the Lightning Temple
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Lure. Stripped of gear, grab the local weapons and lure the five constructs into the rolling-boulder traps.
shrines.solution: This is a Proving Grounds trial, so all your own gear is stripped — you fight with only what's inside. Grab the starter weapons on your left (Old Wooden Bow, Thick Stick, Long Stick) and the Fire Fruits near the wall; fusing a wooden crate onto a stick makes a solid two-hander. The room's flashing wall lasers are the weapon: tripping one drops heavy metal boulders that roll across the floor. Lure the roaming Soldier Constructs into the boulders' path (or trip a laser from a distance) so they get flattened — flaming arrows from Fire-Fruit-fused shots also work, and one headshot drops the lone archer construct across the water. Once every construct is down, run up the ramp and use Ascend on the mesh overhang to reach the exit; the chest up there holds a Captain II Blade. Then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Motsusis Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Desert, hidden inside the South Lomei Labyrinth maze southeast of the desert
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: the only challenge is reaching it. Solve the South Lomei Labyrinth maze, then claim the free Light of Blessing.
shrines.solution: There's no puzzle here, just the maze in the way, so this is a Rauru's Blessing shrine and the Light of Blessing is free once you reach it. The shrine sits at the heart of the South Lomei Labyrinth in the Gerudo Highlands. The simplest route is to launch from Gerudo Canyon Skyview Tower and glide in, then work down through the maze toward the center, following the trail of acorns and Chickaloo Tree Nuts that marks the path; Tulin's gust helps clear blocking sand. Parts of the labyrinth are coated in Gloom, so bring gloom-resistant food or armor before you go in. Inside, open the treasure chest for a Large Zonai Charge, then touch the monk's altar at the back to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Rakakudaj Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands, along the Gerudo Canyon Path near Gerudo Canyon Stable
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing unlocked by The Gerudo Canyon Crystal quest: deliver the crystal along the path to make the shrine appear.
shrines.solution: This shrine stays sealed until you finish its shrine quest, The Gerudo Canyon Crystal, so do that first. Go to the shrine and interact with it to start the quest; it's missing its crystal, and a beam of light points the way down the canyon toward it. Follow the beam to a green crystal hidden among rocks and debris to the south, near the water. Nearby you'll find Zonai parts (big wheels on stone slabs and a steering stick); use Ultrahand to build a small cart, attach the crystal to it, and drive it back along the path the beam shows, up the sloping rocks and small waterfalls. If you get hung up on a ledge, hop off and lift the cart over with Ultrahand. At the end, set the crystal into the shrine's empty socket to unlock it. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine, so there's no puzzle: open the chest for a Mighty Zonaite Longsword, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Turakamik Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands, north of Gerudo Canyon Mine, east of Gerudo Canyon Stable
shrines.oneLine: Hidden Metal: use Ultrahand to clink the electrified metal balls onto the others, sending current through gears to open the gates.
shrines.solution: This is an electricity puzzle. In the first room, use Ultrahand to grab the charged metal ball hanging from the battery and clink it onto the other metal ball beside it; current flows and a big gear starts turning. Use Recall to reverse the slowly spinning gear, then climb on and ride it up to the higher platform. The next room has two ball-and-chains hung slightly too far apart: grab the right-hand ball, pull it as far right as you can and let go so it swings, then grab the second ball and attach it as the first swings close, completing the circuit. Use Ascend on the platforms jutting from the wall to climb to the top room, where another gear guards a chest holding a Strong Zonaite Shield; Recall it to reach the chest. For the final gate, hang the charged ball over one of the two wall poles, then lay a metal beam across both poles so the current bridges them, opening the gate. Step through and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Kitawak Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands area, on the East Gerudo Mesa ridge that separates the desert from Faron
shrines.oneLine: Upward and Forward: Ultrahand planks onto the raised bridges to lower them, then use the gears and catapults to launch upward.
shrines.solution: This puzzle is about using stone slabs to cross gaps and then catapulting upward. At the first gap, use Ultrahand to grab the stone slab from the wall on your right and attach it to the far end of the standing platform ahead so it tips over into a bridge; walk across. Take a slab and stick it onto the slowly spinning gear, then hop on as it comes back around and ride it across to a chest holding Arrows x10. To reach the altar you need a catapult: take the slab you used as a ramp and attach it to the far end of the hinged launch platform on the right. Stand on the far edge and shoot the glowing switch with a bow and arrow; the platform flings you up to the altar. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Otutsum Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands, in the north of Risoka Snowfield, far northeast of Gerudo Highlands Skyview Tower
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: no trial. Brave the cold snowfield to reach it, then grab the Light of Blessing and the chest inside.
shrines.solution: There's no trial inside, so this is a Rauru's Blessing shrine and the Light of Blessing is free; the only real challenge is surviving the cold to reach it, in Risoka Snowfield in the Gerudo Highlands. Before you go, cook cold-resistance food or equip warm gear like the Snowquill set, since this corner of the snowfield is freezing. Once you're inside, open the treasure chest in front of you for a Topaz (a gem you can sell or fuse onto a weapon for a lightning attack), then walk to the monk's altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Mayamats Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands, southeast of Gerudo Highlands Skyview Tower
shrines.oneLine: A Route for a Ball: build a track with Ultrahand and use Ascend, then roll the correct ball into the floor socket by the gate.
shrines.solution: This puzzle is about moving a large ball and a small ball with Ultrahand and Ascend. Ride the wind across to the next ledge, then use Ascend to pop up beneath the platform holding the big ball, and grab the ball with Ultrahand. For the optional chest, set the big ball in the middle of the gap and use it as a stepping stone to cross to the chest, which holds a Large Zonai Charge. Now wedge the big ball into the funnel-shaped notch in the wall and Ascend up through it to the higher platform. From there, extend the big ball out over the railing toward the back of the room and, when the small ball swings close, press the attach button to stick the two together. Drop both onto the railing with the big ball resting on top, then take the small ball and place it in the vessel in front of the altar room to open the gate. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Suariwak Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands, inside Taafei Hill Cave (the Yiga Blademaster Station) northeast of Gerudo Canyon Stable
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing with no puzzle, but the door stays locked until you pass The Yiga Clan Exam side quest in Yiga disguise.
shrines.solution: The shrine's door stays locked until you pass its quest, The Yiga Clan Exam, so do that first. The shrine sits inside Taafei Hill Cave, reached through the Yiga Blademaster Station whose entrance is behind a big waterfall in the Gerudo Highlands (look for the door with two frog statues outside). Equip the full Yiga Set so you're in disguise, then talk to the Blademaster to start the exam. He tasks you with offering a Mighty Banana at each of five frog statues scattered around the region (he provides the bananas and marks the spots); drop one in each statue's bowl, then return to him. He opens the inner door, where chests hold a Ruby and a Pristine Eightfold Longblade, and grants access to the shrine. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle: open the chest ahead for a Big Battery, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Rotsumamu Shrine
shrines.location: Gerudo Highlands, in a gully between Vatorsa Snowfield and Sapphia's Table beside a Depths chasm
shrines.oneLine: A Balanced Plan: attach heavy weights to the seesaws with Ultrahand to balance and ride them up, or shortcut with Recall.
shrines.solution: This puzzle is about balancing seesaws with heavy weights so you can climb them. Cross the first seesaw to the upper area, then attach the heavy barrel to the lower (left) side of the next seesaw with Ultrahand so it holds level and you can walk up it. For the optional chest, attach a plank to the left side of the last seesaw and a heavy block onto that, ride the seesaw down to its lowest point, then grab the heavy block with Ultrahand and yank it loose; as the seesaw snaps up it launches you, so jump and glide across to the chest, which holds Large Zonaite. To finish, reattach the heavy block to the left side, walk up the now-balanced seesaw, and go through the door. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Makurukis Shrine
shrines.location: Hyrule Ridge Surface, north of Tabantha Bridge Stable on a ledge of Mount Rhoam (approx -2847, 0630, 0233).
shrines.oneLine: Combat Training: Archery. Grab the bow by the door and headshot each construct. For the trio, Ascend a side pillar and shoot in bullet time as you fall.
shrines.solution: This is a combat-training shrine that teaches bow headshots. There's a bow and arrows on the rack by the entrance if you need them, so grab those first. The first challenge is a single Training Construct: take aim the moment you can move and put an arrow through its head before it reacts (if it lines up a shot at you, just strafe left or right to dodge). Landing that headshot triggers the second challenge, three constructs at once. The key trick is to get airborne: jump off any raised edge or pillar in the arena and aim your bow while you're falling, which slows time to a crawl and gives you plenty of room to put an arrow through all three heads before you land. Clearing them opens the way to a chest holding a Strong Construct Bow. Then step to the monk's altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Runakit Shrine
shrines.location: Hyrule Ridge Surface, west of Lindor's Brow Skyview Tower between Upland Lindor and Mount Rhoam (approx -2531, 1170, 0178).
shrines.oneLine: Built to Carry. Use Ultrahand to attach stone cylinders or slabs to the orb so it rolls down the rails, then drop it into the floor switch to open the bars.
shrines.solution: This puzzle is about making a ball roll down sloped rails to a floor switch. At the start, jump to the two ladders and use Ascend to pop straight up to the top quickly. Use Ultrahand to set the ball on the first railing so it rolls down on its own to the next platform. For the next stretch the ball needs help to ride the rail: grab the loose poles nearby and Ultrahand them onto the ball in a cross shape so the poles sit on the rails and carry the ball down with them. Keep ferrying the ball forward this way, building a simple minecart from the loose slabs and poles where there's no clean rail to carry it across. For the optional treasure, use Ultrahand to lean the flat stone slabs together into a long ramp up to the raised alcove and cross to a chest holding a Construct Bow. Finally, pull the ball free and drop it into the round hole in the floor; its weight presses the switch and the gate opens. Walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Sonapan Shrine
shrines.location: Hyrule Ridge Surface, on the eastern cliff of Satori Mountain, southwest of Lookout Landing (approx -1921, 0357, 0228).
shrines.oneLine: Missing Pathways. Use Ascend on overhangs and slide the stone bricks to build paths across the gaps, repositioning blocks to reach each higher ledge.
shrines.solution: There are gaps everywhere here, and the answer is Ascend plus a movable metal cube rather than climbing. From the start, stand under the overhang and use Ascend to rise up through it to the next level. For the side chest, grab the metal cube with Ultrahand and set it on top of the stone block so that part of it juts out as an overhang, then stand beneath the exposed part and use Ascend to pass straight up through it, then drop down to the chest holding five Arrows. To keep climbing, reposition that same cube: set it directly on top of a central ledge, stand underneath, and Ascend up through both the ledge and the cube to reach the next platform. Repeat the pattern, sliding the cube into place under wherever you need to reach and Ascending through it, and use your paraglider to glide across any gap a cube can't bridge. Once you reach the top, walk to the monk's altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Taki-ihaban Shrine
shrines.location: Hyrule Ridge Surface, inside Lindor's Brow Cave just east of Lindor's Brow Skyview Tower (approx -1829, 1149, 0147).
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. No puzzle inside; the cave is the trial. Gloom Hands lurk at the platform base, so sprint past and climb up to claim the Light of Blessing.
shrines.solution: This shrine sits inside Lindor's Brow Cave, so reaching it is the trial. Fast-travel to Lindor's Brow Skyview Tower and head east along the cliff to find a hole; drop in and glide down through the chasm to the pool below. Make for the cave mouth with the big stone structure glowing greenish. As you near the shrine platform, Gloom Hands rise out of the ground and chase you. You don't have to fight them: just sprint straight for the platform and scramble up the wall to the shrine, which puts you out of their reach (lingering to fight risks summoning Phantom Ganon, so running past is the safe call for a beginner). Activate the terminal. This is a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle inside, so the Light of Blessing is essentially free. Open the treasure chest straight ahead for a Large Zonai Charge, then walk to the monk's altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Usazum Shrine
shrines.location: Hyrule Ridge Surface, just south of Satori Mountain Foothill Cave at the foot of Satori Mountain (approx -2139, -0874, 0093).
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Activate the shrine; its green beam points to the cave. Take the crystal from the Hinox inside, then carry it back to the shrine to claim the blessing.
