shrines.solution: This shrine stays sealed until you do its shrine quest, The Satori Mountain Crystal, so tackle that first. Make your way to the shrine at the southeast foot of Satori Mountain and activate the pedestal; a green beam shoots out pointing toward the nearby Satori Mountain Foothill Cave. Follow the beam into that cave and head around the bend, where you'll find a Hinox with the green crystal worn as a necklace. To beat it, shoot its single eye with a bow to stun it, then rush in and attack while it's down (you can also fire at its eye from a distance and dodge the rocks it throws). When it falls it drops the Satori Mountain Crystal. Pick the crystal up with Ultrahand, carry it back to the shrine, and set it in place; the shrine rises and opens. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle, so the Light of Blessing is essentially free. Open the treasure chest straight ahead for a Strong Zonaite Spear, then walk to the monk's altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Gasas Shrine
shrines.location: Southwest of Tanagar Canyon, in the far southwest corner of Tabantha Frontier (approx -4153, 0098, 0055).
shrines.oneLine: Well-Timed Cuts: cut the rope to drop the big cube onto the ramp with an overhang, then Ascend up through it to cross the gap.
shrines.solution: This one is named Well-Timed Cuts, and the trick is cutting ropes at the right moment. At the first gap a large cube hangs from a rope above a sloped wall: shoot the rope with an arrow so the cube drops and wedges against the ramp at an angle, then use Ascend to rise straight up through it and reach the next ledge. Next, use Ultrahand to join the two long metal slabs into a bridge to cross, and watch for two treasure chests hanging overhead. Slide your bridge under each chest in turn and shoot its rope so it drops onto the bridge instead of into the pit; one holds a Large Zonai Charge and the other a Small Key. For the final part, a ball swings on a rope: as it swings toward you, shoot the rope so the ball drops onto the platform and rolls into the floor recess, opening the way out. Step to the altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Gatakis Shrine
shrines.location: North of Rospro Pass Skyview Tower; launch from the tower and glide north to reach it (approx -3652, 1806, 0168).
shrines.oneLine: Ride the Winds: glide on the updrafts, weave through the laser gaps, and open your paraglider in the gusts to rise to each exit.
shrines.solution: This is Ride the Winds, a gliding-and-wind shrine, so keep your paraglider handy throughout. From the entrance, run forward and jump off the ledge to glide across the first gap. Line up with the two vertical laser beams ahead and gently steer the glider through the gap between them without touching them. Drop down the big hole in the center; a slab spins inside it, so dive (press the jump/dive button) through the opening in the slab as it lines up. In the next chamber there's a large block of ice below you, plus enemies on platforms. Jump on the ice a few times to shatter it and clear your footing. Before leaving, glide over to the tallest platform, defeat the Construct guarding it, and open the treasure chest for a Strong Construct Bow. Then glide toward the exit, and the updraft there will carry you upward when you open your paraglider, lifting you to the altar. Touch the altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Ikatak Shrine
shrines.location: Southwestern Tabantha Frontier, just above Gisa Crater south of Rito Village (approx -3950, 1138, 0112).
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: no puzzle inside. Appears after the shrine quest. Open the chest and touch the altar for the Light of Blessing.
shrines.solution: This shrine stays buried until you finish its shrine quest, The Gisa Crater Crystal, so do that first. Walk up to the empty shrine pad and examine it to start the quest; a thin beam of green light points from it toward the crystal you need, and there's a hole nearby to drop into. Head down into the Gisa Crater Cave (go by daylight so there are fewer enemies) and deal with the Ice Likes and other foes inside. At the bottom you'll find the green crystal plus a pair of Zonai rockets. Use Ultrahand to attach a rocket to each side of the crystal, stand it so the rockets point almost straight up, then strike a rocket to blast the crystal up and out of the crater; use Ascend to get yourself back to the surface. Find the crystal up top and carry it back to the shrine pad to make the shrine rise. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle: open the treasure chest for a Big Battery (a large Zonai battery capsule), then touch the altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Iun-orok Shrine
shrines.location: Underground inside Tanagar Canyon West Cave, in the valley below the canyon (approx -3539, 0851, -0119).
shrines.oneLine: The Right Roll: glue only TWO balls together (not three) so the orb rolls straight down the ramp and lands on the target.
shrines.solution: This is The Right Roll, where you roll heavy balls down ramps onto targets. In the first room, use Ultrahand to grab the ball and roll it down the ramp into the big floor button to open the door. In the next room the ramp is too steep for a single ball, so glue TWO balls together with Ultrahand, set them at the top of the ramp balanced on each side, and let them roll down to strike the target, which opens the next gate. The room after that has three balls of different sizes: for the optional chest, stack them largest-to-smallest into a staircase leaned against the pillar (smallest at the base so you can step up) and climb to the chest for 10 Arrows. For that final target, instead of rolling the balls you carry them up and tap the target directly to open the last gate. Then head to the altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Mayausiy Shrine
shrines.location: Deep inside the Forgotten Temple, north of Tanagar Canyon near Lindor's Brow (approx -1165, 2602, -0083).
shrines.oneLine: Building Blocks: use Ultrahand to grab and rotate the loose L-pieces so the incomplete square matches the finished model beside it.
shrines.solution: This is Building Blocks, hidden deep inside the Forgotten Temple, and it's a shape-matching puzzle. In the first room you'll see a completed cube on the left and an incomplete one on the right; use Ultrahand to grab the loose white L-shaped pieces, rotate them, and fit them onto the unfinished block so it matches the completed cube. That opens the gate. In the next room, defeat the Captain Construct first. Before the main puzzle, grab a couple of the blocks and stack them into a step (or use Ascend) to reach the raised platform in the corner, where the treasure chest holds a Large Zonai Charge. Then solve the room's puzzle the same way as the first, this time fitting three pieces onto the incomplete shape and rotating each until it snaps in to complete the cube, which opens the last gate. Walk to the altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Nouda Shrine
shrines.location: Inside Kopeeki Drifts Cave; ride a horse from Snowfield Stable to reach the snowy cave entrance (approx -2319, 2200, 0173).
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Intermediate. Stripped of your gear, grab the supplied weapons, fuse enemy parts onto them, and Ascend up to clear each tier.
shrines.solution: This is a Proving Grounds: Intermediate combat shrine, so the moment you enter you're stripped of all your weapons, armor, shields, and Zonai devices and must use only what's provided inside. On the ground floor, grab the weapons set out near the entrance and take down the Constructs, including an archer up on a platform; headshots and sneakstrikes help you avoid damage. Burn the leaves on the right wall (Fire Fruit makes a quick fire arrow) to expose a Shock Emitter Zonai device, then Fuse it to a weapon or shield, because it's excellent for stunning the tougher Constructs, and it does wide damage when you strike the metal floor near them. Climb up and clear the upper Constructs, using Ascend where it lets you pop onto a higher platform behind enemies. Once every Construct is defeated, the door to the side chest opens; grab the chest for a Hearty Elixir. Then walk to the altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Oromuwak Shrine
shrines.location: On a mountaintop southeast of the Lucky Clover Gazette, near Brightcap Cave (approx -3079, 1617, 0243).
shrines.oneLine: A Launching Device: fuse a Rocket to a minecart, point it up the rails, hop in and ignite it to ride up to the next area.
shrines.solution: This is A Launching Device, built around fusing Zonai Rockets to minecarts. In the first room, use Ultrahand to set a Rocket on the rail pointed at the large target, then hit it with a weapon to fire it into the target and open the door. In the main room, bring a Rocket over and attach it to the back of the minecart on the rails, hop in, and strike the rocket to blast yourself straight up to the next platform. For the optional chest, build a cart with two Rockets pointing up, ride it high, then hop out and glide over to the platform with the chest, which holds a Ruby. In the final section, fuse Rockets to a cart facing forward, place it carefully (there's no rail here), climb in and ignite it to launch yourself across to the exit. Then touch the altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Turakawak Shrine
shrines.location: Northern Tabantha Frontier near Tabantha Hills, at the region's north edge (approx -3497, -0197, 0066).
shrines.oneLine: Stacking a Path: use Ultrahand to stack the climbable blocks into a tower against each ledge, then climb to reach the ladder and exit.
shrines.solution: This is Stacking a Path, and it's all about stacking climbable blocks with Ultrahand. In the first room, use Ultrahand to position the large climbable block and add the second on top to make a double stack you can climb. One tip that trips people up: orient the blocks so the climbable (grated) face is reachable; if it's turned the wrong way Link can't grip it. For the optional chest, carry your double stack so it sits under the gated section of the ceiling, climb to the top, and use Ascend to pass up through the grate into the caged area above, where the chest holds a Magic Rod. For the exit, attach your stack on top of the metal block to make a triple stack with the metal block on the bottom, move it to the corner nearest the upper ledge, then climb up and jump across to reach the way out. Then touch the altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Eutoum Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), inside Goflam's Secret Hot Spring, northern Hebra Mountains
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Infiltration. You start stripped of gear, so grab the weapons by the entrance, then sneak or fight past the Soldier Constructs.
shrines.solution: This is a Proving Grounds: Infiltration combat trial, so it strips all your own weapons, shields, armor, and Zonai devices at the door — you fight only with what's provided. Grab every weapon and bow lying near the entrance before you step into the main room, then sneak instead of charging in. Use Ascend on a nearby ledge to pop up to the walkway above, line up behind a Soldier Construct while its back is turned, and a falling sneakstrike (or a quiet ground sneakstrike) does big damage. Pick up the weapon it drops and use Fuse to attach a Soldier Construct Horn or one of the floor spikes to hit much harder. Lure the rest out one at a time with arrows so you never face the whole room at once, and clear all the constructs to open the way to the altar. Open the chest in the altar room for a Sapphire, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Mayaotaki Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), at the center of North Lomei Labyrinth in North Tabantha Snowfield
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Solving the North Lomei Labyrinth maze to reach it is the trial; the shrine itself has no inner puzzle.
shrines.solution: This is a Rauru's Blessing shrine at the heart of the North Lomei Labyrinth — the maze is the whole trial and the shrine itself has no inner puzzle. Bring cold-resistance gear (Snowquill set or spicy food); the labyrinth floor also has patches of dark Gloom, so pack a Gloom-recovery dish or the Depths armor. Enter from the western camp where a trail of Hylian Pine Cones marks the route: light the campfire, toss a pine cone into it to blow an updraft you can glide on, and follow the corridors inward. If you'd rather skip the maze, climb a niche to the top of the labyrinth walls (or drop in from the sky islands above) and glide straight down to the two torches at the shrine. Inside, open the chest for a Diamond, then walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Orochium Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), near Snowfield Stable in South Tabantha Snowfield, northwest of the Forgotten Temple
shrines.oneLine: Courage to Fall. Step off the edge and ride the rising gusts of wind to drift safely down onto the lower platforms toward the exit.
shrines.solution: The name is the hint — at the laser walls you're meant to step into a beam on purpose so the trapdoor drops you to the level below. Use Ultrahand to open the stone door at the entrance, then take the path to the left, beat the lone Construct, and use Ascend to reach the upper level. Drop down and follow the path for a chest holding 5 arrows. Now touch the lasers to collapse the floor; in the room below, walk to the far end avoiding the beams and use Ascend under a platform for a chest with a Small Key. Ascend back up, defeat the constructs guarding the center, and use the key to open the door. Carry the ball out with Ultrahand, ride a moving platform to the upper level, and drop the ball into its socket to open the final room. There, place the provided Zonai Wing on the rail facing the exit, attach the ball to it, and smack the fans to sail it across; remove the ball, set it in the vessel by the altar room to open it, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Oshozan-u Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), atop an icy hill west of North Lomei Labyrinth
shrines.oneLine: Mallet Smash. Wield the big hammer with Ultrahand to smash blocks and pound the switches that open the way forward.
shrines.solution: This shrine is about building battering rams and spinning mallets with Ultrahand to smack switches. In the first room, grab the log and attach it to the metal cube on the rail so it points at the wall switch, then stick a Zonai rocket on the back of the cube and strike it — the cube slams forward and the log hits the switch, opening the next door. For the side chest, fuse a rocket to your shield and fire it to launch yourself up to the ledge, where the chest holds a Zonaite Bow. In the final room, use Ultrahand to attach the larger log to the spinning gear next to the switch, fix a rocket to the far end of the log, then hit the rocket so the log whips around and strikes the switch, opening the way to the altar. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Otak Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), inside Icefall Foothills Cave (melt the ice blocking the entrance with fire)
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Traps. You start weaponless, so use the provided gear to fight Constructs while dodging the spike and floor traps.
shrines.solution: The Icefall Foothills Cave mouth is sealed by ice, so melt it with a Fire Fruit, a Bomb Flower, or a flaming torch; inside, melt the ice deeper in and break through the ice floor to drop into the shrine. This is a Proving Grounds: Traps combat trial — it takes away your own gear, so grab the club, bow, and arrows by the entrance before you advance. Six Soldier Constructs wait in pairs, so don't aggro them all at once: shoot to pull them over one at a time, duck behind the pillars, and grab the arrows the archers fire back to refill your ammo. Fuse construct parts onto your club to keep it hitting hard, and lean on the room's own traps — shoot down a hanging lamp so it drops fire onto leaves and a nearby construct. Clear all six and the door opens; the chest holds a Mighty Construct Bow. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Rutafu-um Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), inside Hebra Mountains Northwest Cave, northwest of Hebra East Summit
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Do The Northwest Hebra Cave Crystal quest: carry the crystal through the cave to its pedestal to spawn the shrine.
shrines.solution: This shrine stays sealed until you finish its shrine quest, The Northwest Hebra Cave Crystal, so do that first. Find the Hebra Mountains Northwest Cave entrance (west of Selmie's Spot near Hebra East Summit) and drop in; clear the Bokoblins by the frozen lake, then examine the shrine's terminal to start the quest — the green crystal sits underwater out in the lake. To reach it, fuse an icicle (or any ice block) to a weapon and swing it at the water's surface to freeze a sheet of ice; hop across and keep making sheets until you're over the crystal (firing Ice-Fruit arrows works too). Grab the crystal with Ultrahand, haul it back to shore, then set it on the pedestal to raise the shrine. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle — open the chest for a Topaz, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Sahirow Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), atop Corvash Peak, east of Rospro Pass Skyview Tower, north of Rito Village
shrines.oneLine: Aid from Above. Use Ascend to phase up through the ceilings, rising past the laser nets to reach each higher level and the exit.
shrines.solution: This shrine is all about using Ascend to phase straight up through ceilings to skip the laser nets — Ascend needs something solid overhead, so look for the platform or panel above you each time. In the first room, crouch to slip under the wall lasers. In the next room a metal panel slides along an overhead track — stand directly beneath it and use Ascend the moment it passes over you to ride up onto the platform. For the side chest, grab a nearby metal cube with Ultrahand and set it in the path of the red laser; that trips the gate so you can reach a chest holding a Spicy Elixir. Use a cube to cross the gap, then crouch past the moving laser. At the finale, wait for the moving laser grid to come close, Ascend up onto the structure carrying it to get above the beams, then glide down to the exit. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Sisuran Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), northwest of Pikida Stonegrove Skyview Tower, northeast of Hebra East Summit
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Do The North Hebra Mountains Crystal quest: beat the Frost Talus (Fire Arrows or Yunobo) and haul its crystal to the pedestal.
shrines.solution: This shrine is buried until you finish its shrine quest, The North Hebra Mountains Crystal, so trigger and complete that first. Examine the Sisuran Shrine pedestal — the crystal is missing, which starts the quest and shoots a green beam toward where it sits. Follow the beam to a frozen boulder: the crystal is the weak point of a Frost Talus you must defeat. Its icy body freezes you on contact, so thaw the ice with fire — Fire-Fruit or Bomb-Flower arrows, or a Ruby-fused weapon — then dash in, climb its back, and pound the green crystal with your strongest weapon; repeat until the Talus falls. Use Ultrahand to lift the freed crystal and haul it back to the pedestal (a vehicle helps over the snow) to raise the shrine. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle — open the chest in the center for a Sapphire, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Tauyosipun Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), on the western slopes southwest of Hebra West Summit
shrines.oneLine: Forward or Backward? Use Recall to reverse moving platforms and rails so they carry you the direction you actually need to go.
shrines.solution: Bring cold-resistant gear or warm food before you arrive. This shrine is solved almost entirely with Recall — reversing an object's recent motion. First puzzle: a heavy ball rolls down a ramp at you; aim Recall at it to send it rolling back up the ramp, then run up behind it to the next platform. For the chest, a wheel keeps turning so a chest slides away from you — use Recall on the wheel to reverse it and bring the chest within reach, then grab the Strong Construct Bow inside. The later rooms repeat the idea: a glowing ball rolls down twin ramps, so Recall it over the gap and tap the button again to cancel Recall so it drops straight into the goal; and in the last room, place a ball on a back-and-forth platform with Ultrahand, then Recall it so it retraces the platform's path over the goal and cancel Recall to drop it in. Sink the final ball to open the way, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Wao-os Shrine
shrines.location: Hebra Mountains (Surface), inside West Lake Totori Cave, west of Rito Village
shrines.oneLine: Lever Power. Use Ultrahand with the lever-arm contraptions to apply force, swinging platforms or launching yourself across the gaps.
shrines.solution: Wao-os sits inside West Lake Totori Cave, west of Rito Village; to find the cave, climb the stone pillar (or Vah Medoh's Perch) at Rito Village and spot the bird-shaped snow patch to the west — the entrance is at the bird's head. Inside, the Lever Power trial has you build seesaw catapults with Ultrahand to fling an iron ball at orange targets. Attach the bowl to the end of the hanging board to make a catapult arm, set the iron ball in the bowl, then lift the metal box high over the opposite end and drop it — the seesaw whips the ball into the first target. For the taller target, attach the wooden plank to extend the board, move the bowl out to the new far end, line the box's drop-shadow over the lever, and launch again. For the side chest, climb into the bowl yourself and drop the box to fling yourself up, then glide right to a ledge with a chest holding a Spicy Elixir. Hit the targets to open the way, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Sikukuu Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Mountains, southeast of Thyphlo Ruins (0696, 2792, 0225)
shrines.oneLine: Spinning Gears: use Recall to reverse the big gears so platforms carry you upward, riding them to the exit.
shrines.solution: This is a puzzle shrine built around big spinning gears, and Recall is the key. In the first room a ball rides a turning gear; wait for the orb to drop onto the big gear ahead, then use Recall on that gear to reverse its spin so the orb falls into the floor hole and opens the gate ahead. In the next room, use Ultrahand to turn the knob (crank) on the pair of gears to wind them up with motion. For the optional treasure, turn the knob counter-clockwise a few times, Recall the gear to reverse it, then quickly slot yourself into the gear and ride it down into a hidden chamber holding a Zonaite Longsword; use Ascend on the ceiling to climb back up. To finish, turn the knob clockwise, slot into the gear, and Recall again so its reversed motion carries you up to the monk's altar. Touch the altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Marakuguc Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, on a cliff above Goron City (1761, 2508, 0437)
shrines.oneLine: Wheeled Wonders: Ultrahand the two wheeled carts together, ride your build, and strike the wheels to roll across the lava.
shrines.solution: This puzzle shrine, Wheeled Wonders, is about building wheeled vehicles with Ultrahand to roll across lava. At the start, use Ultrahand to grab the bridge piece below and reconnect the broken bridge, then cross. Attach a bridge slab to a wheeled platform and activate a wheel by hitting it with a melee strike or arrow so it drives forward, carrying you across. Next, grab one of the two matching wheeled carts with Ultrahand and stick it onto the other to make a bigger four-wheeled platform, then ride it over the lava. After the Soldier Construct's room, attach the large metal slab to the four-wheeled platform and hit a wheel so it drives forward, pushing the loose balls into the hole on the far side to open the gate. For the optional chest, use that curved metal slab as a ramp up the corner pillar (or just use Ascend) to reach the chest holding a Strong Construct Bow. Then walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Timawak Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, overlooking Bedrock Bistro (1798, 1635, 0311)
shrines.oneLine: Against the Flow: cross cooled-lava platforms, then Ultrahand spare platforms into a bridge to reach the orb and chest.
shrines.solution: This puzzle shrine, Against the Flow, has you crossing lava on stone slabs, and Recall is the trick. A Zonai Hydrant pours water onto the lava to make slabs drift across; wait for several slabs to line up, then hop across (and defeat the Construct on the far side). In the orb room, use Recall on a slab to ride it back along the path it floated in on toward your goal. For the optional treasure, ride toward the elevated ball, spot the chest tucked in an alcove, then grab a slab with Ultrahand, push it toward the chest, pull it back, and Recall it so it carries you in to open the chest for a Strong Zonaite Shield. To finish, hit a Zonai Fan for an updraft, set a Zonai Hydrant to make a slab over the lava, attach a second Zonai Fan to the back of that slab with Ultrahand to make a little raft, then activate the fan to ride across to the monk's altar. Touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Isisim Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, inside YunoboCo HQ East Cave (1841, 2841, 0363)
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: In Reverse. Use only provided gear; Recall the gears to flip enemy approaches and clear the Constructs.
shrines.solution: This is a Proving Grounds combat shrine, In Reverse, so your own gear is stripped and you fight with only what's inside. Grab the Wooden Stick, Thick Stick, and Old Wooden Shield by the entrance, head up the first ramp, and take out the first Construct (you can also detonate the Zonai Time Bombs near it). Pick up the bow the bow-wielding Construct drops, then use Recall on the moving gear to ride it up to the next floor and fight the Construct above. When a Construct lobs a Time Bomb at you, use Recall on it while it's still in the air to send it flying back to explode on the enemy; those bombs can also blow open breakable walls/blocks to reveal items and parts you can Fuse onto your weapon. Clear every Construct and the gate to the side chest opens, holding a Magic Rod. Then step to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Sitsum Shrine
shrines.location: Death Mountain, west side near the summit (2367, 2597, 0790)
shrines.oneLine: A Controlling Device: take a steering stick, drive a cart across the lava, then attach it to a fan-wing and fly to the exit.
shrines.solution: This puzzle shrine, A Controlling Device, teaches you to drive Zonai vehicles with a Steering Stick. For the first lava river, the path branches three ways; hop on a cart and take it across. For the optional chest, look for a runed orb on a shelf to the right, grab it with Ultrahand and set it into the orb hole on the orange ring at the lava intersection to open the chest doors; the chest holds a Mighty Construct Bow. Drive on around the corner and deal with the Soldier Construct. At the gap, use Ultrahand to take a Steering Stick off a cart and attach it to one of the fan-wings (planes), then fly toward the swirling green light beam, remembering that pushing the stick forward dips the nose down and pulling back lifts it up. Land by the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Sibajitak Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, north of Death Caldera (2399, 3269, 0402)
shrines.oneLine: Alignment: use Recall to line up the rotating pillar's segments, then Ascend straight up through them to reach the exit.
shrines.solution: This puzzle shrine, Alignment, is about lining up the segments of a rotating pillar so you can climb straight through it with Ascend. The central pillar is divided into three sections that each rotate; use Recall on a section to spin it back so its opening stacks with the others. For the optional chest, you only need the bottom and middle sections aligned: Recall those two so their openings line up, then stand under the bottom section and use Ascend to pop up onto a platform where a chest holds a Strong Zonaite Longsword. To finish, use Recall to line up all three pillar sections, position yourself beneath the stacked openings, and use Ascend to rise straight up through them to the top. Step to the monk's altar there and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Kimayat Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, west of Skull Lake (2871, 3625, 0239)
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Smash. Defeat all the Constructs (melee on the floor, archers above) with only the weapons provided.
shrines.solution: This is a Proving Grounds combat shrine, Smash, so your gear is stripped and you fight with only what's provided. Grab the Long Stick, Wooden Stick, and the Old Wooden Bow with its 10 arrows near the entrance. Melee Constructs roam the floor while archer Constructs stand on an elevated platform. Use Ascend to reach the side ledges, grab the Spiked Iron Balls there, and Fuse them onto your sticks for heavy hitters, then clear the ground-level Constructs first. For the archers above, use the room's water: attach an Ice Fruit to an arrow and shoot the pool next to the stone column to freeze an ice block to stand on, then smash the stone column propping up their platform so the remaining Constructs tumble into the water and are defeated. With every Construct down, the gate opens to a chest holding a Captain's Construct reaper-type weapon. Then step to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Mayachideg Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, west of Kanalet Ridge (3062, 1817, 0216)
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: The Hunt. Use only the items inside (start with a Wooden Stick) to hunt down and defeat the Constructs.
shrines.solution: This is a Proving Grounds shrine, so the moment you enter you lose all your own weapons, bows, shields, and armor — you can only fight with what's provided inside. In the entrance room, grab the Wooden Stick, then notice the two Homing Carts; hit each one to switch it on and it rolls forward to ram nearby enemies. Use Ultrahand to drag those two Homing Carts into the main room, where a group of Soldier Constructs is waiting, and let the carts crash into them. If you get swarmed, knock a Construct down to the lower level with the stick, then Ultrahand the Cannon on the floor: it auto-fires at whatever is in front of it, so just rotate it to aim at the remaining Constructs and let it blast away. Once every Construct is destroyed your own gear is returned and a gate opens to a chest (a Captain-series spear). Walk to the monk's altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Mayak Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Mountains, west of East Deplian Badlands (1270, 3733, 0106)
shrines.oneLine: A timing puzzle: launch up, Ultrahand a boulder onto the ramp, then swing the glowing post so it lands on the target.
shrines.solution: This whole shrine is a timing trial. Step onto the launching pad to ride up to the platform above, and use Ultrahand to set the stone sphere onto the ball track up there so it starts rolling down toward the gap. Quickly drop back to the floor and stand by the orange pedestal/switch — the trick is the timing: hit the pedestal just before the sphere rolls off the end of the track, and the trigger it reveals opens the gate ahead in time. Repeat the same launch-up, place-the-sphere, dive-down-and-hit-the-pedestal sequence at the next launching pad to clear the second gate. For some treasure, look for a chest hanging from a rope on the wall and shoot the rope with an arrow (or glide over and slash it) to drop it; it holds a Large Zonai Charge. Then head to the far end and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Minetak Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Mountains, inside Deplian Badlands Cave (0394, 3485, 0068)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: reach the shrine deep in the cave, open the chest, and touch the altar for the Light of Blessing.
shrines.solution: There's no puzzle here — Minetak is a Rauru's Blessing shrine — but it's buried inside the dark, dangerous Deplian Badlands Cave, so the real work is getting there. The cave is pitch black, so bring Brightbloom Seeds to throw for light (or wear the Miner armor for a built-in glow), and there's an enemy camp outside you can sneak past. Drop into the hole at the entrance, then make a left at the first intersection and keep heading forward; you'll reach a chamber with a couple of Horriblins guarding a chest. Inside the shrine, the Light of Blessing is essentially free: head to the treasure chest, which holds a Big Battery (it extends your Zonai energy cell), then continue to the monk's altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Jiotak Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, inside Isle of Rabac Gallery cave (1833, 3179, 0257)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: wear Flamebreaker gear, navigate the hot cave to the shrine, grab the chest, and claim the blessing.
shrines.solution: Jiotak itself is a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle — the challenge is the blazing-hot Isle of Rabac Gallery cave you cross to reach it, so equip the Flamebreaker armor set (or down a Fireproof Elixir) before going in or the heat will keep burning you. Inside, use Ultrahand to attach a Zonai Fan to the back of a mining cart and set the cart on the rail tracks, then hop aboard and hit the fan to get rolling. As you ride, watch for target signs beside the rails: shoot one with an arrow (or use Yunobo) to flip the track switch and steer the cart toward the shrine; if you miss, the cart loops around for another try. When the shrine's green light comes into view off the side, jump off and glide over to it. Inside, open the chest for a Magic Staff, then walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Momosik Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, near Death Caldera (2957, 2759, 0524)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: appears after the crystal quest; enter, open the chest for a Big Battery, and take the blessing.
shrines.solution: This shrine stays sealed until you finish its shrine quest, The Death Caldera Crystal, so do that first. Find the dormant shrine on the eastern side of Death Mountain and activate its terminal — a bright green tether shoots out pointing toward the shrine's missing crystal. Head northwest to a cave blocked by blue rocks (the Death Mountain East Tunnel); wear the Flamebreaker armor set (or use a Fireproof Elixir) because the heat is extreme. Inside, deal with the Horriblin, then use Ultrahand to build a cart with a Zonai Fan, set it on the tracks, and ride to the end. The crystal sits on a lava rock that turns out to be an Igneo Talus — fire an Ice-fruit-fused arrow to cool it, climb up, and break the ore on its back to free the crystal. Carry the crystal back through the cave (the cart works again) and set it on the shrine to make it rise. Inside it's a free blessing: open the chest for a Big Battery, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Moshapin Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, inside Lake Intenoch Cave (2678, 1905, 0131)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: carry the green crystal to the shrine to unlock it, open the chest, then claim the blessing.
shrines.solution: Moshapin won't appear until you finish its shrine quest, The Lake Intenoch Cave Crystal, so head into Lake Intenoch Cave in Eldin Canyon first — it's blisteringly hot, so wear the Flamebreaker armor set or carry Fireproof Elixirs. Fuse a rock to a weapon to make a hammer and smash the breakable boulders ahead (the left path hides a side chest with a Strong Construct Bow). Beyond them you reach a lava lake with the green crystal; interact with it and a beam shows where it must go — across the lava. Defeat the fiery monster guarding the device, then pick up the Zonai Hydrant beside it and spray the lava to freeze a solid surface (do NOT rely on Splash Fruit). Attach a nearby Zonai Fan to make a raft, load the crystal aboard, and ride across, following the beam. Set the crystal on the dormant Moshapin Shrine to unlock it. Inside it's a free blessing: open the chest, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Kisinona Shrine
shrines.location: Eldin Canyon, between Maw of Death Mountain and Cephla Lake (2568, 1245, 0173)
shrines.oneLine: Wind Power: attach and activate the Zonai fans onto the turbine to generate wind and push platforms to the exit.
shrines.solution: This is the Wind Power shrine, a single-room puzzle built around a big wheel ringed by turbine vanes, with two Zonai Fans sitting nearby. Use Ultrahand to pick up both fans and attach them to the wheel on opposite sides, laid on their sides so they face opposite directions — that way they spin the wheel rather than fight each other. Then shoot the fans with an arrow to switch them on; the spinning wheel drives the surrounding turbines, and once it's powered the door to the exit opens (it stays open even after the fans stop). Before you leave, grab the chest on the side ledge: detach one fan and lay it flat at the base of the pillar so its airflow points straight up, then jump into the updraft and open your paraglider to ride up to the ledge. The chest holds a Mighty Construct Bow. Head out and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Domizuin Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Highlands, southeast of South Akkala Stable up the mountain (3305, 1443, 0426)
shrines.oneLine: A Prone Pathway: Hit the inside and outside rotation pillars to roll the giant cube until its steps line up, then platform to the exit.
shrines.solution: This puzzle is all about rolling a giant cube using its two rotation switches — one on the outside, one inside — never climbing. Start by Ascending up the pillar at the entrance, then hit the outer rotation switch to spin the big cube and reveal an opening; walk inside. Look right for a short pillar jutting from the wall and use Ascend underneath it to reach the first chest (Arrows x10). Use the inner switch to roll the cube further so a hole opens, drop down, and grab a second chest (a Zonai Charge). Back in the main room, hit the inner switch three times to bring a long pillar up against the ceiling, then quickly run to grab the Strong Zonaite Shield. To finish, alternate the outer and inner switches so the cube's steps line up with the exit — when a tall pillar nearly touches the ceiling, stand beneath it and use Ascend to pop out on top, then drop down to the green pad. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Gatanisis Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Highlands, far east near Ulria Grotto by the Akkala Sea coast (4501, 0826, 0095)
shrines.oneLine: A Well-Timed Bounce: Recall the spring platform as the ball rolls across it to fling the ball up into the switch.
shrines.solution: This one is pure timing with Recall. A large ball rolls down and crosses a springy bounce platform, but on its own it just rolls past a raised bullseye target on the far wall. Watch one full cycle so you learn the ball's path: it drops onto the platform, presses it down, then the platform springs back up. The trick is to use Recall on that spring platform just as the ball is rolling across it — Recall replays its upward bounce at that exact moment, flinging the ball up and into the orange target ring, which turns it green. For the optional chest, stand on the platform, wait for a ball to land and bounce you up, then glide over to the chest (Bomb Flower x5). If you mistime the shot, just wait for the ball to reset and try again. To finish, go to the far end of the platform and use Ascend on the now-green target ring above you, then glide across to the altar. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Jochi-iu Shrine
shrines.location: Deep Akkala, just northeast of East Akkala Stable (4350, 2972, 0164)
shrines.oneLine: Courage to Pluck: Ultrahand metal blocks out of the Jenga-like tower without toppling it, then climb to the altar.
shrines.solution: A tall Jenga-style tower of metal blocks stands ahead with a stone ball balanced on top, and the goal is to get that ball into the round recess in the floor to open the gate — without toppling the stack onto yourself. Use Ultrahand to pull out the horizontal block holding up the top platform; the top partly drops and the ball rolls free. Grab the ball quickly with Ultrahand before it falls away, carry it to the floor recess, and drop it in to open the door. For the chests, take your time: pull a block from the bottom of the stack and lean it like a ramp against the raised ledge, then climb (or Ascend) up to the first chest (a Zonaite Bow). The second chest sits inside the tower — pull the back piece of the level it rests on and Ultrahand the chest out to the side ledge (Large Zonaite). Pull blocks to the side, not straight out, so the stack doesn't crash down too soon. Once the door is open, walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Gemimik Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Sea, southeast of East Akkala Stable on the spiral Rist Peninsula island (4521, 2126, 0001)
shrines.oneLine: Turbine Power: Attach the propeller to the motor and bridge the wiring with a metal plate to power the fan and braziers.
shrines.solution: This is a wiring-and-fan puzzle. Use Ultrahand to grab the propeller (fan) and attach it to the bare motor in the center of the room — but it won't spin until it has power. Drag the metal plate from the far-left corner over the gap in the electrical wiring on the floor; the metal completes the circuit, electricity flows, and the propeller spins into a strong updraft. Ride that updraft: walk up the ramp, glide into the air current, and from the top glide to the back-right of the room for the chest (a Mighty Zonaite Shield). To finish, fetch the Flame Emitter near the stream of fire at the back-left and attach it to the spinning propeller — the whirling flame lights every brazier at once, opening the way (briefly sliding the metal plate off the wires to stop the spin can make attaching it easier). Then walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Rasiwak Shrine
shrines.location: Deep Akkala, northeast of Akkala Ancient Tech Lab (4663, 3263, 0002)
shrines.oneLine: Flotational Brilliance: Fuse spheres and fans to metal planks so they float, then ferry a boat onto the exit switch.
shrines.solution: The theme is floating things across water. First, shoot the rope holding a suspended platform so it drops into the pool, then use Ultrahand to stick a floaty sphere onto it so it bobs up — ride it across. In the main room, build a boat: attach a floaty sphere (or two) to a stone plank so it floats, add a Zonai fan to the back for propulsion, and steer it across the water. For the chest, float your boat under the chest hanging by a rope, shoot the rope with an arrow to drop it, and grab it with Ultrahand (it holds a Magic Scepter). To open the exit, peel a floaty sphere off your boat and Ultrahand it onto the trigger so it stays weighed down, opening the gate to the altar. Then walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Kamatukis Shrine
shrines.location: Deep Akkala, north of Tempest Gulch (3427, 3345, 0070)
shrines.oneLine: A Precise Strike: Aim launched balls and rotating cogs so a ball strikes the target switch to open the way forward.
shrines.solution: This is a pendulum-and-aim puzzle. A ball sits on a launcher and you must knock it across a gap into a target hole. Use Ultrahand to attach a block to the pendulum arm to make a giant hammer, pull it back, and release so it swings and smacks the ball forward into the well — it takes timing and a few tries, so ease off the very-full pull if the ball overshoots. If you miss, hit the reset crystal nearby to spawn a fresh ball and try again. For the optional chest, look to your left for a second, harder version where the target well slides side to side; keep the hammer fully raised and release only as the moving well swings toward the center so the ball drops in — a door opens revealing the chest (a Mighty Zonaite Longsword, which boosts the power of fused Zonai devices). Once the ball drops into the main target, the way opens — walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Sinatanika Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Highlands, northeast of Ulri Mountain Skyview Tower (3842, 2299, 0048)
shrines.oneLine: Combat Training Sneakstrike: Stay crouched in the patrolling construct's blind spot and slip behind for a one-hit sneakstrike.
shrines.solution: This is a combat-training shrine where only a Sneakstrike — a hit from directly behind an unaware enemy — does any damage; everything else bounces off. Grab one of the provided weapons up front so you don't wear down your own. For the first part, crouch to stay silent and slip along the walls behind the construct, only moving past gaps in cover while its eye-beam is pointed the other way; once you're right behind it and the strike prompt appears, attack for a one-hit Sneakstrike. The second part repeats this against a construct that now patrols a wider area — watch its route first, push the stick gently so you close the gap in time without going full-speed-loud, and stay in its blind spot. Once both are down, head into the inner chamber for the chest (a Sneaky Elixir). Then walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Rasitakiwak Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Highlands, southeast of Tarrey Town near Kaepora Pass (4161, 1324, 0229)
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds Vehicles: Stripped of gear, fuse the emitters and cannon to a Zonai vehicle and ride it to crush the constructs.
shrines.solution: This is a Proving Grounds shrine, so all your weapons, shields, and Zonai devices are taken away — you fight powerful constructs using only what's provided here. Don't try to brawl; build a war machine. Move forward staying out of the constructs' sight, then use Ascend to reach the elevated platform and board the Zonai plane parked there. Fly it into the central cage, where you'll find a sturdy Zonai vehicle plus a Flame Emitter, Shock Emitter, Hydrant, and Cannon. Use Ultrahand to fuse those weapons onto the spiked front of the vehicle, step on the trigger to open the cage, and drive out, mowing down and burning the constructs (watch you don't beach the vehicle on a wall and stall). When every construct is destroyed your gear is returned and the altar door opens; open the chest in front of the altar for a Magic Rod, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Jochi-ihiga Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Highlands, northeast of Akkala Falls near Lake Akkala (3813, 1218, 0090)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: Buy Hagie's green crystal in Tarrey Town and boat it to the light beam to reveal the shrine, then claim the reward.
shrines.solution: This shrine stays hidden until you finish its shrine quest, Rock for Sale, so do that first. Go to Tarrey Town and talk to Hagie, the man standing beside a glowing green crystal near the western edge of town — he offers to sell you the rock for 100 rupees, but his wife Ruli steps in and haggles it down to 50, so you buy it cheap. Pay Hagie the 20-rupee fee to use his railcart, attach the crystal to the railcart with Ultrahand, and ride south to the construction site by Lake Akkala. Carry the crystal to the lakeshore, attach it to a Zonai wing with a fan on the back, and float it across the water; once the quest completes, interact with the crystal and it shines a green beam pointing toward the hidden shrine — follow the beam to raise the shrine. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle, so the Light of Blessing is essentially free — grab the chest for a Diamond, then walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Igashuk Shrine
shrines.location: Akkala Sea, inside Lomei Labyrinth Island in the far northeast (4655, 3712, 0131)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing: Navigate the Lomei Labyrinth Island maze to reach the shrine, then walk to the altar for the free reward.
shrines.solution: There's no puzzle inside this one — the whole challenge is reaching it. The shrine sits in the heart of Lomei Labyrinth Island, the big square maze off the far-northeast coast of Akkala (an easy glide northeast from Ulri Mountain Skyview Tower, or north from the cliffs by Akkala Ancient Tech Lab). Drop in and wind through the corridors toward the center; a researcher has marked the path with acorns and tree nuts to follow, and the simplest finish is to reach the lit ladder that climbs up into the central chamber where the shrine stands. It's a Rauru's Blessing shrine, so once you're inside the Light of Blessing is free. Grab the treasure chest ahead for a Large Zonai Charge, then head up the stairs to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Mogisari Shrine
shrines.location: Lomei Sky Labyrinth, Akkala Sea Sky (4655, 3501, 1010)
shrines.oneLine: Solve the Lomei Sky Labyrinth maze; ascend and navigate the floating labyrinth to reach the shrine at its heart.
shrines.solution: This shrine sits at the heart of the Lomei Sky Labyrinth in the Akkala Sea Sky, so the maze IS the challenge: launch from Ulri Mountain Skyview Tower and glide in, then climb the floating labyrinth, activating the terminals around it; ride the strong updraft that appears up to the top terminal to drop the shrine entrance. Inside it's the 'Courage to Jump' trial, a low-gravity racecourse: glide down to the two parked cars, take the one on your RIGHT, and drive up the right-hand ramp (the left ramp dumps you into the chasm) across the lava pit, past the spikes/lasers and hostile Constructs. Keep to the left of the track. Near the end you'll smash through breakable stone blocks, then stop and bust the wall to reach a chest holding a Mighty Zonaite Shield. At the final gap, grab one of the Zonai Rockets nearby, attach it to your cart facing forward with Ultrahand, and blast across to the altar. Touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Gikaku Shrine
shrines.location: Sky Mine, Akkala Sea Sky (4506, 2165, 1155)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing 'The Sky Mine Crystal'. Fetch the crystal from Luminous Stone Island and return it to the Sky Mine shrine.
shrines.solution: This shrine stays sealed until you finish 'The Sky Mine Crystal' shrine quest, so do that first. Examine the dormant Gikaku Shrine on the Sky Mine island to start the quest, then examine the nearby terminal to power up the launch pads. Ride the launch pads upward island by island to climb the stack. Along the way you'll hit a spherical island with a hole in it: position the moving platform so it faces the sphere and shoot a box through the hole to solve that step, then continue up to the mineral-covered island where the green crystal sits on top. Grab the crystal with Ultrahand and simply drop it straight down onto the shrine island below (if it misses it respawns on its pedestal, so just try again), then climb down, carry it over, and set it on the shrine. Once Gikaku appears it's a Rauru's Blessing with no puzzle inside: open the chest for a Ruby, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Natak Shrine
shrines.location: Sokkala Sky Archipelago, Akkala Sky (3671, 1484, 1158)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing 'The Sokkala Sky Crystal'. Catapult into the sphere, set the spring, and launch the crystal to the shrine.
shrines.solution: This shrine is hidden behind 'The Sokkala Sky Crystal' shrine quest, so trigger it by examining the dormant Natak Shrine in the Sokkala Sky Archipelago; a green beam then points toward the missing crystal. Use the central launch pad to get airborne and glide into the hole in the side of the big spherical island. Inside, use Ultrahand to rotate the small model orb in the center so its opening faces the direction of the green beam, then position the spring-and-platform launch pad to face that opening. Set the green crystal on the launch pad and strike the spring with a melee hit: it fires both you and the crystal back across to the shrine's island in one shot (it may take a couple of tries to get the orb's angle right). Then carry the crystal over and place it on the shrine to reveal it. Inside Natak there's no puzzle: open the chest for an Enduring Elixir, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Kadaunar Shrine
shrines.location: South Eldin Sky Archipelago, Eldin Sky (1881, 1203, 1251)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing 'Water Makes a Way'. Use a water source to float and route the crystal to the shrine's foundation.
shrines.solution: Despite the 'Water Makes a Way' name this is a real puzzle shrine, not a free blessing. In the first room, grab the Hydrant with Ultrahand and carry it to the edge of the lava: its water cools the molten surface into solid Lava Slabs you can stand on. Pick up those hardened slabs and glue them together with Ultrahand into a bridge, holding the Hydrant up to keep making more as you cross. Use the bridge to build a ramp up to the side ledge where a chest holds a Strong Construct Bow. Fuse a rock to a weapon and smash the breakable wall ahead. In the next room the lava is a slow-moving stream, so work fast: keep a Hydrant aimed at it to harden fresh Lava Slabs, chain them with Ultrahand, and lay a bridge (about six slabs long) across to the far side. Cross to the back and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Mayam Shrine
shrines.location: Great Hyrule Forest Sky (0340, 2814, 1821)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Transport the green crystal across the floating islands to the shrine's pedestal to reveal it.
shrines.solution: This shrine is locked behind 'The North Hyrule Sky Crystal' shrine quest, so examine the dormant Mayam Shrine in the Great Hyrule Forest Sky to start it; a green beam points to the missing crystal on the North Hyrule Sky Archipelago just to the south. Waiting there is a Flux Construct I, a cube-monster boss: hit its glowing weak-spot block (the one with three eyes), and when it scatters into small cubes strike that weak cube with your strongest weapon to bring it down. It drops the green crystal. Use one of the floating platforms parked nearby, attach a couple of Fans and a Rocket with Ultrahand, set the crystal on it, and fly it back to the shrine's island, then grab the crystal and place it on the shrine to reveal it. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing with no puzzle, so the Light of Blessing is essentially free. Open the chest for a Magic Rod, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Simosiwak Shrine
shrines.location: Bravery Island, Great Hyrule Forest Sky (0163, 1972, 0759)
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Lights Out. Gear is stripped; grab the provided weapons and flame emitters to defeat three Constructs.
shrines.solution: Before this 'Proving Grounds: Lights Out' shrine will appear you have to complete Bravery Island's Dive Ceremony: talk to the Steward Construct at the island's base to begin, climb to the terminal at the top and examine it, then leap off the edge and dive through every green ring on the way down into the pool below. Clear them all and the shrine materializes. Inside, the Proving Grounds strip away all your own gear and the room is dark, so you fight with only what's provided. Grab the Long Stick and Old Wooden Shield on the left and the Light Shield over on the right, then find the Flame Emitter devices on the ground and fuse one to your weapon for fire damage (and light). Hunt down all three Constructs in the dim maze, swapping to a fresh Flame Emitter if your weapon breaks. With them defeated the gate opens; open the chest for a Bright Elixir, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Jinodok Shrine
shrines.location: South Hyrule Sky Archipelago (-1256, -1482, 1008)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing 'South Hyrule Sky Crystal'. Guide the crystal across the islands to the shrine to claim the reward.
shrines.solution: This shrine is hidden behind 'The South Hyrule Sky Crystal' shrine quest, so examine the dormant Jinodok Shrine on the South Hyrule Sky Archipelago to start it; a green beam points to the missing crystal on a separate island. Just to the north is a large apparatus you can grab with Ultrahand: reposition it so the rock path it carries bridges across to the crystal's island, then paraglide over and walk across to reach the crystal (there's also a hanging chest here you can swing up to for an Old Map). Carry the crystal back over, then use Ultrahand on the apparatus again to swing the path around so it bridges back toward the shrine side. Walk the crystal across and set it on the shrine to reveal it. Once it appears it's a Rauru's Blessing with no puzzle inside, so the Light of Blessing is essentially free. Open the chest for a Diamond, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Joku-usin Shrine
shrines.location: Thunderhead Isles, over Sarjon Woods (1077, -3349, 0786)
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Short Circuit. Use only in-shrine gear; grab the Shock Emitter and use electric attacks to beat the Constructs.
shrines.solution: This 'Proving Grounds: Short Circuit' shrine floats in a chamber over the Thunderhead Isles, and that whole storm-shrouded area stays pitch-dark until you've progressed the 'Secret of the Ring Ruins' quest (part of the Find the Fifth Sage chain), which clears the weather, so it's far easier afterward. To get inside, find the rails beneath the chamber, build a big letter 'T' from the nearby wooden platforms, attach a Fan facing backward, set it on the rails, ride it under the shrine, and use Ascend to pop up through the floor. The Proving Grounds strip all your gear. Grab a stick on the left, then cross to the back and fuse the Shock Emitter to it near the altar chamber. The trick is electricity: lure the Constructs onto the central metal-floor platform and swing your Shock-Emitter spear, the charge travels through the metal floor and zaps every Construct standing on it at once; use Ascend to reach any raised archer Constructs. Clear them, open the chest for an Electro Elixir, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Joku-u Shrine
shrines.location: Dragonhead Island, Thunderhead Isles (1378, -3342, 0429)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing inside the eye of Dragonhead Island. Reach it via the Thunderhead Isles for a free Light of Blessing.
shrines.solution: This is a Rauru's Blessing shrine, so there's no puzzle inside, the journey there is the whole challenge. It sits on Dragonhead Island within the Thunderhead Isles, right outside the doorway that leads to Mineru's Mask, and the entire area is wrapped in a blinding lightning storm with near-zero visibility (and a real risk of falling off) until you've progressed the 'Secret of the Ring Ruins' quest, which clears the weather, so it's far easier to reach after that. Make your way through the Thunderhead Isles down to Dragonhead Island (the lower island shaped like a dragon's head) and find the shrine just outside that doorway. Inside, the Light of Blessing is essentially free: open the treasure chest for a Diamond, then walk to the monk's altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Siyamotsus Shrine
shrines.location: South Lomei Castle, Gerudo Sky (-1795, -3296, 1011)
shrines.oneLine: Cross the dark castle interior; light torches and time the platforms in the gloom to navigate through to the shrine.
shrines.solution: Don't let the 'Unlit Blessing' name fool you, this is a puzzle shrine with a twist: as you climb the entrance stairs the far end seems to zoom away and turn into a vertical wall. At the start, pick a Fire Fruit from the trees by the entrance and grab the provided arrows, and open the central chest for a Mighty Construct Bow. Now look across at the wall for the two UNLIT pillars/torches set among lit ones, one on the lower right and one on the upper left. Take out your bow, fuse the Fire Fruit to an arrow, and shoot each unlit one to light it. Lighting both reveals a moving platform/trampoline beneath the chest behind you, step onto it to be launched up into the air, then glide across to the upper ledge and the altar. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Rakashog Shrine
shrines.location: East Gerudo Sky Archipelago, Gerudo Highlands Sky (-1713, -2120, 1149)
shrines.oneLine: A Reflective Device. Use Ultrahand to aim mirrors and bounce light beams onto hexagonal switches to open the doors.
shrines.solution: This is a light-reflection puzzle (A Reflective Device) that uses Ultrahand and Zonai mirrors. At the entrance, grab the mirror by the gate with Ultrahand and angle it so its reflected light beam hits the orange hexagonal switch above the door, opening it. In the next enclosure, lift the mirror into the rectangular hole in the wall and tilt it until the light reaches the hexagon on the far side. Then aim a mirror at the floor hexagon between the two ramps to start a moving platform, and ride it up. Upstairs, defeat the Constructs; one drops a Mirror Club. Use the mirrors (and the Mirror Club) to bounce light onto the external hexagon, which opens a small side chamber with a chest holding a Large Zonai Charge, then redirect the beam to the last hexagon behind the wall to open the monk's room. Touch the altar at the back to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Mayasiar Shrine
shrines.location: Starview Island, Gerudo Highlands Sky (-3547, -0320, 1976)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Rotate Starview Island's mirror wheels to light the top switch; the shrine then appears (no interior trial).
shrines.solution: Mayasiar is a Rauru's Blessing shrine, so there is no trial inside, but it stays hidden until you solve the mirror puzzle on Starview Island around it. Reach the spherical island by launching from the Gerudo Highlands Skyview Tower and gliding north. Start by pushing the central mechanism counter-clockwise until its light hits the northern mirror on the same level; that mirror kicks the beam up to a mirror on the ceiling, which bounces it to a mirror on a higher platform. Ride the updrafts that circle the central mechanism up to that platform and rotate its mirror so the light crosses to the next mirror, relaying the beam onward. Finish by turning the mechanisms so the light lands on the orange wall hexagon; once it turns green, the shrine rises. Inside, open the treasure chest for a Star Fragment Staff, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Taunhiy Shrine
shrines.location: Courage Island, Hyrule Ridge Sky (-2402, 0825, 0615)
shrines.oneLine: Combat Training: Archery. Use air vents and your glider to stay airborne, then hit the lone Construct three times with a bow.
shrines.solution: This shrine is hidden until you finish the Dive Ceremony on Courage Island, so do that first. At the top of the island, examine the sigil/hologram to start it, then dive and glide through every green ring that appears; clear them all and Taunhiy Shrine rises. (Return later and pay the Steward Construct a Zonai Charge to redo the dive within 35 seconds for the Glide Shirt armor.) Inside it's Combat Training: Archery, and only arrows count. Pick up the bow from the shrine floor. In the first phase a lone Construct floats above you; ride the air updraft to get airborne, press and hold ZR to aim in slow motion, and hit it with a few arrows. The second phase works the same way with three Constructs, riding the vent up and shooting each one from the air. Once they're down the way opens; the chest holds a Strong Construct Bow, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Ganos Shrine
shrines.location: Tabantha Sky Archipelago (-3370, 0467, 1695)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing 'Tabantha Sky Crystal'. Carry the crystal across the Tabantha islands to the shrine pedestal.
shrines.solution: Ganos stays sealed until you complete its shrine quest, The Tabantha Sky Crystal, so start that first by examining the shrine pedestal. Follow the green beam of light down to the island below, where a Flux Construct III guards the green crystal. You can defeat it by hitting its glowing block, but you don't have to; just grab the crystal off it with Ultrahand and shake it free. Then build a flyer from the Zonai fans and batteries on the island (one easy design is fans on each side of a grate, facing down, with a steering stick in the middle), attach the green crystal, and pilot it up to the shrine island. Offer the crystal to the shrine to complete the quest and open it. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing with no puzzle. Open the chest for a Diamond, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Ga-ahisas Shrine
shrines.location: Lightcast Island, Tabantha Frontier Sky (-3596, 0961, 1699)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Drain the water, then redirect mirror light through the cave to open the way to the shrine.
shrines.solution: Ga-ahisas is a Rauru's Blessing shrine that stays hidden until you solve the Lightcast Island puzzle above it. First, examine the green terminal at the northern end of the island to drain the water, then clear the boulders blocking the cave mouth (a Bomb Flower works) and head inside. The cave is a mirror-relay puzzle: use Ultrahand to grab the mirror by the entrance and set it so the outside light shines on it, then carry the second mirror to the far platform (you can drop it in the water, climb up, and pull it out) and angle it so the beam relays through the tunnel. Defeat the Construct at the end, grab the light-reflecting shield it drops, and shine light onto the orange hexagon; this opens the doorway and reveals carved steps leading down to the shrine. Inside there is no trial. Open the chest for a Star Fragment, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Ijo-o Shrine
shrines.location: West Hebra Sky Archipelago (-3860, 2682, 0702)
shrines.oneLine: More Than Defense. Use a shield and Recall to reflect or ride incoming objects and cross the gaps to the exit.
shrines.solution: Ijo-o (More Than Defense) is about using shields creatively, not Recall. In the first room, defeat the Soldier Construct carrying a flame-emitter shield and take it; hold ZL to spray fire and melt the wall of ice blocking the way forward. In the next room, defeat the next Construct and grab its stone-slab shield, then use the flame shield to melt the ice on the right to expose a chest holding 5 Arrows. Hold up the stone-slab shield to block the jet of flames in the corridor and pass through. In the final room, beat the last Construct, take a shield from the rack, and use Fuse to stick a Zonai rocket onto it. Equip the rocket shield, press ZL to blast straight up, then pull your paraglider and glide over to the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Mayaumekis Shrine
shrines.location: Rising Island Chain, Hebra Sky (-2948, 3050, 0896)
shrines.oneLine: Downward Force. Bounce off the roofs of the flying ships and use dropped weight to launch yourself up to the terminals.
shrines.solution: Mayaumekis (Downward Force) is a vertical platforming puzzle using the bouncy flying ships with springy sails. At the locked gate, shoot the switch on the far side through the bars with an arrow to open it, climb the stairs, and defeat the Soldier Construct II at the top. Now bounce upward: land on a ship's sail to launch high, pull your paraglider at the peak, and glide to the next moving ship, repeating to climb the chamber. There is a hidden chest atop a pillar in a corner; bounce off a sail and glide to it for 10 Arrows. For the final switch, you'll see one in a barred cage near the ceiling; bounce up off a sail and, as you rise past the bars, draw your bow to trigger slow-motion and shoot the switch. With the way open, bounce up through the center of the barred structure on the last ship and glide down to the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Kahatanaum Shrine
shrines.location: Rising Island Chain, Hebra Sky (-3295, 3430, 1347)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Navigate the Rising Island Chain's ascending platforms to reach the shrine for a free blessing.
shrines.solution: Kahatanaum is a Rauru's Blessing shrine, so the only real challenge is reaching it; come dressed for the bitter Hebra cold (the Snowquill set from Rito Village helps) for the climb. It sits on the Rising Island Chain northwest of the Wind Temple, the same ascent you make during the Tulin of Rito Village main quest, so glide between the rising islands and use Ascend to pop up through ledges (Tulin's gust ability makes this far easier) until you reach the shrine entrance. Inside there is no puzzle, so the Light of Blessing is essentially free. Open the chest in front of you for a Large Zonai Charge, then walk to the monk's altar at the back and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Taninoud Shrine
shrines.location: East Hebra Sky Archipelago (-1801, 3406, 0949)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing 'The East Hebra Sky Crystal'. Build a fan flyer to bring the crystal back to the shrine, then claim it.
shrines.solution: Taninoud stays sealed until you finish its shrine quest, The East Hebra Sky Crystal, so start that first by examining the shrine base. Use the moving platform near the shrine to launch toward the larger sky island, then glide underneath a floating platform to a ledge with wooden vines; cut them with a sharp weapon to open the cavern, head in past the Zonai devices, and lift the green Sky Crystal off its pedestal with Ultrahand. Now build a fan flyer: attach the crystal to a floating platform, then place a fan on each side (facing down) and add a battery or two for power so it stays balanced. Ride it back and offer the crystal to the shrine to complete the quest. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing with no puzzle; open the chest for a Topaz, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Tenbez Shrine
shrines.location: North Lomei Castle, Hebra Sky (-0966, 3540, 1011)
shrines.oneLine: Gravity and Velocity. Use ramps and falling momentum to roll balls and carts onto distant switches and targets.
shrines.solution: Tenbez (Gravity and Velocity) is a timing puzzle built around a gravity-changing device, not Ultrahand building. In the first room, a ball sits on a moving platform; hit the grey-and-orange gravity switch in the center so it turns green and the ball flies up to strike the orange target high on the wall, opening the next gate. Before moving on, grab the hidden chest: with the gravity lowered, ride the right-hand catapult/platform up toward the wall and reach the alcove with the chest for a Large Zonai Charge. In the second room, leave the gravity changed and watch the ball float up and over toward the walled enclosure across the way; the instant it is directly above the enclosure, hit the switch to restore normal gravity so the ball drops straight down onto the target inside, opening the final door. Step through and touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Jirutagumac Shrine
shrines.location: Near Wellspring Island, Lanayru Sky (2916, 0534, 0965)
shrines.oneLine: A Flying Device. Build a fan-powered flyer with Ultrahand and pilot it across the gaps to the shrine's exit.
shrines.solution: This is the "A Flying Device" puzzle, so you're meant to build a flyer rather than climb. From the entry room, grab a Wing Zonai device with Ultrahand, set it on the incline ahead, and ride it down to the lower area; you can also grab a wheeled cart and attach the Wing to it for the next slope. Before the finale, get the chest the easy way: lay the Wing across the small gap beneath the chest's ledge to make a bridge, stand on it, then use Ascend to pop straight up to the chest, which holds a Large Zonaite. For the exit, carry a Wing to the start of the runway that points at the altar and attach a Fan to its back (held parts glow yellow when they'll stick), with the Fan blasting away from the goal so its thrust pushes the Wing toward the altar. Climb onto the Wing, switch the Fan on, and it rockets down the runway into the air; lean to steer and glide onto the far platform. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Igoshon Shrine
shrines.location: Water Temple path, Lanayru Sky (3481, 0664, 1325)
shrines.oneLine: Orbs of Water. Ride water orbs and use Recall and Ultrahand to ferry yourself across the gaps to the goal.
shrines.solution: This "Orbs of Water" shrine is about riding floating water orbs across the gaps. At the start, step into an orb rising from its pedestal and let it carry you up, then dash out (press X) and glide to the ledge. Before moving on, grab the chest: a chest keeps dropping in midair nearby, so use Ultrahand to take the nearby water orb and hold it under the falling chest so the chest gets caught inside it, then carry it to solid ground and open it for a Large Zonai Charge. Next, a water orb falls onto the platform's edge, so use Recall on it to reverse its motion and ride it back upward. For the long horizontal gap, just stand in the path of a sideways-traveling orb and let it ferry you across. At the final gap there's a stone block on a metal rail and a loose metal panel: use Ultrahand to slide the block into the path of the water orbs being fired from the wall, then attach the metal panel on top of the block at an angle so it acts as a ramp that launches the incoming orb up toward the altar. Ride that orb across and glide off near the goal. Touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Sihajog Shrine
shrines.location: Valor Island, Lanayru Sky (4546, -0846, 1135)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Complete Valor Island's Dive Ceremony (fall through the green rings) to make the shrine appear.
shrines.solution: This shrine stays hidden until you finish Valor Island's Dive Ceremony, so do that first. At the base of Valor Island, find the Steward Construct and tell it you're ready for the Dive Ceremony; it transports you to the island's summit. Jump from the cliff edge and, as you free-fall, a series of green rings appears in the air — pass through every one of them to unlock the shrine (this run is untimed, so take your time and steer carefully). Clear them all and Sihajog Shrine rises into view. (If you later redo the ceremony and pass through all the rings in under 35 seconds, you'll also be awarded the Glide Mask, but that's optional.) Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine, so there's no puzzle. Open the treasure chest for a Diamond, then walk to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Mayanas Shrine
shrines.location: South Lanayru Sky Archipelago, near Valor Island (4613, -0947, 1790)
shrines.oneLine: The Ice Guides You. Make ice plates on water with Ice Fruit or Frost Emitters to build sliding ramps onto the targets.
shrines.solution: This "The Ice Guides You" shrine is about making ice plates float on the water and sliding them into targets. Create ice by throwing an Ice Fruit into the water (or by aiming a Frost Emitter at it), which freezes a flat plate you can grab with Ultrahand. In the first room, grab an ice plate, slide it down the ramp ahead, and time it so it strikes the moving target; expect a few tries. The second room past the altar is trickier and guards the chest: use Ultrahand to join the nearby square metal plates into one long panel, then prop it at a slant near the right wall of the spiky platform so it acts as a guide. Make another ice plate and drop it down the ramp on the right so it hits your metal guide and gets steered left into the target sitting below the spikes. Striking that target opens the way to a chest holding a Mighty Zonaite Shield. With the targets struck, go to the monk's altar and touch it to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Josiu Shrine
shrines.location: North Necluda Sky Archipelago (1760, -1208, 0924)
shrines.oneLine: Rauru's Blessing. Ferry the green crystal across the Necluda sky islands to the shrine base to reveal it.
shrines.solution: This shrine is buried until you finish the shrine quest "The North Necluda Sky Crystal," so do that first. Examining the shrine site starts the quest; you'll see a green crystal stranded on a separate floating island and a rotatable curved-rail apparatus between the islands. Use Ultrahand to rotate the apparatus clockwise until one end connects to the moving island, hop onto the moving island, then use Recall on the apparatus so it swings back and the moving island links up with the island holding the crystal. Step across, pick up the green crystal with Ultrahand, set it on the moving island, glide back, and rotate the apparatus once more to bridge the moving island back to you. Finally carry the crystal to the Josiu Shrine base to make the shrine appear. Inside it's a Rauru's Blessing shrine with no puzzle. Open the treasure chest for a Large Zonai Charge, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Yansamin Shrine
shrines.location: Zonaite Forge Island, Necluda Sky (2353, -1783, 1475)
shrines.oneLine: Proving Grounds: Low Gravity. In reduced gravity with only provided gear, defeat the Constructs to clear the trial.
shrines.solution: This is "Proving Grounds: Low Gravity," so all your own gear is taken away and you fight in reduced gravity using only the weapons the shrine provides. Start by taking the weapons from the rack near the entrance, then deal with the first lone Construct; a long polearm lets you poke it from a safe distance. Loot what enemies drop and keep arming yourself as you climb. To move up between the floating platforms you can use Ascend where there's a platform overhead, or fuse a Rocket from the supplies onto a shield and fire it to rocket upward. Work your way through every Construct spread across the upper levels — closing fast on archers, and knocking weapons away from the heavier sword Constructs — and use a shield to break line of sight when more than one comes at you so you can fight them a few at a time. With every Construct down, head into the statue room where a chest holds a Large Zonaite, then touch the monk's altar to claim your Light of Blessing.

shrines.name: Ukoojisi Shrine
shrines.location: West Necluda Sky Archipelago (1468, -2168, 0585)
