COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Carry one and it AUTO-REVIVES Link on death (consumes the fairy). Restores 5 hearts if used raw; cook with a monster part for a Fairy Tonic (no stat effect, just healing). Sneak-catch or grab while gliding - it flees once it notices you.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Floats near circular ponds on the Sky Islands and in some surface caves; easiest to farm from Great Fairy Fountains' sky-island ponds.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Bokoblin Horn
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped by Bokoblins everywhere across Hyrule's surface, Sky, and Depths. The most common monster part.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +0:40 to elixir duration. No effect of its own — pair with a critter to set the elixir's effect.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Bokoblin Fang
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped by Blue and Black Bokoblins (tougher color tiers). Common once you reach mid-game enemy camps.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +1:50 to elixir duration — over twice a horn's. A reliable mid-tier duration part.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Bokoblin Guts
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped by Silver Bokoblins (and rarely Black). Late-game, high-tier enemies.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +2:40 to elixir duration and a 30% critical-cook chance. A long-duration, high-value part.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Moblin Horn
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped by Moblins on the surface, in the Sky, and in the Depths.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +0:40 to elixir duration. Functions like a Bokoblin Horn — short-duration base part.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Moblin Fang
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped by Blue and Black Moblins. Found at larger monster camps.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +1:50 to elixir duration — a solid mid-tier choice.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Moblin Guts
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped by Silver Moblins (rarely Black). Late-game enemies.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +2:40 to elixir duration plus a 30% crit-cook chance. High-tier, long-lasting.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Lizalfos Horn
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped by Lizalfos in wetlands, deserts, the Sky, and the Depths.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +0:40 to elixir duration. A cheap, very common base part.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Lizalfos Talon
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped by Blue, Black, and elemental Lizalfos.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +1:50 to elixir duration. The Lizalfos mid-tier duration part.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Lizalfos Tail
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped by Lizalfos (rare ~25% drop), and from tougher color tiers. Farm in the Lanayru Wetlands.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +2:40 to elixir duration and a 30% critical-cook chance — high-tier, easy to farm.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Lynel Saber Horn
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped by Lynels (Coliseum, Hebra, the Depths). Every Lynel drops one when defeated.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +0:40 to elixir duration, but worth a lot when sold (40 rupees; more for higher-maned tiers).

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Lynel Mace Horn
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped by Lynels alongside the Saber Horn. Both horn types drop from a single Lynel kill.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +0:40 to elixir duration. Like the Saber Horn, it's better sold (40 rupees) than cooked.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Lynel Guts
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped only by Lynels — one of the rarest, most valuable monster parts in the game.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +2:40 to elixir duration plus a 30% crit-cook chance, and sells for 200 rupees. Best when sold, not cooked.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Hinox Tooth
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped by Hinox (surface, Sky, and Depths). Knock one out and loot its body.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +1:50 to elixir duration — a strong mid-tier part.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Hinox Guts
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped by tougher Hinox (Blue, Black). One of the better long-duration parts.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +2:40 to elixir duration and a 30% crit-cook chance. High value, long-lasting.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Keese Eyeball
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped by Keese (and elemental Keese — Fire/Ice/Electric). Abundant in caves and at night.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +1:20 to elixir duration. Cheap and plentiful — good filler when you don't need long duration.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Chuchu Jelly
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped by Chuchus everywhere. The most abundant monster ingredient in the game.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +0:40 to elixir duration. Endless supply makes it the go-to throwaway duration part.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Aerocuda Eyeball
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Dropped by Aerocudas — the flying eye-creatures common in the Sky and over surface ruins.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Adds +1:20 to elixir duration. A common low-tier part, slightly better duration than a horn.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Dinraal's Scale
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Shoot Dinraal's body (torso/tail). Dinraal, the fire dragon, circles the northern sky — rises from the East Akkala Plains Chasm / Eldin region and loops the Eldin Mountains, Akkala, and Tabantha sky. One part drops per real-world ~10 min, then its body glows again.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Guarantees a Critical Success and adds about 1:30 to the dish's effect duration. (Carries no effect of its own — it boosts whatever critter you cook it with. On weapon fusion it also adds a minor fire effect.)

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Shard of Dinraal's Horn
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Shoot the tip of Dinraal's horn — the rarest, hardest part to hit. Dinraal rises from Eldin/Akkala and flies the northern sky. One part per real-world ~10 min.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Guarantees a Critical Success and maxes the dish to 30:00 — the longest cooking duration in the game. One horn shard takes any timed dish to ~30 minutes.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Naydra's Scale
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Shoot Naydra's body (torso/tail). Naydra, the ice dragon, rises from the Naydra Snowfield Chasm by the Mount Lanayru Skyview Tower and circles the Lanayru / eastern sky. One part per real-world ~10 min.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Guarantees a Critical Success and adds about 1:30 to the dish's effect duration. (No effect of its own. On weapon fusion it also adds a minor ice effect.)

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Shard of Naydra's Horn
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Shoot the tip of Naydra's horn (rare drop). Naydra flies the Lanayru / Mount Lanayru / eastern sky. One part per real-world ~10 min.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Guarantees a Critical Success and maxes the dish to 30:00 — the maximum cooking duration. One horn shard takes any timed dish to ~30 minutes.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Farosh's Scale
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Shoot Farosh's body (torso/tail). Farosh, the lightning dragon, rises from the East Gerudo Chasm and circles the southwest — Gerudo Canyon, Faron Grasslands, and toward Lake Hylia. One part per real-world ~10 min.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Guarantees a Critical Success and adds about 1:30 to the dish's effect duration. (No effect of its own. On weapon fusion it also adds a minor shock effect.)

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Shard of Farosh's Horn
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Shoot the tip of Farosh's horn (rare drop). Farosh flies the Gerudo / Faron / Lake Hylia southwestern sky. One part per real-world ~10 min.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Guarantees a Critical Success and maxes the dish to 30:00 — the maximum cooking duration. One horn shard takes any timed dish to ~30 minutes.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Light Dragon's Scale
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Shoot the Light Dragon's body (torso/tail). The Light Dragon flies one wide fixed loop around all of Hyrule and is the only dragon that never enters the Depths/chasms; reach it via the sky islands or a Skyview Tower launch. One part per real-world ~10 min.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Guarantees a Critical Success and adds about 1:30 to the dish's effect duration. (No effect of its own. On weapon fusion Light Dragon parts add a healing/recovery effect instead of an element.)

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Shard of Light Dragon's Horn
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Shoot the tip of the Light Dragon's horn (rare drop). The Light Dragon flies a continuous loop across the whole map. One part per real-world ~10 min.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: Guarantees a Critical Success and maxes the dish to 30:00 — the maximum cooking duration. One horn shard takes any timed dish to ~30 minutes.

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Star Fragment
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Catch or pick up where a shooting star lands at night; one falls nightly and vanishes by ~5 AM.
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: crit-chance

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Monster Extract
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Buy for 50 rupees at the Tarrey Town General Store (also stocked by some monster-camp/merchant pots).

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Dragon Horn Shard
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Shoot the horn tip of Dinraal, Naydra, Farosh, or the Light Dragon (one horn shard per dragon per Blood Moon).
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: crit

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Dragon Scale
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Strike a dragon's body/tail (Dinraal/Naydra/Farosh/Light Dragon) to knock off a scale (~10-min part cooldown).
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: crit

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Dragon Claw
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Hit a dragon's feet/claws (Dinraal/Naydra/Farosh/Light Dragon; ~10-min part cooldown).
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: crit

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Dragon Fang
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Hit a dragon's mouth/jaw (Dinraal/Naydra/Farosh/Light Dragon; ~10-min part cooldown).
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: crit

COOK_INGREDIENTS.name: Golden Apple
COOK_INGREDIENTS.where: Rare golden apples on apple trees around Hyrule (e.g. the orchard by Sonapan Shrine on Satori Mountain, Hyrule Field, Mido Swamp near the Lost Woods).
COOK_INGREDIENTS.bonus: crit

systems.name: The three layers: Surface, Sky, Depths
systems.what: Hyrule has three stacked worlds. Sky has floating islands and Skyview Towers. The Depths sit beneath the Surface, dark and full of Gloom.

systems.name: The Depths and Gloom
systems.what: The Depths are pitch-black. Toss Brightbloom Seeds or hit Lightroots to light them. Gloom damages and locks hearts until you reach light or eat a sundelion dish.

systems.name: Lightroots mirror shrines
systems.what: Activating a Lightroot lights a chunk of the Depths and marks the map. All 120 Lightroots sit under the 120 Surface shrines, names spelled backwards.

systems.name: Poes and Bargainer Statues
systems.what: Poes are blue flames found in the Depths; they are currency. Spend them at the seven Bargainer Statues to buy Gloom-resistant gear and items.

systems.name: Shrines and Light of Blessing
systems.what: There are 152 shrines across Surface and Sky. Each gives a Light of Blessing. Trade 4 at any Goddess Statue for one heart or one stamina vessel.

systems.name: Heart and stamina swap
systems.what: At the Horned Statue in the bunker beneath Lookout Landing you can rebalance hearts and stamina. It costs rupees: sell an essence for 100, buy the other back for 120.

systems.name: Battery and Energy Cells
systems.what: Energy Cells power Zonai devices. Mine Zonaite in the Depths, trade 3 to a Forge Construct for a Crystallized Charge, then add cells at a Crystal Refinery.

systems.name: Skyview Towers
systems.what: 15 Skyview Towers reveal the regional map and launch you skyward. Open the shutters by solving each tower's small puzzle, then get fired into the Sky.

systems.name: Zonai devices and Autobuild
systems.what: Zonai devices (fans, wheels, rockets) attach with Ultrahand. Autobuild rebuilds saved or recent creations from materials, spending Zonaite for missing parts.

systems.name: Great Fairies
systems.what: Four Great Fairies (Tera, Cotera, Kaysa, Mija) upgrade armor. Unlock each by reuniting the Stable Trotters troupe, led by conductor Mastro, near a stable.

systems.name: Korok seeds and Hestu
systems.what: Find Korok seeds (900 total) and give them to Hestu to expand your weapon, bow, and shield pouches. Many hide behind carry-a-Korok-to-its-friend puzzles.

systems.name: Dragon's Tears and geoglyphs
systems.what: Visit the geoglyphs drawn across the Surface to unlock the Dragon's Tears memories, telling the Imprisoning War story out of order.

systems.name: Sage abilities and Sage's Will
systems.what: The five sages grant abilities you trigger by their avatar (Tulin gusts, Yunobo charges, etc.). Offer 4 Sage's Wills at a Goddess Statue to boost one sage (about 1.3x).

systems.name: The four temples and the fifth sage
systems.what: Wind (Rito/Tulin), Fire (Goron/Yunobo), Water (Zora/Sidon), Lightning (Gerudo/Riju). Mineru, the fifth sage, comes from the Spirit Temple.

upgrades: Hearts and stamina: collect Lights of Blessing from shrines (152 total) and trade 4 at a Goddess Statue for one heart container or one stamina vessel.
Heart/stamina swap: at the Horned Statue in the Lookout Landing bunker you can rebalance later. It is not free; each swap nets about 20 rupees (sell 100, rebuy 120).
Battery (Energy Cells): mine Zonaite in the Depths, trade 3 Zonaite per Crystallized Charge at a Forge Construct, then spend 100 charges per cell at a Crystal Refinery.
Pouch slots: hand Korok seeds to Hestu to add weapon, bow, and shield inventory slots; costs climb as you expand.
Armor: upgrade gear at any of the four Great Fairy fountains once unlocked, spending materials and rupees per level.
Sage power: collect Sage's Wills (20 exist; some guarded by Gleeoks) and offer 4 at a Goddess Statue to strengthen a chosen sage's ability about 1.3x.

fairies.name: Tera
fairies.location: Near Woodland Stable in Eldin, by Pico Pond. You meet conductor Mastro and the Stable Trotters here; awaken Tera first to unlock the others.
fairies.cost: Free to unlock (reunite the Stable Trotters); armor upgrades then cost materials plus rupees

fairies.name: Cotera
fairies.location: Near Dueling Peaks Stable, south of Kakariko Village on the east side of Dueling Peaks.
fairies.cost: Free to unlock (reunite the next Stable Trotters musician); upgrades cost materials plus rupees

fairies.name: Kaysa
fairies.location: Near Outskirt Stable, southwest edge of Hyrule Field, west of the Coliseum Ruins.
fairies.cost: Free to unlock (reunite the Stable Trotters); upgrades cost materials plus rupees

fairies.name: Mija
fairies.location: Near Snowfield Stable in the South Tabantha Snowfield (cold region; pack warm gear).
fairies.cost: Free to unlock (final Stable Trotters performance); upgrades cost materials plus rupees

rupees.method: Sell gems (diamonds, sapphires, rubies) mined from ore deposits
rupees.detail: Smash the black sparkly ore deposits in caves and especially the Depths (a Rock-fused hammer or any heavy weapon breaks them) and sell the gems: Diamond ~500, Sapphire ~260, Ruby ~110, Topaz ~80, Amber ~30 each, making this one of the most lucrative beginner earners.

rupees.method: Sell gems at Goron City (Ramella) for the best price
rupees.detail: Ramella's shop in Goron City buys gems for slightly more than anyone else (selling 10 Diamonds at once nets ~5,500 rupees, ~550 each), so stockpile gems and offload them there rather than at the first merchant you pass.

rupees.method: Hunt Stone/Battle Taluses for bulk gems and diamonds
rupees.detail: Stone and Battle Taluses (and the elemental Igneo/Frost variants) drop a pile of common ore plus a chance at gems — including Diamonds (~500) — when you break the ore on their body; they respawn every Blood Moon, so a circuit of known ones is repeatable income.

rupees.method: Cook and sell Meat Skewers (one of the best food-flips in the game)
rupees.detail: Five Raw Gourmet Meat cooked into one Meat Skewer sells for ~315 rupees; hunt large animals or the Honeyvore Bear east of the Thyphlo Ruins Skyview Tower (the Bokoblin Mask lets you walk up unnoticed) for a near-endless, early-and-late-game money loop.

rupees.method: Sell high-value cooked dishes and elixirs in bulk
rupees.detail: Cooking with 5 ingredients sells for far more than the raw parts, and dumping spare monster parts plus a Monster Extract into the pot makes elixirs worth a few dozen rupees and up each, turning junk drops into rupees.

rupees.method: Sell extra monster parts and materials you don't need
rupees.detail: Bokoblin horns, fangs, guts and similar drops sell for a few to a couple dozen rupees each, so dumping a full inventory of leftover monster parts on a merchant is a fast, low-effort early cash injection (cheaper than gems but plentiful).

rupees.method: Feed Luminous Stones to the Dondons to get rare gems
rupees.detail: At the Dondons north of Lakeside Stable in Bronas Forest (south Faron, by Lake Floria), feed each one up to ~5 Luminous Stones, wait ~15 minutes, and collect what they excrete (Flint, Opal, Sapphire, Ruby, and even Diamonds) for far more value than selling the raw stones — more stones at once raises the odds of high-value gems.

Blupees are glowing blue rabbit-like creatures (often near caves, mostly at night); hit one to make it drop rupees — repeated melee hits yield more than a single arrow, so chase it down before it flees for easy no-cost cash while exploring.

rupees.method: Shoot dragons for parts and farm Bubbul Gems for trade goods
rupees.detail: Arrowing the scales/claws/fangs/horns off the roaming dragons (Dinraal, Farosh, Naydra, the Light Dragon) yields high-value parts, and Bubbul Gems from cave Bubbulfrogs trade to Koltin (at night) for monster masks and rare monster parts you can sell or use rather than for direct rupees.

rupees.method: Repeatable minigames and clearing enemy camps
rupees.detail: Small payouts add up: minigames and side activities (e.g. shooting/racing challenges and toy hunts) and looting/clearing enemy camps for sellable drops are repeatable, and enemy camps respawn after each Blood Moon.

farming.where: Any wide-open area at night; warp between two spots: launch from Gerudo Canyon Skyview Tower, then warp to a sky island like Mayam Shrine.
farming.tip: Falls only at night and vanishes at 5:00 AM; you can collect only ONE per night. Launch from a tower or dive from a sky island, glide to the light trail and grab it mid-air. Then warp to a DIFFERENT location and rest to night again — staying in the same spot won't respawn one.

farming.where: The Depths — mine Zonaite Deposits everywhere; the Great Abandoned Central Mine (reached via the Great Plateau North Chasm) is a dense, easy early spot.
farming.tip: Break deposits with a hammer/Cobble Crusher; deposits respawn each Blood Moon. Large Zonaite drops from mini-bosses (Frox especially) and bigger deposits. Bring gloom protection (Sundelion dishes / armor). Construct enemies also drop Zonaite + Zonai Charges; a Forge Construct in any Abandoned Mine trades 1 Zonai Charge per 2 Zonaite (and 1 Large Charge per Large Zonaite).

farming.where: Floating Coliseum in the Depths — five Lynels back-to-back (ending in Silver Lynels); or a single red-maned Lynel in West Hyrule Plains for an easy kill.
farming.tip: Reach the Coliseum via the Great Plateau North Chasm for the highest-density Lynel run. Guts drop reliably from Silver Lynels (guaranteed), often from White, and rarely from Blue — Red Lynels never drop them. Drop a Puffshroom for a smokescreen, then hit a stunned Lynel freely. All Lynels respawn on the Blood Moon.

farming.where: Bokoblin camps across Hyrule Field, Lanayru, and late-game Eldin; the Depths have higher-tier variants.
farming.tip: Guts ONLY drop from Blue, Black, Silver, or Stalkoblin-tier Bokoblins — red ones never drop guts. Farm black/silver camps for consistent guts. Everything respawns each Blood Moon.

farming.where: Encampments around Hyrule; e.g. Deep Akkala near Kimayat Shrine has a camp next to healing hot springs.
farming.tip: Kill Blue (and higher) Moblins for guts; red Moblins don't reliably drop them. Nearby hot springs let you heal between fights. Respawns on the Blood Moon.

farming.where: Lanayru Wetlands and Lanayru Great Spring for common Lizalfos; Black Lizalfos in Gerudo Desert / East Necluda; Fire-Breath Lizalfos in Eldin.
farming.tip: Elemental Lizalfos tails (Fire/Ice/Electric-Breath) are key fuse and armor-upgrade mats. Higher color tiers = better drops; Blood Moon respawn.

farming.where: Hyrule caves and the Gerudo Desert Depths — Black Horriblins spawn here.
farming.tip: Black Horriblin horns are strong fuse material. Horriblins cling to cave ceilings — knock them down with arrows first. Blood Moon respawn.

farming.where: Fixed Gleeok spots across the surface (Eldin, Lake Hylia, Gerudo Highlands, Hebra, Hyrule Field, Faron, Hyrule Ridge) plus the Gleeok Den in the Depths.
farming.tip: Every Gleeok drops Wings + Guts; the element (Fire/Frost/Thunder Horns) matches the Gleeok type. King Gleeoks can drop ANY of the three horn types, so a King is the most flexible farm. Keese-eyeball arrows home in on the heads; Gleeoks respawn each Blood Moon.

farming.item: Elemental critters (Summerwing Butterfly, Cool/Warm Safflina, Sunset Firefly, etc.)
farming.where: Summerwing Butterflies: Eldin Canyon, Gerudo Highlands, near Tabantha/Kara Kara areas. Warm Safflina: Eldin & Gerudo Desert. Cool Safflina: Hebra/Tabantha cliffsides & Gerudo Summit.
farming.tip: These cook into elixirs and feed armor upgrades (heat/cold resistance sets). Approach insects slowly or they flee — sneak (Stealth set) or freeze the area. They regrow after a few in-game days.

farming.item: Gems via Rare Ore Deposits (Diamond, Ruby, Sapphire, Topaz)
farming.where: Rare (gold-veined) Ore Deposits in the Depths and on cliffs; Eldin/Death Mountain caves (Ruby), Tabantha/Hebra caves (Sapphire), Gerudo Desert caves (Topaz). Diamonds come from Rare Deposits anywhere.
farming.tip: Plain black deposits give common ore + element gems; the gold-flecked RARE deposits are the ones that can drop Diamonds. Use a Cobble Crusher / hammer-fused weapon to break them fast. Ore deposits respawn each Blood Moon. Elemental Taluses (Igneo→Ruby, Frost→Sapphire) also drop gems.

farming.where: Flies along the north of Hyrule, EAST-to-WEST: emerges from the East Akkala Plains Chasm (northeast) and travels the coast to the Drenan Highlands Chasm (northwest, near Tabantha/Eldin).
farming.tip: Shoot a Scale (body), Claw (foot), Fang (mouth), or Horn (head) with arrows — Horn is rarest/most valuable. Each part you shoot off respawns after ~10 minutes of REAL-time gameplay (no Blood Moon needed), so you can ride/board the dragon and keep harvesting. Touching it grants a Fire aura. Wait on a sky island or chasm along its path.

farming.where: Loops around the Necluda/Mount Lanayru area between the Naydra Snowfield Chasm and the East Hill Chasm (above Kakariko).
farming.tip: The Mount Lanayru Skyview Tower is a reliable launch point to intercept Naydra as it climbs; the Dueling Peaks crossing is another easy intercept (no cold gear needed). Shoot the part you need — each respawns after ~10 minutes of real-time play, so you can keep harvesting from the same dragon.

farming.where: Travels from the East Gerudo Chasm across Gerudo and Lake Hylia (passing near the Gerudo Canyon Skyview Tower) before diving into the Hills of Baumer Chasm.
farming.tip: Launch from Gerudo Canyon Skyview Tower and glide onto its path; shoot the part you want. Parts respawn after ~10 minutes of real-time gameplay. Farosh discharges electricity — don't ride it or touch it with a metal weapon equipped.

farming.where: Circles very high over all of Hyrule's sky islands on a fixed loop and never enters the Depths.
farming.tip: Skydive onto its back, walk along it, and shoot/pull off parts (its scales/claws/fangs/horns/spikes are top-tier). Like the other dragons, each part respawns after ~10 minutes of real-time play. Note: there is no story event that makes it fly lower — it stays at sky-island altitude, so reach it by diving from a sky island above its path.

farming.where: Giant: caves with Brightbloom clusters (e.g. near Woodland Stable) yield several Giants per trip. Regular: the large cave below the Great Sky Island starting area and most surface caves.
farming.tip: Throw regular seeds to light the Depths; Giant seeds light a huge radius and permanently reveal areas/Lightroots. Set Sensor+ to a seed to track nearby ones. Both regrow after a few in-game days.

farming.where: Sundelion: sunny spots on the sky islands (e.g. South Eldin Sky Archipelago, North Necluda Sky Archipelago). Big Hearty Radish: North Necluda Sky islands, plus sky-island and surface spots.
farming.tip: Sundelion is THE gloom-recovery ingredient (cooks the dish that heals gloom-damaged hearts) — essential before deep Depths runs. Big Hearty Radish gives the most bonus (full-heart) yellow hearts. Both regrow after a few in-game days.

tips: DO hoard Poes and spend them at the Bargainer Statues in the Depths — those shops take Poes, not rupees, for their exclusive Depths gear (the Dark Set / Hero of the Depths set), lanterns, and materials.
DON'T sell monster, Lynel, Gleeok, or dragon parts blindly — many are required armor-upgrade materials at the Great Fairy fountains, so check the recipe before cashing them in.
DO cook before you sell — dishes fetch noticeably more rupees than the raw ingredients and only cost a few seconds at a pot.
DON'T sell Star Fragments — they're rare and needed to upgrade several armor sets (and to fuse for light), so they're worth far more kept than the rupees you'd get.
DON'T sell Zonaite — convert it into Zonai/Crystallized Charges (3 Zonaite makes 1 Crystallized Charge) to upgrade your battery, which you'll need for vehicles and combat.

DO treat the Hylian Shield as reusable — it's recoverable from Hyrule Castle, rebuyable from Cece in Hateno for 3,000 rupees, or repairable by feeding it to a Rock Octorok, so use it instead of hoarding it.
DO buy arrows in bulk when you have spare rupees (e.g. from the shop at Lookout Landing) so you're never caught without ammo.

DON'T expect to pay a rupee fee to wake the Great Fairies — you unlock each fountain by reuniting the Stable Trotters music troupe and escorting the musicians to play at the fountain, not by spending money.

RUNES.name: Ultrahand
RUNES.from: Ukouh Shrine, your first shrine on the Great Sky Island.
RUNES.what: Grab, move, rotate and glue almost any object together to build bridges, vehicles, and machines from Zonai parts.
RUNES.tip: Hold the button to fine-tune angle and distance; objects snap and turn yellow when they'll stick. Press up on the D-pad to detach mistakes.

RUNES.name: Fuse
RUNES.from: In-isa Shrine, the second Great Sky Island shrine.
RUNES.what: Attach an object or material to a weapon, shield, or arrow to boost damage, change its effect, or add reach.
RUNES.tip: Fuse a rock onto a flimsy stick for a hammer, or a Keese eyeball onto arrows for homing shots. It saves your weak weapons from breaking fast.

RUNES.name: Ascend
RUNES.from: Gutanbac Shrine, the third Great Sky Island shrine.
RUNES.what: Swim straight up through any ceiling above you and pop out standing on top of it.
RUNES.tip: Use it to skip climbing in caves and towers. Aim for a flat ceiling overhead, not slopes, and watch the cursor turn yellow when it's valid.

RUNES.name: Recall
RUNES.from: From Zelda's spirit at the Temple of Time on the Great Sky Island, after clearing the first three shrines.
RUNES.what: Reverse an object's recent movement, sending it back along the exact path it just traveled.
RUNES.tip: Ride a fallen sky rock back up to the islands, or send enemy projectiles flying back. Great for reaching high places without building.

RUNES.name: Autobuild
RUNES.from: From a Construct at the Great Abandoned Central Mine in the Depths, during the quest A Mystery in the Depths (not on the Great Sky Island).
RUNES.what: Instantly recreate saved or recently built Zonai constructions, spending Zonaite for parts you don't have on hand.
RUNES.tip: Save favorite vehicles like a hover bike so you can rebuild them anywhere. Capturing schematics from machines you find expands your options.

CHAMPIONS.name: Vow of Sidon, Sage of Water
CHAMPIONS.note: Vow of Sidon, Sage of Water

ROADMAP.name: 152 Shrines
ROADMAP.note: Every shrine grants a Light of Blessing; four trade for a heart or stamina vessel. The long-haul goal across Surface and Sky.

ROADMAP.name: 120 Lightroots
ROADMAP.note: Each Lightroot mirrors a Surface shrine and lights a patch of the pitch-black Depths.

ROADMAP.name: 1000 Korok Seeds
ROADMAP.note: Tiny puzzles all over Surface, Sky, and Depths. Trade to Hestu to expand your pouches.

ROADMAP.name: Sage's Wills & armor
ROADMAP.note: Upgrade your sage abilities with Sage's Wills, and armor at the Great Fairies once you reunite Mastro's troupe.

ROADMAP.name: Sky & Depths
ROADMAP.note: Sky islands, the vast Depths, Yiga schematics, Zonai device dispensers, and the addisons — a whole game beyond the Surface.

TIPS.name: Build & Fuse freely
items: Ultrahand + Fuse is the heart of the game — fuse rocks/monster parts to weapons for power, and stick Zonai devices together to travel.
Out of battery in the sky? Recall a fallen platform and ride it back up, or glide.
Autobuild (from the Fifth Sage) recreates your favorite vehicles for a little Zonaite.

TIPS.name: Surviving the Depths
items: The Depths are pitch black and full of Gloom that caps your hearts. Carry Brightbloom Seeds (throw or fuse to arrows) and light Lightroots.
Cure gloom-damaged (cracked) hearts with Sundelion dishes or by warping to the Surface.
Every Lightroot sits directly under a Surface shrine — a handy way to find shrines.

TOWERS.name: Lookout Landing Skyview Tower
TOWERS.location: Inside Lookout Landing, the hub settlement just north of Hyrule Castle (approx. -0293, 0137, 0025). You can't miss it — it's the first tower of the game.
TOWERS.climbTip: No puzzle: you receive it as part of the main story after the tutorial, once you reach Lookout Landing and speak to Captain Hoz; from then on every activation launches Link skyward to skydive off.

TOWERS.name: Hyrule Field Skyview Tower
TOWERS.location: South of Lookout Landing in central Hyrule Field (approx. -0753, -1019, 0064).
TOWERS.climbTip: It sits inside a Bokoblin encampment, so clear (or sneak/Bokoblin-Mask past) the Bokoblins and Boss Bokoblin guarding it before you can reach the terminal and get flung into the sky.

TOWERS.name: Lindor's Brow Skyview Tower
TOWERS.location: North of New Serenne Stable / northwest of Lookout Landing, marooned on a raised rock in the middle of a pond (approx. -1901, 1245, 0297).
TOWERS.climbTip: The tower is stranded on a rock in water, so Ultrahand a long bridge from the nearby planks/building materials to cross over, then hit the terminal to soar off. (A campfire updraft and paraglide also works.)

TOWERS.name: Rospro Pass Skyview Tower
TOWERS.location: In the cold Hebra region to the northwest (approx. -3686, 2342, 0233); bring cold resistance.
TOWERS.climbTip: The entrance is choked by thorns — throw Fire Fruit (or a fire arrow) to burn them away, then enter and activate the terminal to launch skyward.

TOWERS.name: Pikida Stonegrove Skyview Tower
TOWERS.location: Northwest of Snowfield Stable in the snowy Hebra highlands (approx. -2318, 3066, 0443).
TOWERS.climbTip: No puzzle, but it's a brutal cold-and-altitude trek — pack two levels of cold resistance (warm clothes/food, e.g. Snowquill set) to survive the climb, then activate the terminal to glide off.

TOWERS.name: Thyphlo Ruins Skyview Tower
TOWERS.location: In the pitch-dark Thyphlo Ruins north of the Great Hyrule Forest (approx. 0343, 3133, 0180).
TOWERS.climbTip: A platform blocks the top — Ascend up inside, then Ultrahand a Zonai rocket (or fan) onto the platform to shift it clear, drop down the shaft to the terminal and shoot into the sky.

TOWERS.name: Eldin Canyon Skyview Tower
TOWERS.location: In the volcanic Eldin region to the east, near Death Mountain (approx. 1637, 1190, 0225); bring heat/flame resistance.
TOWERS.climbTip: Fallen sky-rocks have jammed the door, so you must enter from the open top — wait for a large rock to fall nearby, stand on it and use Recall to ride it skyward, then paraglide into the tower's top and drop to the terminal to launch off.

TOWERS.name: Ulri Mountain Skyview Tower
TOWERS.location: In the eastern Akkala highlands on Ulri Mountain, northwest of Tarrey Town (approx. 3493, 2019, 0188).
TOWERS.climbTip: The tower's terminal has been stolen by an Aerocuda circling behind it — shoot the flying monster down with an arrow, retrieve the terminal (by hand or Ultrahand), and bring it back to the engineer (Sawson) to reinstall it before the tower will activate and fling you skyward.

TOWERS.name: Upland Zorana Skyview Tower
TOWERS.location: West of Zora's Domain in the Upland Zorana area, south of Foothill Stable (approx. 2858, 0582, 0379).
TOWERS.climbTip: Mud-like sludge seals the door — throw a Splash Fruit (or any water / blue Chuchu Jelly) to wash the muck off, then enter and activate the terminal to glide away.

TOWERS.name: Mount Lanayru Skyview Tower
TOWERS.location: High up Mount Lanayru in the far southeast, north of Hateno Village and far east of Kakariko (approx. 3846, -1307, 0539).
TOWERS.climbTip: No puzzle — the only obstacle is the freezing remote peak, so bring warm dishes/clothing and make the long climb up the mountain to the terminal to launch into the sky.

TOWERS.name: Rabella Wetlands Skyview Tower
TOWERS.location: In the rainy Rabella Wetlands of southeast Necluda (approx. 2419, -2755, 0222).
TOWERS.climbTip: Thorns block it but the constant rain won't let them burn — Ultrahand a roof/board over the thorns to keep them dry, then set them alight and activate the terminal to soar off.

TOWERS.name: Sahasra Slope Skyview Tower
TOWERS.location: On Sahasra Slope in West Necluda, north of the Dueling Peaks area (approx. 1344, -1176, 0166).
TOWERS.climbTip: The door can't be opened from outside — drop into the cave beneath the tower, smash the rock walls (hammer/bombs) to reach the flat spot under the floor, Ascend straight up into the interior, then remove the sticks blocking the doors and talk to Billson, who repairs the terminal.

TOWERS.name: Popla Foothills Skyview Tower
TOWERS.location: In the Popla Foothills of central-south Necluda (approx. 0602, -2127, 0098).
TOWERS.climbTip: The tower has lost power — enter the cave below and free the worker trapped inside to restore it, then Ascend up into the tower and activate the terminal to launch skyward.

TOWERS.name: Gerudo Canyon Skyview Tower
TOWERS.location: At the mouth of Gerudo Canyon in the southwest desert (approx. -2432, -2182, 0307).
TOWERS.climbTip: The technician (Sawson) is stuck on a lift below — Ultrahand metal crates onto the hook on the rope opposite the door so their weight acts as a counterweight and hauls his lift up; he then repairs the tower so you can activate it and glide off.

TOWERS.name: Gerudo Highlands Skyview Tower
TOWERS.location: In the snowy Gerudo Highlands in the far southwest (approx. -3959, -1313, 0422); bring cold resistance.
TOWERS.climbTip: The way in is buried in snow — find the cave behind/beneath the tower, follow it underneath, then Ascend up through the bottom into the entrance to reach the terminal and shoot into the sky.

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Great Fairy Tera
GREAT_FAIRIES.region: Eldin Canyon / near Great Hyrule Forest
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: Closed fountain near the Military Training Camp, northwest of Woodland Stable (south of the Lost Woods); coordinates 0920, 1361, 0093.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: No rupee fee. Unlock via the 'Serenade to a Great Fairy' side adventure. Prerequisite: have started 'Potential Princess Sightings' from Traysi/Penn at the Lucky Clover Gazette. Repair the Stable Trotters' wagon (Breezer) and tow the band up to the fountain with a horse + Towing Harness. Tera wants to hear the VIOLIN (played by Violynne). Tera is the FIRST fairy — clearing her quest unlocks the rest of the Serenade chain (Serenade to Mija → Serenade to Kaysa) plus Serenade to Cotera.

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Great Fairy Cotera
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: Closed fountain south of Dueling Peaks Stable, across the river past Little Twin Bridge; coordinates 1766, -2172, 0013.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: No rupee fee. Unlock via 'Serenade to Cotera': bring the Stable Trotters to perform here. Cotera wants the DRUM, so you must first recover the drummer Beetz — found playing his drums northeast of Dueling Peaks Stable — by completing his 'Honey, Bee Mine' side adventure (give Beetz 3 Courser Bee Honey). Once the band with the drummer performs at this fountain it opens. (Requires Tera's 'Serenade to a Great Fairy' first.)

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Great Fairy Kaysa
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: Closed fountain atop a steep hill west/northwest of Outskirt Stable in Hyrule Field; coordinates -1555, -1237, 0085.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: No rupee fee. Unlock via 'Serenade to Kaysa' (available after completing 'Serenade to Mija'): bring the Stable Trotters to perform here. Kaysa wants the FLUTE, so you must first recover the flutist Pyper — found atop a tree near Highland Stable — by completing 'The Flute Player's Plan' side adventure. Once the band with the flute player performs at this fountain it opens.

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Great Fairy Mija
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: Closed fountain northeast of Snowfield Stable (near Orochium Shrine); coordinates -1461, 2995, 0305.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: No rupee fee. Unlock via 'Serenade to Mija' (available after completing Tera's 'Serenade to a Great Fairy'): bring the Stable Trotters to perform here. Mija wants the HORN, so you must first recover the hornist Eustus — stuck in a hole near Piper Ridge — by completing 'The Hornist's Dramatic Escape' side adventure. Once the band with the horn player performs at this fountain it opens.

quests.name: Today's Menu
quests.giver: Burmano (the cook, inside the Emergency Shelter at Lookout Landing)
quests.reward: Fruit and Mushroom Mix (a cooked dish)
quests.oneLine: The shelter's cook is one ingredient short of finishing his dish and asks you to bring it.
quests.how: Talk to Burmano in the Emergency Shelter and he asks for an Apple. Hand over an Apple from your materials (shake any apple tree if you have none) and he gives you a Fruit and Mushroom Mix, completing the quest.

quests.name: The Incomplete Stable
quests.giver: Karson / Lester (Hudson Construction crew at the unfinished stable just inside the south gate of Lookout Landing)
quests.reward: Builds the Lookout Landing mini-stable (board/register horses there) + 1 Pony Point
quests.oneLine: The half-built stable at Lookout Landing is missing a roof plank you can put in place.
quests.how: Available after you clear one Regional Phenomena (one temple). Grab the loose wooden plank lying near the horses with Ultrahand, rotate it to fit the gap in the stable roof and set it in place to seal the hole, then talk to Karson/Lester to finish.

quests.name: Spotting Spot
quests.giver: Lester (at the now-finished Lookout Landing mini-stable, south side)
quests.location: Lookout Landing (south) / Passeri Greenbelt just southwest
quests.reward: Purple Rupee (50), a Swift Carrot, and you keep the horse Spot
quests.oneLine: Lester wants you to catch a particular spotted horse roaming the fields nearby.
quests.how: Complete The Incomplete Stable first. Go a short way southwest to the wild horses near Passeri Greenbelt and find Spot — a beige horse with grey spots and a black mane and tail. Sneak up behind it, mount it, soothe it with the L-stick to tame it, then ride it back and register it with Lester while mounted.

quests.name: Village Attacked by Pirates
quests.giver: Garini (with Mubs, at the Lookout Landing General Store)
quests.reward: Mighty Salt-Grilled Crab
quests.oneLine: A worried merchant has heard his home village of Lurelin is being raided by pirates and wants it made safe.
quests.how: Talk to Garini or Mubs at the Lookout Landing General Store to start it. Travel to Lurelin Village in southeast Hyrule and clear out the monster pirates occupying it (the Lurelin 'Ruffian-Infested Village' liberation), then return to Garini at Lookout Landing to report the villagers are safe and collect the crab.

quests.name: A Deal With the Statue
quests.giver: The Horned Statue (in the Royal Hidden Passage, reached through the Emergency Shelter under Lookout Landing)
quests.reward: Unlocks the Horned Statue trade — sell a Heart or Stamina vessel for 100 rupees and buy either back for 120, so you can freely swap hearts for stamina (and back)
quests.oneLine: A statue offers to take a heart and let you re-spec your hearts and stamina vessels.
quests.how: Available after you clear one temple. Reach the Horned Statue at the end of the Royal Hidden Passage (the same cave as Who Goes There?). Talk to it — it takes one Heart Container and returns it on the second chat, then opens its swap-shop so you can trade hearts and stamina back and forth for rupees.

quests.name: WANTED: Stone Talus
quests.giver: Gralens (Monster-Control Crew, inside the Emergency Shelter at Lookout Landing)
quests.location: Lookout Landing (Emergency Shelter); target in Akkala
quests.reward: 100 rupees; clearing all three big-monster WANTED bounties adds 300 more rupees
quests.oneLine: Gralens posts a bounty on a large Stone Talus and marks it on your map.
quests.how: Available after you finish two 'Bring Peace to...!' raids. Talk to Gralens, choose 'How about big monsters?' then pick Stone Talus to mark it (Akkala). Ascend or climb onto the Talus, run to the ore deposit on its back, and Fuse a heavy rock or hammer to your weapon to smash the deposit until it dies. Return to Gralens for the reward.

quests.name: WANTED: Hinox
quests.giver: Gralens (Monster-Control Crew, inside the Emergency Shelter at Lookout Landing)
quests.location: Lookout Landing (Emergency Shelter); target out in the field
quests.reward: 100 rupees; clearing all three big-monster WANTED bounties adds 300 more rupees
quests.oneLine: Gralens posts a bounty on a sleeping Hinox and marks its location.
quests.how: From Gralens' 'big monsters' menu pick Hinox to mark it. Travel there and hit the Hinox in its single eye to stun it — shoot the eye with an arrow or land a strong fused blow — then pile on damage to the downed body. Repeat until it falls and report back to Gralens.

quests.name: WANTED: Molduga
quests.giver: Gralens (Monster-Control Crew, inside the Emergency Shelter at Lookout Landing)
quests.location: Lookout Landing (Emergency Shelter); target in the Gerudo Desert
quests.reward: 100 rupees; clearing all three big-monster WANTED bounties adds 300 more rupees
quests.oneLine: Gralens posts a bounty on a Molduga that swims through the desert sand.
quests.how: From Gralens' 'big monsters' menu pick Molduga to mark it (Gerudo Desert). Stand on a rock so it can't reach you, then drop or shoot a Bomb Flower (Fuse one to an arrow) onto the sand near it; the blast flips it onto its back, so strike it with strong fused melee hits. Repeat and return to Gralens.

quests.name: Unknown Sky Giant
quests.giver: Gralens (Monster-Control Crew, inside the Emergency Shelter at Lookout Landing)
quests.location: Lookout Landing (Emergency Shelter); target is a Flux Construct in the sky
quests.reward: 100 rupees; clearing all three 'Unknown' unusual-monster bounties also yields a Diamond
quests.oneLine: Gralens relays sightings of a giant block-creature in the sky and marks it for you to slay.
quests.how: After clearing all three big-monster WANTED bounties, ask Gralens about 'unusual monsters' and pick the sky giant to mark a Flux Construct (near the Wind Temple region). Pull a glowing block off it with Ultrahand to expose the core, then Ascend or climb to the core and smash it until the Construct collapses. Report to Gralens.

quests.name: Unknown Three-Headed Monster
quests.giver: Gralens (Monster-Control Crew, inside the Emergency Shelter at Lookout Landing)
quests.location: Lookout Landing (Emergency Shelter); target is a Gleeok at the Bridge of Hylia
quests.reward: 100 rupees; clearing all three 'Unknown' unusual-monster bounties also yields a Diamond
quests.oneLine: Reports of a fire-breathing three-headed dragon send Gralens' bounty to a Gleeok.
quests.how: From Gralens' 'unusual monsters' options pick the three-headed monster to mark a Flame Gleeok at the Bridge of Hylia. Shoot each of its three heads to stun them; when all heads are down the Gleeok crashes, so use a high vantage and Bullet Time (jump then draw the bow) plus fused arrows to finish it. Return to Gralens.

quests.name: Unknown Huge Silhouette
quests.giver: Gralens (Monster-Control Crew, inside the Emergency Shelter at Lookout Landing)
quests.location: Lookout Landing (Emergency Shelter); target is a Frox in the Depths
quests.reward: 100 rupees; clearing all three 'Unknown' unusual-monster bounties also yields a Diamond
quests.oneLine: Gralens marks a huge silhouette lurking below — a Frox in the Depths.
quests.how: From Gralens' 'unusual monsters' options pick the huge silhouette to mark a Frox in the Depths. Toss a bomb or Brightbloom Seed into its mouth when it tries to inhale you (or shoot the glowing bulbs on its back), then climb onto its back while it's stunned and pummel the bulbs. Repeat until it dies and report to Gralens.

quests.name: Bring Peace to Hyrule Field!
quests.giver: Hoz (Monster-Control Crew captain, on horseback in Hyrule Field just southwest of the Hyrule Field Chasm, south of Lookout Landing)
quests.location: Hyrule Field, south of Lookout Landing
quests.reward: 100 rupees; starts the next 'Bring Peace to...!' regional quest
quests.oneLine: Hoz's squad is about to storm a monster camp on Hyrule Field and asks you to join the raid.
quests.how: Find Hoz mounted near the Hyrule Field Chasm and agree to help. Ride to the nearby monster camp, talk to Hoz to begin the assault, then clear every monster (Bokoblins and a Moblin). Wiping out the camp completes it and Hoz moves on to Fort Hateno for the next region's raid.

quests.name: Hestu's Concern
quests.giver: Hestu (the giant Korok, at Lindor's Brow just northwest of Lookout Landing)
quests.location: Lindor's Brow / outskirts of Lookout Landing
quests.reward: Unlocks Hestu so you can trade Korok Seeds to expand weapon/bow/shield pouch slots
quests.oneLine: Hestu is too frightened of some nearby 'trees' to do his job until you deal with them.
quests.how: Go to Hestu at Lindor's Brow; the 'trees' he fears are Evermean monsters. Attack the suspicious trees to make them spring to life and defeat them (a few solid hits each). With them gone Hestu relaxes and will start upgrading your equipment-pouch slots in exchange for Korok Seeds. (If you've already cleared a temple, Hestu instead waits inside Lookout Landing.)

quests.name: Who Goes There?
quests.giver: Jerrin (inside the Emergency Shelter at Lookout Landing)
quests.location: Lookout Landing (Emergency Shelter / Royal Hidden Passage)
quests.reward: 20 Rupees
quests.oneLine: Jerrin heard a creepy voice through a hole in the shelter wall and wants you to check it out.
quests.how: Talk to Jerrin, then go into the Royal Hidden Passage behind the hole. Follow the tunnel and break the rocks blocking the way with a Bomb Flower or a fused hammer/heavy weapon; the voice turns out to be the Horned Statue (talking to it also begins the separate 'A Deal With the Statue'). Return to Jerrin to reassure her and finish.

quests.name: Messages from an Ancient Era
quests.giver: Wortsworth (the scholar studying a fallen tablet at Lookout Landing)
quests.location: Lookout Landing (Wortsworth later relocates to Kakariko Village)
quests.reward: 100 rupees per translated tablet, plus the Zonai Survey Team Fabric (horse caparison) on completion
quests.oneLine: A scholar wants you to photograph ancient stone tablets across Hyrule so he can translate them.
quests.how: Available after you clear a temple, when a tablet falls at Lookout Landing. Talk to Wortsworth (he then moves to Kakariko Village). Travel to each Ring Ruin / ancient tablet he marks, use the Purah Pad camera to photograph the carved text clearly, and bring the pictures back; each readable photo pays out and the full set rewards the Zonai Survey Team Fabric.

quests.name: Hateno Village Research Lab
quests.giver: Robbie (at the Lookout Landing research room) — he then has you meet him at the Hateno Ancient Tech Lab
quests.location: Lookout Landing (resolves at the Hateno Ancient Tech Lab in East Necluda)
quests.reward: Shrine Sensor (Purah Pad upgrade); opens Robbie's follow-up Purah Pad quests (Travel Medallion, Sensor +, Hero's Path)
quests.oneLine: Robbie asks you to come to his Hateno lab so he can upgrade your Purah Pad.
quests.how: Available after the main quest 'A Mystery in the Depths' and gaining Autobuild. Speak with Robbie at Lookout Landing, travel to the Hateno Ancient Tech Lab east of Hateno Village, and he installs the Shrine Sensor. Walk the lab with it until it pings the nearby hidden shrine, find that shrine, then return to Robbie to finish.

quests.name: A New Champion's Tunic
quests.giver: Reading Zelda's Diary in Zelda's Secret Well, behind Link's old house in Hateno Village
quests.location: Hateno Village (East Necluda); completed at Hyrule Castle Sanctum
quests.reward: Champion's Leathers
quests.oneLine: Zelda's diary reveals she ordered a new tunic for Link, hidden in the castle throne room.
quests.how: Read Zelda's Diary down the well behind her old Hateno house to start the quest, then go to the Sanctum (throne room) atop Hyrule Castle. Light the two torches flanking the throne (Flint, a Fire Fruit, or a fused fire item/arrow). The statue behind the throne slides aside, revealing a chest with the Champion's Leathers.

quests.name: Dantz's Prize Cows
quests.giver: Dantz, at Hateno Pasture (east edge of Hateno Village, on the path to the lab)
quests.reward: Fresh Milk (repeatable: 3 Acorns = 1 bottle)
quests.oneLine: A rancher trades you Fresh Milk for acorns to feed his cows.
quests.how: Bring Dantz 3 Acorns. Get them by chopping the trees behind the pasture or by shooting the bushy-tailed squirrels in the nearby woods, then hand them over. He gives a bottle of Fresh Milk, and you can repeat the trade any time.

quests.name: A Bottled Cry for Help
quests.giver: A message bottle washed up on Hateno Beach (southeast coast, East Necluda)
quests.location: Hateno Beach / Mapla Point Cave (East Necluda)
quests.reward: 100 Rupees
quests.oneLine: A bottled letter says a fisherman is trapped in a sea cave and needs rescuing.
quests.how: Read the bottle on the beach, then make your way to Mapla Point Cave to the southeast. Inside, use Ultrahand to pull away the boulder damming the path so Chumin can escape; talk to him for 100 Rupees.

quests.name: Manny's Beloved
quests.giver: Manny, outside the house next to Cece's shop in Hateno Village
quests.reward: Rushroom x10
quests.oneLine: Manny wants to know what Ivee, the general-store girl, likes so he can win her over.
quests.how: Talk to Manny, then ask Ivee (in front of the General Store) what she likes; she asks for 10 Hot-Footed Frogs. Catch them around water near Hateno (the well up the hill behind the store is reliable), give them to Manny, and he rewards you with 10 Rushrooms.

quests.name: Photographing a Chuchu
quests.giver: Sayge, at the Kochi Dye Shop in Hateno Village
quests.reward: Chuchu Fabric (paraglider fabric)
quests.oneLine: The dye-shop owner wants a Compendium photo of an ordinary Chuchu for paraglider inspiration.
quests.how: You need the camera (from 'A Mystery in the Depths') and must have changed the paraglider design once so Sayge will offer this. Photograph a regular (not elemental) Chuchu, then show Sayge the picture to receive the Chuchu Fabric.

quests.name: Teach Me a Lesson
quests.giver: Symin, inside the Hateno Village school (daytime)
quests.location: Hateno Village (East Necluda); a photo is taken in Kakariko Village
quests.reward: Rupees (and the school field opens up, leading to 'Uma's Garden')
quests.oneLine: The teacher needs Compendium photos of historical sites to teach the kids about the past.
quests.how: Talk to Symin by day and accept his photo requests (including the mural in Kakariko Village). Take the pictures with your camera and show him. Completing the chain frees the school's field, which starts 'Uma's Garden'.

quests.name: Uma's Garden
quests.giver: Uma, at the field beside the Hateno Village school
quests.reward: A grown crop of whatever produce you provided
quests.oneLine: An old woman will grow a vegetable for you if you give her a sample to plant.
quests.how: Finish 'Teach Me a Lesson' first to open the field. Give Uma one sample of a growable crop (Sun Pumpkin, Swift Carrot, Hylian Tomato, etc.). Leave and come back later (about an in-game day) to harvest the grown crop and complete the quest.

quests.name: A Letter to Koyin
quests.giver: Koyin, at Hateno Pasture in Hateno Village
quests.reward: Hateno Cheese (used to start 'A New Signature Food')
quests.oneLine: Koyin asks you to fish a dropped message bottle out of the pasture pond.
quests.how: A Message Bottle floats in the pasture pond. Chop nearby trees, Ultrahand the logs into a raft to reach it (or build out to it), grab the bottle, and bring it to Koyin. Talk to her again at the ranch house and she thanks you with Hateno Cheese.

quests.name: A New Signature Food
quests.giver: Reede, in Hateno Village
quests.reward: 100 Rupees; counts toward 'The Mayoral Election'
quests.oneLine: Reede wants to recreate his grandfather's signature dish and needs an ingredient.
quests.how: Get Hateno Cheese first by completing 'A Letter to Koyin'. Bring that Hateno Cheese to Reede; he uses it for his recipe and rewards you with 100 Rupees. This is one of the four prerequisites for 'The Mayoral Election'.

quests.name: Team Cece or Team Reede?
quests.giver: Cece, at her hat shop in Hateno Village
quests.reward: Progress toward 'The Mayoral Election'
quests.oneLine: Mayoral candidate Cece asks you to sway the village's undecided voters.
quests.how: Cece gives you mushrooms to hand out to the last undecided villagers around Hateno to win them over. Deliver them all (talk to each holdout) to finish her campaign errand. One of the four prerequisites for 'The Mayoral Election'.

quests.name: Cece's Secret
quests.giver: Sophie (Cece's sister), in Hateno Village
quests.reward: 10 Ironshrooms; progress toward 'The Mayoral Election'
quests.oneLine: Find out the secret the hat-loving mayoral candidate Cece is hiding.
quests.how: At night, follow/peek at Cece to catch her secretly eating carrots, despite campaigning against farming. Report the secret to complete the quest for 10 Ironshrooms. One of the four prerequisites for 'The Mayoral Election'.

quests.name: Reede's Secret
quests.giver: Clavia (Reede's wife), in Hateno Village
quests.reward: 10 Hylian Tomatoes; progress toward 'The Mayoral Election'
quests.oneLine: Uncover what mayoral candidate Reede keeps hidden in his locked shed.
quests.how: Drop down the nearby Hateno well, follow the underground cave until you are beneath the shed, then use Ascend to pop up inside the locked shed. Read Reede's Diary to learn his secret; report back for 10 Hylian Tomatoes. One of the four prerequisites for 'The Mayoral Election'.

quests.name: The Mayoral Election
quests.giver: Sophie, in Hateno Village (daytime, not raining)
quests.reward: Cece Hat
quests.oneLine: Resolve Hateno's mayoral race between Reede and the hat-maker Cece.
quests.how: First complete the four prep quests in any order: 'Team Cece or Team Reede?', 'Cece's Secret', 'Reede's Secret', and 'A New Signature Food'. Then talk to Sophie during the day (when it isn't raining) to hold the election; Cece wins and gives you the Cece Hat.

quests.name: Homegrown in Hateno
quests.giver: Reede, near the Hateno Village entrance
quests.reward: 5 Sun Pumpkins
quests.oneLine: Defend Hateno's restored vegetable field from waves of monsters overnight.
quests.how: Available after 'The Mayoral Election'. Talk to Reede, then guard the field overnight: smash the heads of the Stalkoblins so they can't reassemble, beat the Stalmoblin that appears around 3 a.m., then clear the final archer/Stalmoblin wave (bomb-fused arrows help, or simply survive to 5 a.m. when they vanish). Reede rewards you with 5 Sun Pumpkins.

quests.name: Hateno Village Research Lab
quests.giver: Robbie, at the Hateno Ancient Tech Lab (hill east of Hateno Village)
quests.reward: The Sensor function for the Purah Pad
quests.oneLine: Help Robbie get his shrine Sensor working again.
quests.how: After helping Robbie at Lookout Landing (he relocates to the lab), talk to him at the Hateno Ancient Tech Lab to get the Sensor, then follow his directions until it pings a nearby Shrine of Light; head to the cave below the lab's hill to find and activate that shrine, then report back.

quests.name: Presenting: Sensor +!
quests.giver: Robbie, at the Hateno Ancient Tech Lab
quests.reward: Sensor + upgrade (track any Compendium creature or material)
quests.oneLine: Robbie upgrades the Sensor to detect anything in your Hyrule Compendium.
quests.how: After 'Hateno Village Research Lab' and with the camera (from 'A Mystery in the Depths'), talk to Robbie again at the lab. He upgrades the Purah Pad to Sensor +, letting you set it to ping any registered material or creature.

quests.name: Presenting: Hero's Path Mode!
quests.giver: Robbie, at the Hateno Ancient Tech Lab
quests.reward: Hero's Path Mode for the map
quests.oneLine: Robbie adds a map feature that traces the path you've traveled.
quests.how: Visit at least 15 shrine locations, then talk to Robbie at the lab. He unlocks Hero's Path Mode, which overlays your last ~256 hours of travel on the map.

quests.name: Presenting: The Travel Medallion!
quests.giver: Robbie, at the Hateno Ancient Tech Lab
quests.location: Hateno Ancient Tech Lab (East Necluda); prototype retrieved at Akkala Ancient Tech Lab
quests.reward: Travel Medallion (set one custom fast-travel point)
quests.oneLine: Build a custom fast-travel point after fetching a prototype for Robbie.
quests.how: Robbie sends you to the Yiga-occupied Akkala Ancient Tech Lab. Defeat the Yiga guards at the door, go inside, and open the chest for the Travel Medallion Prototype. Bring it back to Robbie at Hateno; he upgrades the Purah Pad to register the Travel Medallion.

quests.name: Filling Out the Compendium
quests.giver: Robbie, at the Hateno Ancient Tech Lab
quests.reward: Froggy paraglider fabric
quests.oneLine: Robbie wants help completing the Hyrule Compendium.
quests.how: Available after you have the camera. Register enough Compendium entries (or simply buy missing photos from Robbie for 100 Rupees each) to reach the threshold; Robbie then gives you a paraglider fabric you can equip at Sayge's Kochi Dye Shop.

quests.name: Bring Peace to Necluda
quests.giver: Hoz, with his monster-control crew near Fort Hateno (West Necluda, east of Dueling Peaks Stable before the bridge)
quests.location: West Necluda (near Fort Hateno / Dueling Peaks)
quests.reward: 100 Rupees
quests.oneLine: A soldier asks you to help clear the monsters threatening Hyrule Field.
quests.how: Talk to Hoz, then follow him to the attack site and help his crew wipe out every monster there (fused weapons and bomb arrows clear it fast). Return to Hoz and he rewards you with 100 Rupees.

quests.name: A Picture for Dueling Peaks Stable
quests.giver: Tasseren, at Dueling Peaks Stable (examine the empty picture frame)
quests.reward: Pony Point + Hasty Apple Pie
quests.oneLine: The stable owner wants a photo of Hyrule's most beautiful sunrise to hang on his wall.
quests.how: Examine the empty frame and Tasseren asks for a shot of the sunrise from Tuft Mountain (just west of Lurelin). Climb Tuft Mountain and photograph the sunrise between about 4 a.m. and 6 a.m., then show it to Tasseren for a Pony Point and Hasty Apple Pie.

quests.name: Serenade to Cotera
quests.giver: Cotera, at her Great Fairy Fountain near Dueling Peaks Stable
quests.reward: Unlocks Great Fairy Cotera (armor upgrades)
quests.oneLine: Wake Great Fairy Cotera by reuniting the traveling musical troupe at her fountain.
quests.how: Cotera will only awaken for the full band. Bring the missing drummer back by completing 'Honey, Bee Mine' (give Beetz his Courser Bee Honey), then use Ultrahand to ferry the whole troupe across to Cotera's fountain so she awakens and offers armor upgrades.

quests.name: Honey, Bee Mine
quests.giver: Beetz, southwest of Kakariko Village near Bonooru's Stand
quests.location: West Necluda / Dueling Peaks area (drummer of the Dueling Peaks Stable troupe)
quests.reward: 100 Rupees; the drummer rejoins the troupe (advances 'Serenade to Cotera')
quests.oneLine: The troupe's drummer Beetz wants Courser Bee Honey before he'll rejoin the band.
quests.how: Bring Beetz 3 Courser Bee Honey. Harvest it from hanging beehives in the forests (the woods by the Depths entrance near Kakariko are closest) by shooting the hives, or get it as the reward from 'The Hornist's Dramatic Escape'. Give it to Beetz for 100 Rupees; he heads back to Dueling Peaks Stable, feeding into 'Serenade to Cotera'.

quests.name: Mired in Muck
quests.giver: Bazz, stuck in gloom-sludge in front of the Upland Zorana Skyview Tower (Ternio Trail), west of Zora's Domain
quests.reward: Zora Spear
quests.oneLine: The Zora guard captain Bazz is trapped in gloom-sludge and needs water to wash himself free.
quests.how: Pick a Splash Fruit from the little trees right next to Bazz and either throw it at the sludge on him or Fuse it to an arrow (hold ZR, press up on the D-pad) and shoot — the burst of water washes the muck away. Have a free weapon slot, then talk to him for the Zora Spear.

quests.name: A Token of Friendship
quests.giver: Yona, by the Goddess Statue in Zora's Domain (after the Water Temple)
quests.reward: Zora Greaves
quests.oneLine: Yona reveals the Zora had a gift of legwear waiting for Link, hidden away in the old waterworks.
quests.how: Dive into the whirlpool in East Reservoir Lake (east of Zora's Domain) to drop into the drained Ancient Zora Waterworks. Drop through the hole into the large chamber (a Luminous Stone Talus is here, optional) and look behind the waterfall on the far side for the chest holding the Zora Greaves.

quests.name: The Never-Ending Lecture
quests.giver: Chroma (being lectured by Khira) in the throne room of Zora's Domain (after Sidon of the Zora)
quests.reward: Zora Helm
quests.oneLine: Overhearing a lecture about a lost Zora Helm sends Link to a fish-shaped sky island to recover it.
quests.how: Reach Floating Scales Island in the sky east of the Water Temple (climb Ploymus Mountain and swim up the waterfall in Zora Armor, or fly from a nearby Skyview Tower). Enter the tunnel/grotto on the northeast side of the fish's tail and open the chest inside for the Zora Helm.

quests.name: A Wife Wafted Away
quests.giver: Fronk, near the southwest entrance of Zora's Domain (after Sidon of the Zora)
quests.reward: Hearty Fish Skewer
quests.oneLine: Fronk is worried his wife Mei went off fishing somewhere dangerous and asks Link to find her.
quests.how: Track the quest to Wellspring Island, where Mei is fishing beside a small pool (around 3668, 0567, 1251) — reach it via the waterfall near Shatterback Point or from Igoshon Shrine at the Water Temple. Talk to her to send her home, then return to Fronk in Zora's Domain for the Hearty Fish Skewer.

quests.name: The Moonlit Princess
quests.giver: Seggin, on the bridge leading west out of Zora's Domain (after clean water is restored)
quests.reward: Zora Sword
quests.oneLine: Seggin wants a photo of Mipha's statue holding the Lightscale Trident in the moonlight on a clear night.
quests.how: Sleep at the inn until clear weather and a normal (non-Blood) moon around midnight, then go to Mipha Court and use the Camera to photograph Mipha's statue with the moon behind it — a confirmation mark means it counts. Show the picture to Seggin for the Zora Sword.

quests.name: A Crabulous Deal
quests.giver: Cleff at the General Store in Zora's Domain (after the sludge is cleared)
quests.reward: Sapphire (then repeatable item trades, e.g. Opal, for more crabs)
quests.oneLine: Cleff has become obsessed with Bright-Eyed Crabs and rewards you for bringing him a batch.
quests.how: Collect Bright-Eyed Crabs around Zora's Domain — on the rocks at the base of the support pillars, in the central lower pool, and near the General Store. Photograph one for the Sensor+ to track more, then bring Cleff 10 Bright-Eyed Crabs to receive a Sapphire.

quests.name: Glory of the Zora
quests.giver: Dento, inside the General Store in Zora's Domain (after Sidon of the Zora)
quests.reward: Lightscale Trident (re-craftable)
quests.oneLine: Master craftsman Dento offers to forge the Lightscale Trident if you bring the materials.
quests.how: Gather 1 Zora Spear (chest southwest of Ralis Pond, or dropped by Like Likes), 3 Diamonds, and 5 Flint, then give them all to Dento and he forges the Lightscale Trident. He'll remake it anytime you bring the same materials again.

quests.name: Secret Treasure under the Great Fish
quests.giver: Sidon, atop the highest platform of Zora's Domain next to King Dorephan (after Sidon of the Zora)
quests.location: Zora's Domain / Cave Under Zora's Domain
quests.reward: Vah Ruta Divine Helm
quests.oneLine: Sidon shares a riddle pointing to a hidden treasure beneath two waterfalls under the Domain.
quests.how: From the long bridge out of Zora's Domain, dive into the marked waterfall and dash (A) to enter the Cave Under Zora's Domain — do NOT wear Zora Armor or you'll swim back up the falls. Pass through the second waterfall inside to reach the chest holding the Vah Ruta Divine Helm.

quests.name: The Fort at Ja'Abu Ridge
quests.giver: Gaddison, at the east bridge/entrance of Zora's Domain (after Sidon of the Zora)
quests.location: Ja'Abu Ridge, southeast of Zora's Domain
quests.reward: 100 Rupees
quests.oneLine: Gaddison asks Link to clear the monsters that have seized the fort at Ja'Abu Ridge.
quests.how: Head to Ja'Abu Ridge Fort and snipe the watchtower Bokoblin with a bow before it sounds the horn, then wipe out the rest of the Bokoblins and the Boss Bokoblin. Return to Gaddison on the bridge for 100 Rupees.

quests.name: True Treasure
quests.giver: Kodah, inside the inn in Zora's Domain (after Sidon of the Zora)
quests.reward: Opal x2 (and the cave's Yomizuk blessing shrine becomes reachable)
quests.oneLine: Kodah sends Link to a cave at Tarm Point where a stranded Zora is searching for treasure.
quests.how: Go to the Tarm Point cave and talk to Sasan, who can't swim. Help him raft across the cavern to Finley on the far island — use the rising/falling water level and Ultrahand-built platforms to clear the path so the two reunite, earning Opal x2.

quests.name: The Blue Stone
quests.giver: Ledo, at the end of the walkway at East Reservoir Lake (after Sidon of the Zora)
quests.location: East Reservoir Lake, east of Zora's Domain
quests.reward: Zora Fabric (paraglider fabric)
quests.oneLine: Stonemason Ledo wants a large luminous (blue) stone brought back from a cavern across the reservoir.
quests.how: Build a raft with Ultrahand (a plank, two Fans, and a Steering Stick) and cross to the Reservoir Lakefront Cavern. Ultrahand a large luminous stone onto your raft, ferry it back to Ledo, and he gives you the Zora Fabric (take it to the Kochi Dye Shop in Hateno to restyle your paraglider).

quests.name: An Uninvited Guest
quests.giver: Ami, a child around the side of Wetland Stable
quests.reward: 2 Pony Points
quests.oneLine: A frightened child can't sleep because monsters near the stable are scaring him.
quests.how: Head into the woods behind the stable to the monster camp (a Moblin with Lizalfos). A handy trick is to drop into the stable's well and pop out the nearby South Well right beside the camp for a sneakstrike. Defeat the monsters, then report back to Ami for 2 Pony Points.

quests.name: The Ultimate Dish
quests.giver: Moza, inside the Rikoka Hills Well (southern edge of the Lanayru Wetlands)
quests.reward: Monster Stew (which she keeps upgrading)
quests.oneLine: Moza, obsessed with the 'ultimate dish,' wants you to bring her a failed dish to experiment on.
quests.how: Make a failed dish at any cooking pot — add a mineral for Rock-Hard Food or a monster part for Dubious Food — then bring it down the well to Moza. Pay her a few Rupees and she 'perfects' it into a Monster Stew that restores extra hearts.

quests.name: A Picture for Wetland Stable
quests.giver: Lawdon — interact with the empty picture frame on the wall inside Wetland Stable
quests.reward: 1 Pony Point (plus the standard stable-picture bonus item if it's your last frame)
quests.oneLine: The stable owner wants a photo of the Ring Ruins to hang in his empty frame.
quests.how: Travel toward Kakariko Village and use the Camera to photograph the large ring-shaped Ring Ruins (above Lantern Lake) so the whole ring and its label are in shot. Return and interact with the frame again to show Lawdon the photo for the reward.

quests.name: Goddess Statue of Wisdom
quests.giver: The Goddess Statue at the Spring of Wisdom, atop Mount Lanayru
quests.reward: Sapphire
quests.oneLine: The statue can no longer sense its Mother Goddess Statue and asks Link to investigate, then bring a dragon's claw.
quests.how: With Lv.2 Cold Resistance, climb Mount Lanayru and pray at the statue. First go check the toppled Mother Goddess Statue in the Forgotten Temple and report back; the statue then asks for one of Naydra's Claws. Find the dragon Naydra (around Mount Lanayru / Naydra Snowfield Chasm), shoot a claw off and grab it, then offer it at the spring for a Sapphire.

quests.name: Amber Dealer
quests.giver: Ramella, standing in front of the ore shop in central Goron City
quests.reward: 200 Rupees + she then buys your ore in 10-piece bulk
quests.oneLine: A traveling merchant wants ten pieces of Amber and will start buying your ore in bulk.
quests.how: Mine Amber from the black ore deposits all over Death Mountain and Eldin until you have 10. Talk to Ramella and sell the 10 Amber; she pays 200 Rupees, then afterward buys your other ores ten at a time (she picks which ore, and it changes daily).

quests.name: Simmerstone Springs
quests.giver: Kima, on the raised bridge behind the Goron City inn
quests.reward: Silver Rupee (100)
quests.oneLine: A Goron is hunting for a legendary hot spring and wants you to find it.
quests.how: Talk to Kima, then head into Gorko Tunnel (northwest, behind the city) and follow it to the hidden hot spring inside. Report back to Kima at the bridge to finish; he hands over a Silver Rupee and starts boiling eggs in it.

quests.name: The Ancient City Gorondia
quests.giver: Dugby, across the rail tracks near YunoboCo HQ in Goron City
quests.reward: Zonai Charge + Large Zonai Charge
quests.oneLine: An obsessed Goron historian needs a ride across the broken mine-cart tracks to chase a legend.
quests.how: Grab a nearby mine cart and a Fan with Ultrahand, attach the Fan to the cart and set it on the rails. Ask Dugby to board, then strike/activate the Fan to push the cart across (Part 1). For Part 2, follow Dugby despite his protest, build another fan-cart, clear any rocks blocking the rails with Ultrahand, and ride him to the destination.

quests.name: The Hidden Treasure at Lizard Lakes
quests.giver: Bludo, in the central plaza of Goron City (after Yunobo of Goron City)
quests.location: Goron City / Lizard's Burrow (between the two lizard-shaped lakes)
quests.reward: Vah Rudania Divine Helm
quests.oneLine: Bludo points you to a treasure hidden in a cave between the lizard-shaped lakes.
quests.how: Talk to Bludo, then travel to Lizard's Burrow, the cave set between the two lizard-shaped lakes. Blow up or smash the rubble blocking the entrance (Yunobo's charge or a bomb works), go inside, and open the treasure chest to claim the Vah Rudania Divine Helm (fire resistance).

quests.name: Moon-Gazing Gorons
quests.giver: Tray (with Volcon) near the Goron City inn entrance (after Yunobo of Goron City)
quests.reward: Silver Rupee (100)
quests.oneLine: Gorons want a photo of a mysterious circle of light that only appears at midday in a lakeside cave.
quests.how: Go to Lake Ferona (south of Goron City, by Eldin Canyon Skyview Tower) and enter the cave on the lake's west side. The ring of light only forms around noon, so make a campfire from wood + flint and 'wait until midday', then photograph the circular light with your Camera. Show the picture to Tray and Volcon back in Goron City.

quests.name: Soul of the Gorons
quests.giver: Fugo, in the building north of the Goron City general store (after Yunobo of Goron City)
quests.reward: Boulder Breaker (and he will re-forge it if it breaks)
quests.oneLine: The new smith needs rare materials to forge the legendary Goron weapon, the Boulder Breaker.
quests.how: Bring Fugo 1 Cobble Crusher (chest from Like Likes in Eldin, or held by a Depths shadow soldier), 3 Diamonds (Goron City shop, or rare ore deposits), and 5 Flint (any ore deposit). Hand them over to receive the Boulder Breaker; return to him later to have it re-forged if it breaks.

quests.name: The Abandoned Laborer
quests.giver: Mota, stranded inside the Death Mountain West Tunnel
quests.reward: Large Zonai Charge
quests.oneLine: A worker is trapped on the far side of a broken track inside a scorching tunnel and needs a way out.
quests.how: Wear Fireproof armor or drink a Fireproof Elixir first. Enter the tunnel, glide down to Mota across the broken railing, and clear the rubble hiding the Zonai Rockets (don't use Bomb Flowers in the heat). Ultrahand the Rockets onto a mine cart, have Mota board, strike it to launch him across, and lead him outside.

quests.name: Meat for Meat
quests.giver: Mezer, at the Bedrock Bistro by Goronbi Lake (after the Fire Temple)
quests.reward: Large Zonai Charge
quests.oneLine: The bistro needs a Marbled Rock Roast to cook with and trades you a Zonai charge for one.
quests.how: After the Fire Temple frees the Gorons, visit the Bedrock Bistro and talk to Mezer. Fetch one Marbled Rock Roast from the West or East Restaurant Cave nearby and bring it back to Mezer; he trades you a Large Zonai Charge (and this opens Rock Roast or Dust).

quests.name: Rock Roast or Dust
quests.giver: Cooke, at the Bedrock Bistro counter (after Meat for Meat)
quests.reward: Seared Gourmet Steak
quests.oneLine: The chef needs another Marbled Rock Roast to keep cooking.
quests.how: Talk to Cooke at the counter, then return to one of the Restaurant Caves for a Marbled Rock Roast. The east cave requires riding a mine cart over the lava to reach it. Bring the roast back to Cooke for a Seared Gourmet Steak (he keeps trading one per roast).

quests.name: Cash In on Ripened Flint
quests.giver: Gomo, sitting beside the Bedrock Bistro (after Meat for Meat)
quests.reward: 1,000 Rupees
quests.oneLine: A Goron gambles that if you hand him enough Flint, one piece will be rare 'Ripened Flint'.
quests.how: Talk to Gomo and give him a batch of Flint (he asks for 20, 50, or 100 at once). If any piece in the batch turns out to be Ripened Flint, he pays 1,000 Rupees; otherwise the Flint is consumed, so gather more from ore deposits and try again (50+ at a time improves the odds).

quests.name: Mine-Cart Land: Open For Business!
quests.giver: Bayge, at the Southern Mine (north of Timawak Shrine)
quests.reward: Red Rupee (20)
quests.oneLine: A target-shooting mine-cart minigame: pop the balloons as you ride.
quests.how: Talk to Bayge, put the mine cart on the tracks and ride it; aim your bow and shoot the balloon targets along the route. Hit enough to clear it (a multi-shot or quick-draw bow helps), then collect the Red Rupee.

quests.name: Mine-Cart Land: Quickshot Course
quests.giver: Heehl, at the Southern Mine (unlocks after Open For Business!)
quests.reward: Purple Rupee (50)
quests.oneLine: A faster mine-cart shooting course with more balloons to pop.
quests.how: After clearing Open For Business!, talk to Heehl and ride the faster cart. You have about 80 seconds and need to pop 7 targets. Use a multi-shot or quick-draw bow and lead the balloons; finish to earn a Purple Rupee.

quests.name: Mine-Cart Land: Death Mountain
quests.giver: Kabetta, at the Southern Mine (unlocks after Quickshot Course)
quests.location: Southern Mine / Death Mountain (course above Sitsum Shrine)
quests.reward: Goron Fabric
quests.oneLine: The hardest mine-cart shooting course, looping all the way around Death Mountain.
quests.how: After Quickshot Course, take Kabetta's run; the course is on the west side of Death Mountain (above Sitsum Shrine). The cart moves fast around the mountain and you must pop 8 balloon targets in under two minutes. Clear it to earn Goron Fabric for your paraglider.

quests.name: A Picture for Woodland Stable
quests.giver: Kish, at Woodland Stable (examine the empty picture frame)
quests.reward: 1 Pony Point + Hasty Vegetable Curry
quests.oneLine: The stable wants a photo of a Goron relaxing in a hot spring to fill its display frame.
quests.how: Examine the empty frame so Kish calls you over, then go to the Goron Hot Springs near Goron City and photograph a Goron soaking in the spring with your Camera. Bring the picture back to Kish at Woodland Stable.

quests.name: A Picture for Foothill Stable
quests.giver: Ozunda, at Foothill Stable
quests.reward: 1 Pony Point + Fireproof Elixir
quests.oneLine: The stable wants a photo of the Daruk statue that watches over Goron City.
quests.how: Talk to Ozunda, then travel to Goron City and photograph the large statue of Daruk overlooking the city with your Camera. Return and show Ozunda the picture on your Purah Pad.

quests.name: Hebra's Colossal Fossil
quests.giver: Loone, at the Eldin Great Skeleton (after Eldin's Colossal Fossil)
quests.reward: Purple Rupee (50)
quests.oneLine: Fossil-lover Loone needs the giant skeleton's missing fin bone and spine restored.
quests.how: Talk to Loone, who says the great skeleton is missing its fin bone and a vertebra. Blast the rubble to free the long fin bone and Ultrahand it onto the empty fin socket; melt the ice block freeing the large spine bone and place it into the empty tail slot. Loone rewards you with a Purple Rupee (despite the name, this quest is in Eldin).

quests.name: Misko's Cave of Chests
quests.giver: Domidak & Prissen, outside Cephla Lake Cave
quests.reward: Ember Trousers
quests.oneLine: A cave full of buried chests where only one holds Misko's real treasure.
quests.how: Enter Cephla Lake Cave and dig through the many buried chests; most hold only 1 Rupee, but one contains the Ember Trousers. Grab it to complete the quest (afterward Domidak & Prissen sell you the manuscript clues for 100 Rupees each).

quests.name: Misko's Treasure: Heroines Manuscript
quests.giver: Domidak (buy the manuscript for 100 Rupees outside Cephla Lake Cave)
quests.location: Starts at Cephla Lake Cave (Eldin); treasure in Gerudo Highlands (Statue of the Eighth Heroine)
quests.reward: Tingle's Hood
quests.oneLine: A manuscript clue points to a hidden chest near the Statue of the Eighth Heroine.
quests.how: Buy the Heroines Manuscript from Domidak. Travel to the Statue of the Eighth Heroine at Hemaar's Descent (Gerudo Highlands) and use the Zonai mirrors to reflect sunlight onto the mechanism, opening the cave. Defeat the Gibdos inside, then open the chest in the statue's head for Tingle's Hood.

quests.name: Misko's Treasure: Pirate Manuscript
quests.giver: Domidak (buy the manuscript for 100 Rupees outside Cephla Lake Cave)
quests.location: Starts at Cephla Lake Cave (Eldin); treasure at Cape Cales Cliffbase Cave (Necluda coast)
quests.reward: Tingle's Tights
quests.oneLine: A bought manuscript clue leads to a hidden chest holding a piece of Tingle's outfit.
